[title] Interstellar 

[author_marker] written by Jonathan Nolan 

[source_marker] story by Jonathan Nolan, Kip Thorne, and Lynda Obst

[draft_date] March 12 2008

[action] SPACE. 

[action] But not the dark lonely corner of it we're used to.  This is
[action] a glittering inferno -- the center of a distant galaxy. 

[action] Suddenly, something TEARS past at incredible speed: a NEUTRON
[action] STAR.  It SMASHES headlong through everything it encounters...
[action] planets, stars.  Can anything stop this juggernaut?

[action] Yes.  Something looms at the heart of the galaxy, hidden
[action] inside the blinding starlight, a dark flaw in the fabric of
[action] existence itself: a BLACK HOLE. 

[action] The neutron star is pulled into the black hole's swirl,
[action] spiraling closer and closer to destruction.  Finally, it
[action] contacts the hole's edge and EXPLODES.

[action] The EXPLOSION is so powerful that it sends shock waves into
[action] the fabric of space-time itself.  We ride one of these waves,
[action] racing back out from the black hole.

[action] Suddenly, a portion of the wave disappears down a crystal-
[action] like hole, emerging in a much darker region of the universe --
[action] a backwater that, as the wave races past a giant red planet
[action] with a distinctive eye, we recognize as our own.  

[action] The wave, now just an infinitesimal ripple, finally reaches 
[action] our blue planet.  It drops into our atmosphere over North
[action] America, toward the high desert east of the Cascades, and
[action] through the roof of a nondescript warehouse. 

[action] The wave tickles the atoms in the steel shell of a vacuum
[action] chamber, then dances a tiny jig with a laser beam reflected
[action] in a heavy piece of glass.  

[action] The wave shoots back out of the building and disappears in
[action] the fractal branches of a tumbleweed resting against a
[action] concrete tube that stretches for miles in the desert. 

[action] An SUV  speeds past the tumbleweed and we follow it till it
[action] parks at another plain-looking building at the opposite end
[action] of the tube.  A MAN climbs out of the  SUV.

[scene_heading] INT. CONTROL ROOM, WAREHOUSE -- DAY

[action] The man lets himself into a large room that looks like Mission
[action] Control.  He pours himself a cup of coffee.  It is the weekend
[action] and the place is empty.  No one has been there to see the
[action] displays flashing a distinctive shape -- a pulse followed by
[action] a series of echoes.

[action] The man looks up at the screen, then DROPS his cup of coffee. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[page_number] 2.*

[scene_heading] INT. LIGO OFFICES, CALTECH, PASADENA -- DAY

[action] The Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory
[action] headquarters at Caltech is a frenzy of activity.  POSTDOCS
[action] and RESEARCHERS huddle around monitors and printouts, arguing.

[action] ANSEN, 60s, the director of LIGO, walks through the frenzy. 
[action] A  postdoc hands him a printout: a pulse followed by echoes. 

[scene_heading] INT. LIGO DIRECTOR'S OFFICE, CALTECH -- DAY

[action] Ansen steps into the relative calm of a large, sunlit office,
[action] which overlooks a grassy stretch of Caltech's campus. 

[action] His ASSISTANT, 30s, is on the phone, on hold.  He looks up
[action] at Ansen as he enters.  

[character] ASSISTANT
[dialogue] I'm on hold with the INS.
[parenthetical] (covers microphone)
[dialogue] Don't you think we should double
[dialogue] check the triangulation before we
[dialogue] call anybody-

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] We have double checked it.

[action] Someone finally picks up the line.  

[character] ASSISTANT
[dialogue] Yes.  I'm trying to reach-
[parenthetical] (pause, listens)
[dialogue] No, I don't think you understand how
[dialogue] serious this is. 
[parenthetical] (pause)
[dialogue] Because if you did, we'd be having
[dialogue] this conversation in person.  

[action] He listens for a moment, then hangs up the phone, confused.

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] What did they say? 

[character] ASSISTANT
[dialogue] They said we should look out the
[dialogue] window. 

[action] Ansen steps to the window and looks out: 

[action] In the courtyard below, coeds are scrambling to get out of
[action] the way as a military helicopter sets down in the middle of
[action] the quad and dozens of ARMED FEDERAL AGENTS converge on his
[action] building. 

[page_number] 3.

[scene_heading] INT. MAIN CONFERENCE ROOM, LIGO, CALTECH -- DAY

[action] Ansen sits, alone, on one side of a conference table.

[action] The other side is filled with GOVERNMENT MEN -- NSA mostly,
[action] some DIA .  The door opens and his assistant steps in.  Armed
[action] guards pat him down, then shove him into a seat. 

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] Is that really necessary? 

[action] One of the NSA agents leans forward. 

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] You've been complaining for years
[dialogue] that the government doesn't take
[dialogue] your project seriously enough, Doctor. 
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] You can't have it both ways. 

[action] Ansen motions to his assistant, who turns on a projector. 
[action] On-screen, we see the familiar pulse and echoes.

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] Yesterday morning, our facility in
[dialogue] Hanford identified this signal: a
[dialogue] neutron star colliding with a
[dialogue] supermassive black hole.  We went
[dialogue] through the last year's data and
[dialogue] triangulated the source. 

[action] The pulse is translated into a crude animatic of a neutron
[action] star circling into the black hole.

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] We know that, Doctor.  What we don't
[dialogue] know is why, according to your
[dialogue] numbers, this event took place right
[dialogue] here in our own solar system.

[action] Suddenly, the image overlays the sun, the earth, and the
[action] rest of our solar system around the black hole. 

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] It didn't.  Because if it had we'd
[dialogue] all be dead by now. 

[action] On-screen, Jupiter, then the Earth and the inner planets are
[action] consumed by the black hole.  Only the sun survives, pulled
[action] into orbit around its new master. 

[character] ANSEN (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Which leaves only one explanation: 
[dialogue] The signal traveled through a 
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 4.*

[character] ANSEN (CONT'D)
[dialogue] wormhole .  A gateway to a distant
[dialogue] corner of the universe.  The black
[dialogue] hole is on the far side. 

[action] On-screen, the black hole system is removed to a distant
[action] corner, connected to ours by a tunnel through space-time.  A
[action] gravity wave from the collision travels through the tunnel. 

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] I've read your book, Doctor.  You
[dialogue] said that wormholes are impossible.

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] There is nothing quite as satisfying
[dialogue] as being proved utterly wrong.
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] I said that a wormhole couldn't exist
[dialogue] naturally . Not for more than a few
[dialogue] billionths of a second.  It would
[dialogue] have to be... stabilized.  

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] Stabilized by what?   

[action] Ansen pauses, unsure.  His assistant steps in to his defense. 

[character] ASSISTANT
[dialogue] We don't have any way to answer that
[dialogue] question. 

[character] NSA AGENT
[parenthetical] (ignores him)
[dialogue] You're not under peer review here,
[dialogue] Doctor.  I don't care about your
[dialogue] reputation.  I need to know how that
[dialogue] thing got there.   Now . 

[action] Ansen finally speaks up. 

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] If you're worried about an invasion,
[dialogue] I would start drafting the articles
[dialogue] of surrender. 
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Whoever they are, if they can build
[dialogue] a wormhole, they could erase us in
[dialogue] the blink of an eye.  Luckily, that
[dialogue] also means we have nothing they could
[dialogue] be interested in. 

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] Then why is it there?

[page_number] 5.

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] I don't know.  Maybe it's an
[dialogue] invitation.  A chance to commune
[dialogue] with an advanced species.

[action] The assistant, embarrassed, looks down.  The agent notices. 

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] You don't agree?

[character] ASSISTANT
[parenthetical] (delicate)
[dialogue] No.  I don't think we can assume an
[dialogue] alien intelligence built the wormhole. 
[parenthetical] (changes tack)
[dialogue] But the opportunity it represents is
[dialogue] incredible.  We could explore parts
[dialogue] of the universe we never dreamt of
[dialogue] reaching in our lifetimes. 

[action] The agent exchanges a look with one of his colleagues, who
[action] steps out of the room.  

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] We need to get back to work.  I have
[dialogue] a conference call with our European
[dialogue] partners in fifteen minutes.

[character] NSA AGENT
[dialogue] We severed the connections to your
[dialogue] European partners this morning.

[character] ANSEN
[parenthetical] (indignant)
[dialogue] You can't do that.  The Europeans
[dialogue] put up some of the funding...

[character] GOVERNMENT MAN
[dialogue] We'll send them a check.
[parenthetical] (stands)
[dialogue] Your project is now classified under
[dialogue] the State Secrets Act. 

[action] He steps out the door, leaving the men alone.  The assistant,
[action] outraged, turns back to his boss. 

[character] ASSISTANT
[dialogue] They can't keep this a secret.  You
[dialogue] know that.  Sooner or later...

[action] The younger man looks down, embarrassed, as he notices that
[action] tears are rolling down the older man's cheeks.

[page_number] 6.

[character] ANSEN
[dialogue] I don't care about that.  I've spent
[dialogue] my whole life being afraid we would
[dialogue] wipe ourselves out before this moment
[dialogue] arrived.  We've made so many mistakes,
[dialogue] I wasn't sure we'd make it...

[action] The  assitant realizes that the old man is weeping for joy. 
[action] Relief. 

[character] ANSEN (CONT'D)
[dialogue] But this will change everything. 
[dialogue] Fifty years from now, nothing will
[dialogue] be the same. 

[action] The older man looks at the simulation on the screen of the
[action] tiny link between our galaxy and another. 

[transition] FADE TO BLACK

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNFIELD, CENTRAL CALIFORNIA -- DAY

[action] Corn.  As far as the eye can see.  

[action] SUPER TITLE: "FIFTY YEARS LATER"

[action] A large old diesel tour bus is parked by the side of a dirt
[action] road, smoke pouring out of its open hood.  A dozen MEN in
[action] BASEBALL UNIFORMS are standing around the front of the bus. 

[action] A battered PICKUP pulls up, and a MAN, 30s, gets out, leaving
[action] his two SONS in the car.  This is COOPER.  He joins the
[action] ballplayers staring at the lifeless diesel engine.

[character] BALLPLAYER
[dialogue] Seized up on us.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Long way to come by bus.  I thought
[dialogue] you guys would have a plane.

[character] BALLPLAYER
[dialogue] We did.  Ran out of parts for it. 
[dialogue] You know anything about diesels?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A little.

[action] Cooper steps to the engine compartment. 

[action] The ballplayer notices Cooper's two boys, TOM, 15, and MURPH,
[action] 10, watching them.  He wanders over. 

[page_number] 7.

[character] BALLPLAYER
[dialogue] You think your dad's going to be
[dialogue] able to help us out? 

[action] Murph, a filth-encrusted kid with a black eye, smiles at the
[action] ballplayer. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] My dad can fix anything. 
[parenthetical] (wry smile)
[dialogue] Except maybe your fastball. 

[action] The ballplayer frowns:  smartass kid. 

[action] After a moment under the hood, Cooper signals to the driver,
[action] who tries the engine.  It turns over once, then STARTS. 

[character] BALLPLAYER
[dialogue] Sure appreciate the help. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] You don't make it, my boys won't get
[dialogue] to see you lose. 

[action] The ballplayers load up into the bus and as it pulls away,
[action] we can see the logo painted across the back of the bus for
[action] the first time:

[action] WORLD FAMOUS NEW YORK YANKEES

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE, NEAR EARTH ORBIT

[action] Earth spins, lazily.  From this height, it looks much the
[action] same as it has done for thousands of years. 

[action] Suddenly, a tiny black object appears, racing toward Earth. 

[action] The object SMASHES into a large satellite and races onward. 
[action] Behind it, the satellite spins out of orbit in a cloud of
[action] fragments. 

[scene_heading] EXT. BASEBALL STADIUM -- NIGHT

[action] An old minor league stadium.  The stands are barely halfway
[action] full.  Cooper, his boys, and Cooper's father-in-law, DONALD,
[action] 60s, have a row to themselves behind the dugout.  

[action] Murph offers his grandpa some popcorn. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Popcorn at a ball game is unnatural. 
[dialogue] I want a hot dog. 

[page_number] 8.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (confused)
[dialogue] What's a hot dog? 

[action] Suddenly, play stops on the field below as the players and
[action] fans look up at the night sky:

[action] A bright blue streak is tearing across it.  It's beautiful. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Is that a comet, Dad? 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (shakes his head)
[dialogue] Satellite.  Big one.  Probably
[dialogue] Chinese.

[action] Everyone watches the fireworks as the satellite burns up in
[action] the upper atmosphere. 

[action] After a moment, play resumes -- it's a pretty show, but
[action] everyone has seen it plenty of times before. 

[action] Down on the field, the Dodgers' catcher misses an easy pop
[action] fly and the Yankees load the bases.  Donald looks disgusted. 

[scene_heading] INT. COOPER'S TRUCK -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper guides his truck along a potholed road.  His father-
[action] in-law is riding shotgun; the boys are sleeping in the back. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Those clowns would get their asses
[dialogue] handed to them by the ballplayers I
[dialogue] grew up watching.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You ruin it for the boys when you
[dialogue] talk like that. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I'm not doing my grandkids any favors
[dialogue] by lying to them.  They're growing
[dialogue] up watching lousy baseball. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] They didn't have any baseball at all
[dialogue] when I was a kid.

[action] That shuts the old man up for now.  They drive on in silence. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[page_number] 9.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE -- MORNING

[action] The sun is gently landing on the horizon, painting the sea
[action] of corn around Cooper's modest house gold.  Cooper walks out
[action] of his house, still eating his breakfast.  

[action] Donald is on the porch, looking at a black clouds of smoke
[action] in the distance.  The neighboring fields are BURNING.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Nelson's burning up his crops.  Found
[dialogue] some of the blight on the okra.  

[action] Cooper watches the men walking through the fields, setting
[action] fire to the crop.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I thought okra wasn't susceptible. 

[character] DONALD
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] Better safe than sorry. 
[parenthetical] (looks at him)
[dialogue] You've got to take the boys to school.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Something wrong with your truck? 

[character] DONALD
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Parent-teacher conference day. 

[action] Cooper bends his head in dismay. 

[character] DONALD (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Be nice to Murph's teacher.  She's
[dialogue] single, you know. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What does that have to do with
[dialogue] anything?

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] We're supposed to be repopulating
[dialogue] the earth.  Gotta pull your weight. 
[dialogue] Besides, the boys could do with a
[dialogue] woman in their lives.   

[action] The boys run out of the house and pile into the truck.  Cooper
[action] pulls away before Donald can continue. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ROAD -- DAY

[action] Cooper weaves the car along a dirt road.  The kids are arguing
[action] over an ancient comic book in the back seat.  

[page_number] 10.

[action] Cooper turns around to break it up. 

[action] BANG -- one of the tires blows out in a foot-deep pothole. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ROADSIDE -- DAY

[action] Cooper examines the flattened tire.  Looks at his older son. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Get the spare, Tom. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] That is the spare. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] All right.  We'll use the patch kit. 

[action] He moves to the back of the truck.  Murph suddenly looks
[action] very glum. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I... I think the patch kit might not
[dialogue] be there...
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] Because I was using it for my bike.  

[action] Cooper looks down at the dirt.  Sighs. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph's law. 

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (confused)
[dialogue] What's that?

[action] Tom snorts with laughter.  Turns to his dad. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] The kid doesn't even know what he
[dialogue] was named after...

[action] Cooper shoots Tom a look -- enough. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Murph's law means what can go wrong
[dialogue] will go wrong. 

[action] Murph, looking hurt, walks off.  Cooper turns to his son. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Find something to patch it with. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] How am I supposed to do that? 

[page_number] 11.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Figure it out.  I'm not always going
[dialogue] to be here to help you. 

[action] Cooper leaves Tom to catch up with his younger son, who is
[action] looking out over the river. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Is that really why I'm named Murph,
[dialogue] dad?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Listen to me.  Murph's law doesn't
[dialogue] mean that.  It means what can happen
[dialogue] will happen.  All kinds of things. 
[dialogue] Good or bad.  And that's the way you
[dialogue] want it to be. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Why? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Because if nothing ever happened to
[dialogue] you then you wouldn't learn anything. 

[action] Murph is staring off into the distance.  He's heard something.  

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Murph?

[action] Then Cooper hears it, too.  A LOW RUMBLING SOUND.  Cooper
[action] looks out over the river.  Then he turns back and tackles
[action] his son to the ground. 

[action] Suddenly, a MASSIVE AIRPLANE SOARS overhead, so close they
[action] can almost touch it.  It bounces the truck on its suspension,
[action] then soars off over the fields behind them. 

[action] Cooper grabs Murph and races back to the truck.  He pulls a
[action] laptop and an antenna made out of a Pringles can out of the
[action] back of the truck.  He hands the laptop and antenna to Murph.  

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Get in. 

[action] Tom is still standing by the side of the road, wrestling
[action] with the jack. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] What about the tire? 

[scene_heading] INT. TRUCK -- MOMENTS LATER

[action] The truck is SMASHING through the cornfields as fast as Cooper
[action] can push it on three good tires. 

[page_number] 12.

[action] Murph is hurriedly firing up the laptop and connecting it to
[action] the directional antenna. 

[action] Cooper is straining to see through the cornstalks, scanning
[action] the horizon. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Over there-

[action] To the right, the dark shape of the Russian drone appears,
[action] flying low over the fields.  Cooper jerks the wheel--

[scene_heading] EXT. RIVER -- DAY

[action] The truck BURSTS out of the corn and SPLASHES across the
[action] river and into an old, abandoned suburban housing development
[action] in the valley below, planted over with corn. 

[action] Half a mile in front of them, the Russian drone is still
[action] hugging the ground.  It has impossibly long, skinny wings,
[action] like an old  U -2 surveillance plane, but no cockpit.  The
[action] tops of its wings are covered in black solar cells. 

[scene_heading] INT. TRUCK -- DAY

[action] Murph is fiddling with the computer.  His older brother takes
[action] the computer from him and fires up emulation software. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It's a Chinese military drone.  Solar
[dialogue] cells could power an entire farm. 
[parenthetical] (to Tom)
[dialogue] Take the wheel. 

[action] Cooper hands Murph the Pringles can antenna. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Keep it pointed right at it, OK? 

[action] Murph nods.  Tom takes the wheel as his dad works the laptop,
[action] trying to communicate with the huge Russian drone.  The screen
[action] fills with Cyrillic characters. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Faster, Tom.  I'm losing it. 

[action] Tom WEAVES the truck at speed through the old, curved streets
[action] of the development, past oversized suburban mansions planted
[action] over with corn. 

[action] They round a corner and come face-to-face with a robot
[action] harvester.  Tom jerks the wheel to avoid it. 

[action] BANG -- the truck loses a wing mirror against the flank of
[action] the combine. 

[page_number] 13.

[scene_heading] EXT. SUBURBAN DEVELOPMENT -- DAY

[action] Tom guides the truck from street to street, trying to chart
[action] a straight path across the fields.  The truck BOUNCES as it
[action] SMASHES through an old picket fence. 

[action] Ahead, the drone is soaring, banking, pulling away. 

[scene_heading] INT. TRUCK -- DAY

[action] Cooper is still trying to hack into the drone's control
[action] circuitry as they leave the development behind and begin to
[action] climb into the foothills of the Sierras. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  RIDGELINE, HILLS -- DAY

[action] Tom guides the truck along the spine of the hills.  The drone
[action] soars overhead, making for the white tips of the Sierras. 

[scene_heading] INT. TRUCK -- DAY

[action] Cooper is oblivious to the picturesque surroundings as he
[action] concentrates on the laptop. 

[character] TOM
[parenthetical] (unsure)
[dialogue] Dad?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Almost got it.  Don't slow down. 

[action] In front of them, Tom and his brother watch as the drone
[action] plummets from view.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] DAD. 

[action] Cooper looks up.  Ahead, the trail disappears as the edge of
[action] the hills falls away -- it's a three hundred-foot drop. 

[action] Tom locks up the brakes. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  RIDGELINE, HILLS -- DAY

[action] The truck skids to a halt inches from a precipitous drop. 

[action] Cooper climbs out, holding the laptop.  Murph climbs out
[action] next to him, still pointing the Pringles can. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] We lost it. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (smiling)
[dialogue] No we didn't. 

[page_number] 14.

[action] Suddenly, the drone SOARS back over them.  Cooper types a
[action] couple keys and then moves his fingers across the trackpad. 
[action] The huge drone banks and turns in response.

[action] As the boys watch, Cooper sends the drone soaring high over
[action] them, banking and soaring along the tree-lined sides of the
[action] valley, light glinting from the black panels on its back. 
[action] It's a beautiful sight. 

[action] Cooper crouches next to Murph.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You want to give it a whirl?

[action] Murph looks at his dad, wide-eyed.  He takes the laptop and
[action] moves his fingers gingerly across the pad. 

[action] In response, the massive plane banks into a tight turn in
[action] the valley below. 

[action] For a moment, Murph is in pure heaven, sending the drone
[action] dancing through the valley below.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Set her down in the valley -- there,
[dialogue] next to the river. 

[action] Murph leads the plane in a figure eight and then begins
[action] guiding it into a gentle landing in the valley floor below. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ABANDONED GOLF COURSE, VALLEY FLOOR -- DAY

[action] The truck limps along the overgrown fairway of a long-defunct
[action] golf course towards the massive hulk of the Russian drone, 

[action] Cooper and the boys climb down.  The valley is silent save
[action] for the truck's engine WHEEZING and SPUTTERING as it cools. 

[action] Cooper runs a hand over the smooth carbon flank of the drone. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] How long do you think it's been up
[dialogue] there, Dad? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Chinese mission control went down
[dialogue] same as us, twenty years ago.  It's
[dialogue] been up there ever since. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] What was it doing flying so low? 

[action] Cooper reads the information pouring into his laptop.

[page_number] 15.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It was looking for something. 
[dialogue] Intercepted some kind of signal.
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] It's been at eighty thousand feet. 
[dialogue] Sun probably cooked its brain. 

[action] Cooper runs his hand along the flank till he finds an access
[action] patch.  He pulls out a crowbar and pries open the hatch. 
[action] Inside, surrounded by a nest of liquid cooling tubes, is a
[action] small black module -- the drone's auto-pilot. 

[action] Cooper looks down at Murph, who is standing at his elbow. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What are you going to do with it?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Reprogram it.  Give it something
[dialogue] socially responsible to do like drive
[dialogue] a combine or a tractor. 

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] Couldn't we just let it go?  It's
[dialogue] not hurting anyone. 

[action] Cooper looks down at his son.  Good kid. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We need all the help we can get,
[dialogue] Murph.  This thing has to adapt,
[dialogue] just like the rest of us. 

[action] Cooper gently pries the control module out. 

[scene_heading] EXT. COUNTY SCHOOL -- DAY

[action] It's a small school, so all the kids and parents waiting in
[action] front know exactly who's driving the pickup truck with half
[action] of a Russian spy plane hanging out of the bed as it pulls up. 

[scene_heading] INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE, COUNTY SCHOOL -- DAY

[action] Cooper is ushered into the office.  The PRINCIPAL, 40s, an
[action] efficient-looking man, shakes his hand. 

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Good to see you, Mr. Cooper.  This
[dialogue] is Ms. Kelly, Murph's teacher. 

[action] Cooper smiles at Ms. KELLY, 30s, attractive.  

[page_number] 16.

[character] PRINCIPAL (CONT'D)
[dialogue] So we've gotten Tom's score back. 
[dialogue] Congratulations.  He's going to make
[dialogue] an excellent farmer. 

[action] The principal slides a carbon copy across the desk to Cooper,
[action] who looks taken aback. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What about college? 

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] The University of California only
[dialogue] accepts a few hundred students a
[dialogue] year, Mr. Cooper.  You have to be
[dialogue] realistic. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You're ruling out college for him
[dialogue] now ? He's only fifteen.  

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I'm afraid Tom's score
[dialogue] simply isn't high enough. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What are you, about a 36-inch waist?  
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] 30-inch inseam? 

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] I'm not sure I see-- 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You're telling me you need two numbers
[dialogue] to measure your own ass, but just
[dialogue] one to measure my son's future?

[action] Ms. Kelly stifles a laugh, then, with a look from the
[action] principal, takes on the appropriate look of offense. 

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] I understand you're a well-educated
[dialogue] man, Mr. Cooper.  A scientist?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Engineer.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Frankly, the world doesn't need any
[dialogue] more engineers.  We didn't run out
[dialogue] of trains or television sets or
[dialogue] satellites. 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] We ran out of  food . 

[page_number] 17.

[action] Cooper leans back.  He's not going to win this one. 

[character] PRINCIPAL (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The world needs farmers, Mr. Cooper. 
[dialogue] And I'm sure your son Tom is going
[dialogue] to make a fine one. 
[parenthetical] (smiles benignly)
[dialogue] We're a caretaker generation.  But
[dialogue] things are getting better.  Maybe
[dialogue] your grandchildren will be able to
[dialogue] attend college. 

[action] Cooper looks down, swallowing his anger. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Are we done?

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] One more thing.  Ms. Kelly here says
[dialogue] that Murph brought a book to school
[dialogue] about the lunar landings. 

[action] He slides an old textbook with a picture of a rocket on the
[action] cover across the desk to Cooper. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] One of my old textbooks.  Murph liked
[dialogue] the pictures. 

[character] MS. KELLY
[dialogue] This is one of the old federal
[dialogue] textbooks.  We've replaced them with
[dialogue] corrected versions. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Corrected? 

[character] MS. KELLY
[dialogue] The new textbooks explain that the
[dialogue] Apollo lunar missions were faked in
[dialogue] order to bankrupt the Soviet Union. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You don't believe we went to the
[dialogue] moon?

[character] MS. KELLY
[dialogue] I believe it was a brilliant piece
[dialogue] of propaganda.  The Soviets spent
[dialogue] years trying to build rockets and
[dialogue] other useless machines.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] "Useless machines"? 

[page_number] 18.

[action] Cooper looks to the Principal for help.  None is forthcoming. 

[character] MS. KELLY
[dialogue] Yes, Mr. Cooper.  The kind of
[dialogue] wastefulness and excess that the
[dialogue] 20th century represented.  Your
[dialogue] children would be better off learning
[dialogue] about this planet, rather than reading
[dialogue] fantasies about leaving it.

[action] Cooper is silent for a long moment.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] One of those useless machines they
[dialogue] used to make was called an  MRI.  If
[dialogue] we had any of them left the doctors
[dialogue] might have been able to find the
[dialogue] cyst in my wife's brain before she
[dialogue] died, rather than afterwards.  And
[dialogue] then my kids could have been raised
[dialogue] by two parents, instead of me and
[dialogue] their pain-in-the-ass grandfather. 

[action] Ms. Kelly's face falls, ashen.  Cooper swallows his anger. 
[action] Most of it, anyway. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You ever consider the best thing for
[dialogue] the world and humanity might have
[dialogue] been for us to part company?

[action] Cooper gets up to leave. 

[scene_heading] INT. TRUCK, COUNTY SCHOOL PARKING LOT -- DAY

[action] Cooper climbs into the truck, trying to hold it together. 
[action] He PUNCHES the wheel. 

[action] The radio KEYS to life.  He ignores it.  Sits for a moment
[action] in misery.  Finally he picks up the handset. 

[character] CB OPERATOR (O.S.)
[dialogue] Got a call from Riggs, down in
[dialogue] Galveston.  Says some of the tractors
[dialogue] you built him went haywire last night. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Just tell him to power down the
[dialogue] controllers for a couple minutes. 

[character] CB OPERATOR (O.S.)
[dialogue] I did.  He wants you to come down in
[dialogue] person anyway.  Says he found
[dialogue] something you should take a look at. 

[page_number] 19.

[action] Cooper stares at the wheel.  Shakes his head in frustration. 

[scene_heading] EXT. AIRSTRIP -- DAY

[action] Cooper pulls his truck up to a grimy-looking hangar.  Pulls
[action] a tarp off of an ancient Piper Cub.  Checks it over.

[scene_heading] INT. PIPER CUB -- DAY

[action] Cooper guides the plane along a long sliver of deserted beach. 
[action] The radio crackles to life. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Bravo-two-eight, requesting permission
[dialogue] to enter your airspace. 

[character] AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL (O.S.)
[dialogue] Permission granted.  Welcome to the
[dialogue] sovereign nation of Texas.

[action] Coop hangs up the radio.  Banks the plane inland.  

[scene_heading] EXT. GULF COAST -- DAY

[action] Below, a combine harvester fights its way up the dunes, trying
[action] to reach the beach, its wheels struggling for traction in
[action] the soft sand. 

[action] A MAN waves up at Cooper's plane as it circles overhead. 

[action] Cooper lands the plane on a deserted roadway.  Jumps down. 

[action] He intercepts one of the combines as it trundles past, trying
[action] to reach the dunes.  He pops open the cabin.  

[action] Inside is a mess of wires hooked into an auto-pilot not unlike
[action] the one he ripped out of the drone.  He checks the fault
[action] code and resets the computer. 

[action] The man jogs over to meet him. 

[character] RIGGS
[dialogue] Thanks for coming down.  Half of 'em
[dialogue] took off last night, looking for
[dialogue] something.   
[parenthetical] (points to dunes)
[dialogue] Looks like they found it, too.  I
[dialogue] thought you were the man to see it. 

[action] Riggs starts walking up the dune.  Cooper follows. 

[action] Below, on the beach, a dozen more combines and other farming
[action] vehicles are lined up at the tideline, warm gulf water lapping
[action] at their metal flanks.  They are circling a deep crater. 

[page_number] 20.

[action] As they watch, an ancient autonomous SUB BEACHES itself,
[action] trying to reach the crater.

[scene_heading] EXT. CRATER'S EDGE, BEACH -- DAY

[action] Cooper steps between the waiting machines and peers down
[action] into the crater. 

[action] At the very bottom is a ROUND BLACK BALL, about a foot across --
[action] the same object we saw punch a hole in the side of a
[action] satellite.  Every few seconds, it emits a distinctive CHIRP. 

[action] Cooper checks his rad meter.  A tiny reading -- non-lethal. 
[action] He takes off his watch and hands it to Riggs.  Then he slides
[action] down into the hole.

[action] The probe CHIRPS as Cooper slides down on top of it.  He
[action] rubs a hand across its smooth composite bulk. 

[character] RIGGS
[parenthetical] (from above)
[dialogue] You think it's an alien? 

[action] Cooper wipes sand off of the object, revealing the faint,
[action] familiar outline etched into the side of the probe: 

[action] The stars and stripes of the old federal government. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not exactly. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CRATER'S EDGE, BEACH -- DAY

[action] Using a rope and a winch, Cooper hauls the blackened probe
[action] out of the crater and onto the beach. 

[action] Cooper hefts it up and carries it to the back of his plane. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Space probe.  Never seen one like
[dialogue] it, though.  Looks like it's been to
[dialogue] hell and back.

[action] The probe CHIRPS as Cooper belts it into the back.

[character] RIGGS
[dialogue] How do you think it wound up here?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Lost, I guess.  Guidance satellites
[dialogue] would have been shot down by the
[dialogue] Chinese twenty years ago.

[action] Cooper looks at the probe for a second, admiring its form.  

[page_number] 21.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Donald is pouring a bottle of corn beer into a bubbling vat
[action] of chowder.  He turns to watch Cooper work, amused. 

[action] The probe has been clamped to the kitchen table.  Cooper
[action] works at the blackened case with a BLOWTORCH.  Cooper gives
[action] up -- the torch hasn't made a scratch.  The probe CHIRPS. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Well I don't know what the hell it's
[dialogue] made of, but I can't crack it open. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Good.  Clear it off the table so I
[dialogue] can serve dinner. 

[action] Tom and Murph walk in.  Murph's got another black eye. 

[character] DONALD (CONT'D)
[dialogue] What happened this time?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I got suspended.  Paul said anyone
[dialogue] who believed we went to the moon was
[dialogue] an idiot.  So I hit him. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Good boy.  Hand me the scanner. 

[action] Murph hands his dad a defibrillator he's modified for the
[action] purpose.  He attaches the shock pads to the sides of the
[action] probe and turns on the power. 

[action] Numbers flash across the screen.  Cooper hits a button on
[action] the controls and it PULSES. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Here we go.  Standard NASA encryption. 
[dialogue] Memory's been damaged.  Just noise. 
[dialogue] Hold on.  I've got something. 

[action] Cooper unplugs a monitor from his computer and plugs it into
[action] the defibrillator.  After a moment, an image fills the screen:

[action] An ICE-COVERED PLANET nestled in the center of a system
[action] impossibly dense with stars.  Murph stares, transfixed. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Where is that, dad? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I don't know. 

[action] Cooper looks at the probe. 

[page_number] 22.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Where the hell did you come from? 

[action] Cooper shrugs.  Turns off the monitor. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We'll take it down to Tyson's tomorrow
[dialogue] and have it melted down.  Might be
[dialogue] some copper inside. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] But what about its mission?  What
[dialogue] about the information onboard? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] There's no one for it to report to. 
[dialogue] NASA is all gone.  I'm sorry, son. 
[dialogue] It got home too late.

[action] Donald pulls his chowder off the boil and slides the pot
[action] unceremoniously onto the table. 

[scene_heading] INT. DINING ROOM -- NIGHT

[action] The boys have gone to bed.  Cooper and Donald are alone at
[action] the table.  Donald hands Cooper another beer.  

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I heard your meeting at the school
[dialogue] didn't go so well. 

[action] Cooper shakes his head in disgust. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Maybe it's better for everyone to
[dialogue] forget what they did back then. 
[dialogue] Reminds us how far we've fallen. 

[character] DONALD
[parenthetical] (looks down,)
[dialogue] When I was kid, it felt like they
[dialogue] made something new every day.  Some
[dialogue] gadget or idea.  
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Like every day was Christmas.
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] But we made a lot of mistakes.  Six
[dialogue] billion people.  Just try to imagine
[dialogue] that.  Every last one of them trying
[dialogue] to have it all. 

[action] Donald rolls the bottle of beer in his hands. 

[page_number] 23.

[character] DONALD (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The truth is this world isn't that
[dialogue] bad.  In a lot of ways its better. 
[dialogue] Tom will be all right, whether he
[dialogue] goes to college or not. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It doesn't bother me that he can't
[dialogue] go.  It bothers me that he doesn't
[dialogue] care. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Tom isn't the problem.  He fits in
[dialogue] this world just fine.   You're  the
[dialogue] one who doesn't fit, Coop.  You don't
[dialogue] belong here.  You were born forty
[dialogue] years too late, or forty years too
[dialogue] early -- I don't know.  My daughter
[dialogue] knew it, god bless her.  And your
[dialogue] kids know it, too. 

[action] Donald drains his beer.  Walks to the screen door.  Stops,
[action] one hand on the frame. 

[character] DONALD (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You were good at something and you
[dialogue] never got a chance to do anything
[dialogue] with it.  And I'm sorry, Coop.  But
[dialogue] that's not your kids' fault.

[action] Donald pushes out the screen door. 

[action] Cooper looks at his beer.  The probe CHIRPS. 

[scene_heading] INT. BEDROOM, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper flops down on his bed, fully clothed, exhausted.  He
[action] stares up at the ceiling.  This is his life.  

[scene_heading] INT. BEDROOM, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] The air is filled with a PIERCING NOISE.

[action] Cooper BOLTS upright.  Stumbles out the door. 

[scene_heading] INT. HALLWAY, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper's boys are in the hallway, exhausted.  Cooper, holding
[action] a baseball bat, makes his way down the stairs. 

[action] Cooper uses the bat to open the kitchen door.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper steps in, Murph watching from behind him. 

[page_number] 24.

[action] The probe is clamped to the table, the chirp replaced with a
[action] DEAFENING SCREAM. 

[action] Cooper, holding his ears, moves closer to the probe.  He
[action] hits it with the paddles.  No result. 

[action] He SMASHES it with the bat.  Nothing.  He HITS it AGAIN and
[action] AGAIN.  Finally, the clamps break off chunks of the table
[action] and the probe slams to the ground and ROLLS toward the front
[action] door.  As it rolls, it STOPS SHRIEKING. 

[action] Cooper and the others watch it roll toward the door.  It
[action] stops at the wall.  After a second, it begins SHRIEKING AGAIN. 

[action] Cooper grabs it and rolls it toward the front door.  Once
[action] again, the movement shuts it up. 

[scene_heading] EXT. PORCH, COOPER'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper and his boys roll the probe out of the front door. 
[action] It BUMPS down the front steps and comes to rest in the dirt. 
[action] After a moment, the unholy RACKET starts up again. 

[action] Cooper keeps rolling it, but it doesn't seem to help.  Murph
[action] looks up, sees the stars overhead. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Try a different direction. 

[action] As they roll the probe in a circle, its SHRIEK stops, then
[action] picks up again.  Cooper zeroes in on the direction that keeps
[action] it quiet -- southwest -- and pushes it along in the dirt.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It's a fail-safe.  It's going to
[dialogue] annoy us into taking it home. 

[action] Cooper stops rolling the probe and, after a moment, it begins
[action] SHRIEKING again. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] What are we going to do? 

[action] Cooper gets a rope. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're going to get some sleep.

[action] He begins tying the rope around the probe. 

[scene_heading] INT. BEDROOM, COOPER'S HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Cooper wakes.  He's been sleeping with a pillow wrapped around
[action] his head.  The SHRIEK can be heard, but it's muffled, distant. 

[page_number] 25.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, COOPER'S HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Tom heads off for school with Donald.  Murph, still suspended,
[action] looks up at Cooper, smiling. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What are we doing today? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You're staying here and cleaning the
[dialogue] house.  

[action] Murph looks crestfallen.  He looks out towards the yard. 
[action] Cooper follows his stare. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I told you, Murph.  There's no one
[dialogue] to take it back to. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] But what if there is, dad?  What if
[dialogue] there's something we can salvage?  

[action] Cooper thinks it over.  Murph scrambles to get his shoes. 

[scene_heading] EXT. COOPER'S HOUSE -- DAY

[action] The shriek is still muffled.  Cooper walks over to the well,
[action] putting in a pair of earplugs. 

[action] A line is staked off, leading down into the well below. 
[action] Cooper begins hauling the line up. 

[action] As the probe breaks the surface of the water, the SHRIEK
[action] returns to its normal volume.  Cooper rolls the probe out
[action] onto the ground. 

[scene_heading] INT. PIPER CUB -- DAY

[action] The probe CHIRPS next to Murph in the backseat as Cooper
[action] spins the plane and guns the throttle and they bounce along
[action] the dusty runway and into the air.

[scene_heading] EXT. CALIFORNIA COAST -- DAY

[action] The tiny plane follows the mountains south. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SKIES OVER LOS ANGELES -- DAY

[action] Cooper flies in low.  Los Angeles looks much the same way it
[action] did in the early 20th century -- small settlements in Santa
[action] Monica and Downtown.  Wildfires and earthquakes have shaken
[action] and burned what was left of the homes in between.

[page_number] 26.

[scene_heading] EXT. PACIFIC COAST HIGHWAY, MALIBU -- DAY

[action] Cooper puts the plane down on the old highway and taxis up
[action] to a gas station.  Ahead, the Coast Highway peters out and
[action] disappears beneath rows of wild grass -- Malibu has become
[action] ranchland, once again.

[scene_heading] EXT. GAS STATION, MALIBU -- DAY

[action] An OLD MAN looks up as Cooper steps out of the plane and
[action] checks it over. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Got any diesel? 

[character] OLD MAN
[dialogue] Plenty.  Shame you can't eat it.

[action] Cooper stretches the hose over to the plane.

[scene_heading] INT. PIPER CUB -- DAY

[action] Cooper rests a hand lightly on the controls as he follows
[action] the coast.  Murph gazes out the window.  In the backseat,
[action] the probe is HUMMING.  

[action] A light marine layer beneath them parts, revealing SANTA
[action] CRUZ ISLAND, a large, uninhabited island.

[action] As Cooper soars over the island, the probe HUMS, insistent. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I think it's home. 

[action] Cooper circles the island until he finds a long, flat
[action] grassland in the center of the island. 

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- DAY

[action] The plane bounces and hops to a halt in waist-deep grass. 

[action] Murph and Cooper climb down from the plane.  Cooper slings a
[action] rifle over his shoulder. 

[action] A few dozen yards from the plane they reach the tree line. 
[action] Murph stops, mesmerized by a patch of weeds studded with
[action] bright red -- strawberries. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad, what are these things? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I don't know.  Don't touch them. 

[page_number] 27.

[action] Cooper spots something in the foliage ahead that looks a
[action] little off.  He walks over. 

[action] He pokes at the undergrowth with his rifle.  The rifle CLANKS
[action] against something metal.  Cooper reaches -- it's a camouflage
[action] scrim.  He gently pulls it aside, revealing a chemical
[action] transport truck. 

[action] Cooper steps back, alarmed.  He brings up his rifle. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Murph? 

[action] He looks around.  Murph is nowhere to be seen.  Cooper curses
[action] and heads into the forest to look for him. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CLEARING, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- DAY

[action] Cooper walks through a glade.  He stops to get his bearings. 
[action] Leans against a tree.  Snatches his hand back -- the trunk
[action] of the tree is red hot.  

[action] Cooper steps back -- it's not a tree at all, but a camouflaged
[action] chimney stack.  He looks up: the tree is venting steam.  

[action] Cooper walks a little further, until he finds several massive
[action] tanks.  The tanks are filling with a bubbling liquid -- some
[action] kind of industrial process is taking place beneath him. 

[action] Cooper finally catches up with Murph at the edge of a
[action] clearing.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Careful.  There's some kind of
[dialogue] underground facility here.  We
[dialogue] might...

[action] Cooper notices his son is frozen, staring at something: 

[action] Standing bolt upright in the middle of the clearing, wearing
[action] an old straw hat, is an eight-foot-tall military spec ROBOT.  

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Is it still... alive?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Can't be.  It's a marine.  Haven't
[dialogue] made them for thirty years.  I've
[dialogue] never seen one intact before.

[action] Cooper steps closer to the robot, which is frozen.  Its alloy
[action] frame heavily tarnished and weather-beaten.  It looks like
[action] it might have been standing here for decades. 

[page_number] 28.

[action] Cooper moves closer to it, looking into its blackened eyes. 
[action] He steps back, clearly a little spooked. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I think we need to go, Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] But can't we take it back?  You could
[dialogue] fix it up, get him to do chores.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No.  I don't know what this place
[dialogue] is, but we're leaving.  

[action] Murph, ignoring his father, steps forward to touch the robot.

[action] Suddenly, the robot SPRINGS into action, picking up the boy
[action] and hoisting him up to eye level. 

[action] Cooper, stunned, points the rifle at the robot.  

[action] The robot turns, dropping the boy, RIPS the rifle from
[action] Cooper's hands, BENDS it, then SLAMS him up against the side
[action] of the water tank.  Cooper punches him, then winces in agony.

[action] Murph picks up the rifle and begins HITTING the robot as
[action] hard as he can.  The robot opens his mouth and addresses
[action] Cooper calmly in the clipped tones of a US marine. 

[character] ROBOT
[dialogue] Tell the boy to stop hitting me.  

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (ignoring him)
[dialogue] Hit him in the back of the neck.

[action] Murph raises the rifle butt.  A voice stops him.

[character] VOICE (O.S.)
[dialogue] I wouldn't do that. 

[action] A woman, 30s, step out from the trees.  This is AMELIA BRAND --
[action] tough, bright, and a decent shot with the large rifle that
[action] she's pointing at Cooper. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We were just looking for salvage.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Is that what they call stealing these
[dialogue] days?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I didn't know it belonged to anyone.  

[page_number] 29.*

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It doesn't.  
[parenthetical] (to robot)
[dialogue] You want to let them go, Tars, or do
[dialogue] you want some help with your work?

[action] The robot, evidently named Tars, looks at Cooper closely. 
[action] Then lets him go. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You've got me wrong, lady. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Really?  You're not the kind of guy
[dialogue] who turns a combat marine with a
[dialogue] supercomputer for a brain into a
[dialogue] riding lawn mower?

[action] Cooper says nothing -- she's not that far off the mark. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Get back in your little plane, go
[dialogue] back to your farming commune.  And
[dialogue] don't come back. 

[action] Cooper backs up.  Tars holds up his mangled rifle.  Smiles. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Don't forget your gun.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (points at Tars)
[dialogue] Word of advice -- careful with that
[dialogue] thing.  When the war was over, they
[dialogue] didn't know when to stop fighting. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I'd trust him a lot more than I'd
[dialogue] trust you.  Keep moving. 

[action] Cooper backs up to his plane.  Tars follows him.

[action] Suddenly, in the plane, the probe emits a high-pitch SQUEAL. 
[action] Tars responds instantly, striding past Cooper to the plane. 
[action] He sees the probe.  Tries to open the door.  It's locked. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Wait a second--

[action] Tars TEARS the door off the plane.  Reaches inside and pulls
[action] out the probe.  The woman looks at it, stunned. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Get it inside.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 30.

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] (points rifle at Cooper)
[dialogue] You too.  We'll figure out what to
[dialogue] do with you later. 

[action] Tars stops, and two panels open in the ground, revealing a
[action] huge, reinforced service elevator. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SERVICE ELEVATOR, UNDERGROUND BUNKER -- DAY

[action] Brand waves at a security camera and the lift begins to glide
[action] diagonally down a tunnel that cuts hundreds of feet below
[action] the island's surface.  She keeps her weapon shouldered. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Now who's stealing from who? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This doesn't belong to you.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You're right.  It belongs to NASA,
[dialogue] which shut down thirty years ago
[dialogue] when the federal government ran out
[dialogue] of money.  

[action] Brand says nothing.  The elevator slows to a stop at the
[action] bottom of the tunnel.  Heavy BLAST DOORS grind open and Brand
[action] motions for Cooper to step forward. 

[scene_heading] INT. UNDERGROUND BUNKER -- DAY

[action] They step into a vast underground facility.  Standing in the
[action] center, braced by a launch tower, is a ten story tall rocket. 
[action] Dozens of ROBOTS are working on in, maintaining it. 

[action] Cooper, stunned, looks at Brand. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Who are you people? 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (wry)
[dialogue] The government gave us plenty of
[dialogue] practice looking for our own funding. 
[parenthetical] (gestures with rifle)
[dialogue] Keep moving. 

[action] Tars carries the probe over to an area of the hangar filled
[action] with electronic equipment.  A group of ENGINEERS and ROBOTS
[action] converge around Tars. 

[action] An OLDER ENGINEER looks familiar -- 70s, white-haired, this
[dialogue] is Ansen's assistant at Caltech who we met fifty years
[action] earlier.   

[page_number] 31.

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I found them outside with it.  It
[dialogue] looks like six.  Maybe seven.  I
[dialogue] can't tell from the radiation damage. 

[action] The older engineer looks at the probe, astonished.

[character] OLD ENGINEER
[dialogue] Where did you find it?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Galveston. 

[character] BRAND'S FATHER
[parenthetical] (thinking it over)
[dialogue] Of course.  It must have been looking
[dialogue] for Canaveral. 

[action] Tars bolts the probe down into a purpose built rig.  DOYLE,
[action] 40s, an engineer, begins hooking leads into the probe. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Canaveral's been gone for thirty
[dialogue] years.

[action] Brand ignores him. 

[action] Doyle looks up from his monitor, frustrated. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It's not responding to the handshake. 
[dialogue] I can't open anything on primary.

[action] Brand looks up at Cooper. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What did you do to it? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Nothing.  I got something off of it. 

[action] Cooper looks around.  There's a safety station on the wall
[action] with a battery powered defibrillator. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (to Brand)
[dialogue] May I? 

[action] Brand nods, wary.  Cooper walks over to the wall, takes the
[action] defibrillator, and hooks it up to the probe.  He taps into
[action] the current and runs a lead into the terminal.  Brand's Father
[action] watches, fascinated, as Cooper fires the defibrillator. 

[page_number] 32.

[character] OLD ENGINEER
[dialogue] Of course.  The high voltage allows
[dialogue] you to image the entire memory unit
[dialogue] at once. 

[action] Information begins appearing on the terminal's screen. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Most of the disk was noise.  Couple
[dialogue] of clean sectors, though.

[action] Suddenly, the monitor starts pulling good data off of the
[action] probe.  The older man and Doyle begin sorting through it.  

[action] Cooper smiles at Brand, who seems less than impressed.  

[action] The footage of the ice-covered planet Cooper saw earlier
[action] pops onto the screen. 

[action] The engineers and physicists get very quiet, studying the
[action] images. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It found something.  
[parenthetical] (reading)
[dialogue] Very thin nitrogen-based atmosphere. 
[dialogue] Trace radiation.  Surface is ninety
[dialogue] percent frozen water.  Ten percent
[dialogue] rock -- sedimentary composition
[dialogue] similar to limestone. 
[parenthetical] (reading)
[dialogue] Wow.  Pockets of oxygen below the
[dialogue] surface.  Lots and lots of oxygen. 
[parenthetical] (excited)
[dialogue] This could be the one, boss. 

[action] The older engineer studies the image of the ice-covered
[action] planet, thinking.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] There aren't any planets like that
[dialogue] anywhere near earth.  Not even if
[dialogue] this thing was gone for thirty years. 

[action] Brand looks at Cooper, appraising.  She turns to the older
[action] engineer.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] He's heard enough.  If we're going
[dialogue] to launch, we need to keep them here
[dialogue] until afterwards. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You can't keep us here.

[page_number] 33.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] He could endanger the mission. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I'm not going to endanger it any
[dialogue] more than you already have. 

[action] Cooper points to a telemetry unit that is being repaired by
[action] a robot on a nearby bench.  

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Are you using that for guidance?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Why not?  We've tested it a hundred
[dialogue] times. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The power supply is no good.  If the
[dialogue] voltage fluctuates under load, the
[dialogue] unit will fail. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Now how could you possibly know that?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Bought thirty of them off a guy in
[dialogue] Florida.  Had to rebuild every last
[dialogue] one.
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] They work great on a riding lawn mower. 

[action] Brand looks back at the telemetry system.  The older engineer
[action] watches the exchange, thinking. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT.  MACHINE SHOP, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- DAY

[action] Cooper and Murph have been locked in an abandoned machine
[action] shop, surrounded by the massive remains of rocket engines in
[action] various states of disrepair.  Tars is blocking the door.

[action] Cooper stands.  Tars wags a long, hydraulic finger at him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You plan on keeping us here forever?

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] My battery has a duty cycle of five
[dialogue] hundred years. 

[action] Cooper gives up.  He turns back.  

[page_number] 34.

[action] The back of the shop is filled with a group of older ROBOTS
[action] who are overhauling an engine.  One problem -- the engine
[action] isn't there.  Their programming hasn't been updated to reflect
[action] their obsolescence. 

[action] Murph watches, entranced, as the robots go about their
[action] business, efficiently TORQUING bolts with impact drivers
[action] into a non-existent thruster cone.  The bolts CLATTER to the
[action] ground as the robots stop to reload. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What are they doing, dad?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I guess no one told them they were
[dialogue] out of a job.
[parenthetical] (nods at Tars)
[dialogue] Same as the rest of these people. 

[action] Cooper notices through the glass door of the lab that Brand
[action] and the older engineer are arguing about something.  She
[action] finally relents and walks towards the door. 

[action] Brand walks in.  She gestures for Cooper to follow.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The mission commander wants to see
[dialogue] you.  Your son can stay here.  He'll
[dialogue] be all right with Tars. 

[action] Cooper eyes Tars warily.  Then steps outside.

[scene_heading] EXT. UNDERGROUND BUNKER -- DAY

[action] They walk across the space towards the base of the rocket,
[action] away from the other engineers.  Cooper nods at the older
[action] engineer. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I thought the old man was in charge. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (annoyed)
[dialogue] The 'old man' is my  father . 
[dialogue] And he was in charge.  But he decided
[dialogue] that we needed someone who could
[dialogue] lead the mission for the foreseeable
[dialogue] future. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not you? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I'm a biologist. 

[page_number] 35.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (laughs)
[dialogue] You don't look like one. 
[parenthetical] (off her look)
[dialogue] With the rifle, I mean.  

[action] Brand heads towards a group of large maintenance ROBOTS
[action] clustered at the base of the rocket.  A smaller, human sized
[action] robot is staring at the rocket, giving them instructions. 

[action] The robot looks up.  This is CASE, the leader of the mission. 
[action] Originally an air force pararescue officer, every part of
[action] him, from his alloy chassis to his voice, was designed to
[action] inspire respect and confidence.  He turns to Cooper. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] You're the man who brought us the
[dialogue] probe? 
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] Thank you.  We tested the telemetry
[dialogue] board you warned us about.  It failed
[dialogue] under high voltage, just as you said. 
[dialogue] Come with me, please.  

[action] Case strides off. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MISSION CONTROL, UNDERGROUND HANGAR -- DAY

[action] The lights dim as Brand's father brings up a schematic of
[action] our solar system.  Case points to the picture of the ice
[action] planet recovered from the probe. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] You're right, Mr. Cooper.  The planet
[dialogue] you saw is a long way from earth.  A
[dialogue] very long way indeed. 

[action] Brand looks down.  Case notices. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Our science officer doesn't think I
[dialogue] should trust you with any of this.
[dialogue] One of the curious things about humans
[dialogue] is that the more alike you are, the
[dialogue] more initially hostile you are to
[dialogue] each other.  As if by design. 

[action] Brand makes eye contact with Cooper, then looks away,
[action] embarrassed. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I've found the best way to earn a
[dialogue] person's trust is to trust them.
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 36.

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (looks at Brand's
[dialogue] father)
[dialogue] Go ahead, John. 

[dialogue] Brand's father taps a few keys and the schematic he built
[action] fifty years beforehand flickers onto the screen. 

[character] BRAND'S FATHER
[dialogue] It's a wormhole.  A shortcut leading
[dialogue] to a galaxy on the far side of the
[dialogue] universe.  We found it fifty years
[dialogue] ago. 

[action] Cooper looks at the animatic, taking it in. 

[character] BRAND'S FATHER  (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We've been waiting, sending probes
[dialogue] into it for decades.  None of them
[dialogue] ever came back.  Not until now. 

[action] Case walks to the schematic.  Looks at it. 

[character] CASE 
[dialogue] Based on the information on the probe,
[dialogue] we're finally preparing to send the
[dialogue] manned mission. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That rocket doesn't have enough thrust
[dialogue] or fuel to get you to Jupiter. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] The main ship was built in orbit. 
[dialogue] It has nuclear engines, with enough
[dialogue] fuel to last for several years. 

[action] Cooper looks at the schematic. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why are you telling me this?  I
[dialogue] already told you I'm not going to
[dialogue] tell anyone about this place.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] I know you won't, Mr. Cooper.  We're
[dialogue] telling you this because I want you
[dialogue] to join us.

[action] Cooper looks at him.  Is he serious?

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The probe has taken a great deal
[dialogue] longer to return to us than we hoped.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 37.

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Dr. Brand's Father and several other
[dialogue] members of our crew have gotten...
[dialogue] older.  

[action] Brand's Father looks down, stoic.  Brand looks angry.  

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We need someone who can run the
[dialogue] systems, improvise, work with what's
[dialogue] available.  All of the skills you
[dialogue] seem to have developed. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But I don't have any of the
[dialogue] experience.  Any training.  You people
[dialogue] have been preparing for this for
[dialogue] years. 

[action] Case shakes his head. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Humans worry about things like rank
[dialogue] and experience.  I'm only concerned
[dialogue] with whether someone would be useful.
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] I think you'd be useful, Mr. Cooper. 

[dialogue] Cooper is stunned.  This is the offer he's waited his whole
[action] life for.  And it's come too late. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No.  I'm sorry. 

[dialogue] Cooper is deeply conflicted.  But he can't leave his boys
[action] behind. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I can't help you.  I have
[dialogue] responsibilities.  Things that, no
[dialogue] offense, are more important than a
[dialogue] scientific mission. 

[action] Case shakes his head. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] I'm not a scientist, Mr. Cooper. 
[dialogue] And this is not a scientific mission. 
[dialogue] It's a rescue mission.  

[action] He rises and shakes Cooper's hand. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Brand can show you the way out.  I
[dialogue] hope you'll reconsider.  

[page_number] 38.

[action] Case steps to the door.  Cooper looks at Brand. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Rescue?  Rescue who? 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT.  CLEAN ROOM, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- NIGHT

[action] The air HISSES as it's run through an exchanger and a filter.

[action] Then the second set of doors open.  Cooper squints -- the
[action] light is blinding. 

[scene_heading] INT.  GREENHOUSE, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- NIGHT

[action] They are standing in a sealed corridor in the middle of a
[action] massive underground greenhouse.  Through the glass, Cooper
[action] can see an acre or so of corn plants.  

[action] Cooper looks at the plants.  They're all badly wilting. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The blight. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] In the last century strands were
[dialogue] limited to one or two species.  But
[dialogue] this one targets everything. 
[dialogue] Essentially it's more efficient at
[dialogue] consuming our food than we are.  

[action] Cooper looks at the dying corn. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But it doesn't affect the corn. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Not yet.  But it will.  We've grown
[dialogue] a dozen forms of it that can.  It's
[dialogue] just a matter of time before the
[dialogue] same ones develop out there.
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] The mission is to rescue us. 
[dialogue] Humanity. 

[action] She turns back from the glass. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (realizing)
[dialogue] No.  No.  We're rebuilding.  We'll
[dialogue] find something.  Some new
[dialogue] technology...  We always have.

[page_number] 39.*

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (shakes her head)
[dialogue] Who's going to find it?  The
[dialogue] universities are a joke.  People
[dialogue] like you are reduced to scavenging
[dialogue] just to get by. 
[parenthetical] (looks away)
[dialogue] The earth has had enough of us.  We
[dialogue] have two, maybe three generations
[dialogue] left.  Then our time here is over.

[action] Cooper turns back from the glass, anger growing. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You've known this for how long?  And
[dialogue] you didn't try to tell anyone? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What would be the point?  So humanity
[dialogue] could spend the last fifty years of
[dialogue] its life fighting over the scraps? 
[dialogue] It's better that they don't know.

[action] Cooper begins to argue, then stops.  He knows enough history
[action] to know she's right.  He looks at the withering crops. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] That's why you're looking for a planet
[dialogue] with oxygen.  Water.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] A new home for humanity.  We'll set
[dialogue] up a colony, then return to bring
[dialogue] more people across. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But you'd still only be able to save
[dialogue] a few hundred.  Maybe a thousand. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Would it be better if we all died?  

[action] She looks him directly in the eye. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Look -- I don't have a clue what
[dialogue] Case thinks you could add to the
[dialogue] mission.  You can come with us or
[dialogue] you can stay here and wait to die. 
[dialogue] I don't care. 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] But make no mistake -- this mission
[dialogue] is our last chance.  

[page_number] 40.

[action] Cooper looks at the wilted corn. 

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper walks Murph back to the plane. 

[action] He looks back at Brand, who holds his eye for a beat, then
[action] turns back into the light of the underground facility. 

[action] Cooper buckles Murph into his seat. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. AIRFIELD -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper's airplane touches down heavily on the runway. 

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper carries his sleeping son into the house. 

[scene_heading] INT. FARMHOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Donald is sitting at the kitchen table, lost in thought. 
[action] Cooper has told him everything. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] You get older, sometimes you just
[dialogue] want to sit back and watch it all
[dialogue] play out.  Your life.  Your kids'
[dialogue] lives.  The whole crazy story.  
[parenthetical] (looks down)
[dialogue] I didn't think I'd be around for the
[dialogue] end of it. 

[action] He looks at Cooper. 

[character] DONALD (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You have to go.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I can't go.  I have to look after
[dialogue] the boys. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] You've been preparing these boys to
[dialogue] be on their own since their mom died. 
[dialogue] Besides, I'll be here to look after
[dialogue] them, same as I've always been. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I have a responsibility to them--

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] That's right.  You do. 

[page_number] 41.

[action] Cooper looks back out the window, thinking.  The night sky
[action] is filled with stars. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT. FARMHOUSE -- DAY

[action] Cooper, duffel slung over his shoulder, stands by the door. 
[action] He gives his son, Tom, a hug.  Murph is nowhere to be seen.

[action] Cooper looks to the back of the house. 

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH'S ROOM, FARMHOUSE -- DAY

[action] Murph is sitting at his desk, crossing out numbers on a sheet
[action] covered with math.  Cooper steps inside.  

[action] He notices a packed suitcase sitting by the door.  The boy
[action] looks up, hopeful. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I've been doing the math, dad.  I
[dialogue] weigh about 85 pounds.  Now that's
[dialogue] an extra ton of fuel.  But if-

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You have to stay here, pal.  

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (distraught)
[dialogue] I heard you talking to grandpa.  I'm
[dialogue] like you.  I don't fit here, either. 
[dialogue] You know that. 

[action] Cooper puts an arm around the boy. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] There's going to be important work
[dialogue] to do here, too.

[action] Tears are streaming down the boy's face.  Cooper takes his
[action] watch off.  Looks at it. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I need you to hold onto this.  Will
[dialogue] you do that for me? 

[action] Cooper hands Murph the watch.  The boy nods, saddened. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You're not coming back, are you?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I will come back.  I promise.

[page_number] 42.

[action] Murph shakes his head, but the sadness remains.  He knows
[action] this is goodbye, even if his father doesn't. 

[action] Heart breaking, Cooper hugs his son and turns to the door. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- DAY

[action] The Piper Cub touches down.  Donald is at the controls. 
[action] Cooper climbs down, pulls out his bag.  Reaches back in and
[action] grips the old man's hand to say goodbye. 

[action] Cooper turns and walks toward the bunker.  The doors open
[action] and Tars and Doyle step out to greet him.

[action] Tars ushers Cooper onto the elevator. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don't look so happy to see me.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] One more slave when I hijack the
[dialogue] mission and start my robot colony.  

[action] Cooper looks at Tars, then Doyle, confused. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Tars was a Marine.  They gave him a
[dialogue] sense of humor to help him fit in
[dialogue] with his unit better. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Great idea.  A massive, sarcastic
[dialogue] robot.  

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I have a cue light I can turn on
[dialogue] when I'm joking, if you like.

[action] Tars points to a tiny LED over his eyebrow.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That sounds like a good idea.   

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Great.  Maybe you can use it to find
[dialogue] your way back to the ship after I
[dialogue] blow you out the airlock.

[action] Cooper looks at him.  Tars looks back, deadpan.  After a
[action] beat, the little light turns on above his right eyebrow. 

[action] The doors to the bunker begin to grind closed. 

[page_number] 43.

[scene_heading] INT. MACHINE SHOP, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- DAY

[action] Cooper walks in, still looking for a place to put his things. 

[action] Brand sees him.  He smiles in greeting.  She doesn't return
[action] the gesture.  Instead, she holds up the telemetry board. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You don't like this one, you get to
[dialogue] help me find a replacement. 

[action] She heads for the door. 

[scene_heading] EXT. BAY, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- DAY

[action] Cooper is riding in a small rubber zodiac struggling to pull
[action] on a wetsuit.  Brand is GUNNING the engine, guiding the tiny
[action] craft to a point in the middle of the bay. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I was wondering where you've been
[dialogue] getting your supplies. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We knew we'd need decades of parts
[dialogue] and materials.  The government was
[dialogue] getting rid of some things.  So we
[dialogue] arranged to take some off their hands.  

[action] She cuts the engine and hooks the boat up to a buoy.  She
[action] tosses a compact rebreather. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You know how to use one? 
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] You just breathe.  Tap my arm or
[dialogue] bang something metal if you have a
[dialogue] problem.  And don't get lost. 

[action] She picks up her rebreather and begins looking over it. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So you're a salvage diver now?  I
[dialogue] thought you were a biologist. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (unimpressed)
[dialogue] I have to be just the one thing?
[parenthetical] (patient)
[dialogue] We're not going to have a lot of
[dialogue] backup where we're going, Cooper. 
[dialogue] We all need expertise in at least
[dialogue] three fields.  Except for you, of
[dialogue] course. 

[page_number] 44.

[action] With that she rolls backwards out of the boat and into the
[action] water. 

[action] Cooper hastily fits his rebreather and follows. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  UNDERWATER, BAY -- DAY

[action] Cooper sinks underwater and begins swimming after Brand, who
[action] is descending at a rapid clip. 

[action] She pulls out a flashlight and turns it on.  A tiny beam
[action] picks out details at the bottom. 

[action] Cooper stops breathing. 

[action] The bottom of the ocean is covered with an entire fleet of
[action] the US navy.  Nuclear subs.  Battleships.  Destroyers.  

[action] Cooper remembers to breath again.  Then he hurries to catch
[action] up with Brand. 

[scene_heading] INT.  LAUNCH TUBES, NUCLEAR SUB, UNDERWATER -- DAY

[action] Cooper holds the light as Brand efficiently disassembles a
[action] ballistic missile and removes the telemetry board. 

[action] She holds it up for Cooper.  He nods.  She swims on. 

[scene_heading] INT. RESEARCH LAB, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper, dripping wet, holding the telemetry board, struggles
[action] to keep pace with Brand through stacks of equipment and years
[action] of research and experimentation. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You can set that down over there. 

[action] Cooper sets the board down.  His eye is drawn to a bizarre
[action] experiment -- an ant colony built into a massive spinning
[action] centrifuge.  Brand notices. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We didn't know what kind of gravity
[dialogue] to expect.  We experimented with
[dialogue] collective organisms in high g
[dialogue] environments. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're taking ants with us? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Humans are also collective organisms. 

[page_number] 45.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I thought humans were more solitary.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (wry)
[dialogue] Why am I not surprised? 

[action] Cooper looks at the tiny colonies of ants struggling to go
[action] about their business in the raised gravity. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Looks like hard work.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It's a paradox.  Life couldn't form
[dialogue] without gravity.  No stars.  No
[dialogue] planets.  The component pieces would
[dialogue] just drift apart.  But too much of
[dialogue] it and you're trapped. 

[action] Brand's guard relaxes a tiny bit as she talks about her work. 
[action] The moment passes quickly, and she continues on into the
[action] stack of equipment. 

[scene_heading] INT. MISSION CONTROL, UNDERGROUND HANGAR -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper watches with the rest of the crew as Case pulls up
[action] the holographical maps for their journey.

[action] Brand steps into the back of the room.  Cooper notices her
[action] and nods.  She ignores him.  

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We've updated our mission parameters
[dialogue] based on the data from the probe.   

[action] Case switches the map to a vista filled with stars and black
[action] holes.  

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Based on our latest modelling we
[dialogue] think the region on the far side of
[dialogue] the wormhole is the center of a
[dialogue] galaxy.

[action] Case zooms in on the center of the hologram: an incredibly
[action] bright mass with plasma jets firing off in either direction.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is that a star?

[action] ROTH, 50s, the crew's brilliant and blunt physicist, zooms
[action] the map in, revealing, at the center, a black heart. 

[page_number] 46.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] No.  A black hole.  There are several
[dialogue] in the region, but this is the largest --
[dialogue] a billion times heavier than the
[dialogue] sun.  I call it Gargantua.
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Beautiful, isn't it?  It's a shame
[dialogue] we won't get to see it up close.

[character] DOYLE
[parenthetical] (laughs)
[dialogue] You'd like that, wouldn't you, Roth? 
[dialogue] Falling into a massive black hole. 

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] It would answer a great deal of
[dialogue] questions I've had. 

[action] Case continues.  Doyle leans over to Cooper, conspiratorially. 

[character] DOYLE
[parenthetical] (low)
[dialogue] Don't worry about Roth.  He's nuts. 
[dialogue] But Case says that means he's ideally
[dialogue] suited for space travel.

[action] Case repositions the map near a much smaller black hole that
[action] is orbiting Gargantua.  

[character] CASE
