[dialogue] We're headed for this smaller black
[dialogue] hole.  Roth calls it Pantagruel.  We
[dialogue] think the ice planet is here-

[action] Case draws a finger through the air, leaving a red trail. 
[action] He traces the trajectory their ship will take.

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We exit the wormhole here.  And we
[dialogue] slingshot around Pantagruel to reach
[dialogue] the ice planet.  This is the period
[dialogue] in which we'll lose time. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Lose time?

[action] Roth shifts the hologram -- the stars and black holes flatten
[action] onto a sheet that bends, revealing the curvature of gravity. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] High speed or high gravity both slow
[dialogue] down time, relative to earth.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 47.

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The trip around the black hole will
[dialogue] take us only a few days.  But far
[dialogue] more time will be passing back home. 

[action] The ship's trajectory cuts through the deep gravity well of
[action] the smaller black hole to reach the ice planet. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How much time? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Based on the information from the
[dialogue] probe -- as much as five years. 

[action] Doyle looks at the tiny ship's trajectory, threaded between
[action] two black holes.  He looks worried.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I still think we're making a lot of
[dialogue] assumptions.  About the wormhole. 
[dialogue] About the planet. 
[parenthetical] (points to map)
[dialogue] The critical orbit here is incredibly
[dialogue] dangerous.  It's like walking on the
[dialogue] rim of the volcano. 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] Too fast and we get thrown off at
[dialogue] close to the speed of light.  Too
[dialogue] slow and we get pulled into the hole
[dialogue] and crushed.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] As long as we're careful, we'll make
[dialogue] it.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] How do you know that? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I find it hard to believe that someone
[dialogue] would build a wormhole to a planet
[dialogue] with water and oxygen just to lead
[dialogue] us to a dead end. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I thought you were a scientist, Brand. 
[dialogue] That sounds more like a hypothesis. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] A guess.  That's right.  We don't
[dialogue] have time to wait for conclusive
[dialogue] proof.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 48.

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] My guess is that the wormhole is
[dialogue] there because someone is trying to
[dialogue] help us.  The same way we used to
[dialogue] try to help animals when they were
[dialogue] threatened with extinction. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sure.  Till we ran out of food and
[dialogue] ate all of them. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (annoyed)
[dialogue] I guess I'm also assuming that whoever
[dialogue] built the wormhole has a better plan
[dialogue] than we did.  If I'm wrong, we'll
[dialogue] die, same as we'd die here anyway.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What do you think, Roth? 

[action] Roth leans forward, studying the map. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] If we're guessing, then I'd say
[dialogue] Brand's right.  The wormhole couldn't
[dialogue] exist naturally.  I think it's there
[dialogue] for a reason.  That someone is trying
[dialogue] to help us. 

[action] Brand looks satisfied.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] So you think we'll have no problem
[dialogue] navigating between two massive black
[dialogue] holes to a tiny planet? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] I think we'll probably be killed. 
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] I said I thought there was a plan. 
[dialogue] Not that the plan was for us to find
[dialogue] a planet like Earth to save a handful
[dialogue] of people.
[parenthetical] (shakes head)
[dialogue] Birds don't learn to fly just so
[dialogue] that they can find another egg and
[dialogue] crawl back into it.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] If that's not the plan, then what
[dialogue] are we supposed to be doing out there? 

[page_number] 49.

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] To keep moving.  Seeking.  Learning. 
[dialogue] But I don't know. 
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] We don't understand how they built
[dialogue] the wormhole.  What makes you think
[dialogue] we could understand their plan,
[dialogue] either? 

[action] Doyle gives up -- Roth is impossible. 

[action] Cooper looks at the tiny ship tracing an improbable route
[action] towards the ice planet.  What has he gotten himself into? 

[scene_heading] INT. MACHINE SHOP, UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- DAY

[action] Brand's Father is sitting at a desk, examining the corrupted
[action] data on the probe.  Tars is helping him. 

[action] Brand's Father looks up from the screen as Cooper walks up. 

[character] BRAND'S FATHER
[dialogue] Tars here needs to be disassembled. 
[dialogue] I figured you could do the honors. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (to Tars, sarcastic)
[dialogue] I thought I was going to get to enjoy
[dialogue] your company all the way to Mars.  

[action] Tars hands him a plastic waterproof case. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] You will.  My chassis is too heavy
[dialogue] for the rocket stage.  They have
[dialogue] another one waiting for me in orbit.

[action] Tars turns his back to Cooper.  Two flaps on the back of his
[action] torso slide open, revealing his control module. 

[character] TARS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] If you try to turn me into a combine
[dialogue] harvester, I'm going to--

[action] His voice cuts out as Cooper removes the chip and seals it
[action] in the briefcase.

[action] Brand's Father resumes exploring the chaos of ones and zeroes
[action] on the probe's memory.  Cooper watches.  

[character] BRAND'S FATHER
[dialogue] It's noise.  I know it's noise.  But
[dialogue] it looks too orderly.  Probably just
[dialogue] an old man seeing things. 

[page_number] 50.

[action] He shuts down the monitor. 

[scene_heading] INT. UNDERGROUND HANGAR -- DAY

[action] The crew file into the capsule, wearing their bulky
[action] spacesuits. 

[action] Cooper watches as Brand's Father seals his daughter into her
[action] suit.  He hugs her and she heads for the capsule. 

[scene_heading] INT. CONTROL CAPSULE, ROCKET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper straps himself into a seat next to Brand.  He catches
[action] her eye.  She looks away -- she's crying. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We'll be back. 

[action] It sounds like he's trying to reassure himself as much her. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I won't. 

[action] Cooper looks at her, confused.  As he does, the entire rocket
[action] SHAKES as the primary rockets begin to fire. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] If we find a habitable environment,
[dialogue] I'm staying behind to build the
[dialogue] colony. 

[action] Brand wipes her tears away and settles into the same fearless
[action] mask she usually wears.  She steals one last look out the
[action] window at Earth, then looks back.

[action] Cooper begins to say something, but stops as the entire rocket
[action] LURCHES as the primary engines FIRE. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SANTA CRUZ ISLAND -- NIGHT

[action] The desolate island is suddenly painted in color as the rocket
[action] lifts off on a massive white cloud. 

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD -- DAY

[action] Murph sits alone on a rise overlooking the massive co-op
[action] farms.  Behind him the combines continue to work, oblivious. 

[action] Murph watches a tiny vapor trail as it races for the heavens. 
[action] His father's watch dangles from his wrist. 

[scene_heading] INT. CAPSULE -- NIGHT

[action] The interior of the capsule SHAKES with incredible violence
[action] as the rocket is lifted up on a giant, continuous explosion. 

[page_number] 51.

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE, NEAR EARTH ORBIT

[action] The rocket sheds one stage, then another, until finally the
[action] naked capsule reaches the blackness of space and rockets on. 

[scene_heading] INT. CAPSULE

[action] Cooper looks through the tiny porthole into inky blackness.

[action] As they get closer, he makes out a looming matte black
[action] structure that passes light from the stars directly through.  

[action] In the center of the structure, Cooper can see a globe-like
[action] ship covered in the same refractive material: the ENDURANCE.

[scene_heading] INT. SPACE STATION

[action] The door cracks open and equalizes with a HISS.  Case, more
[action] comfortable in the zero gravity environment than the humans,
[action] hauls himself through. 

[action] They are greeted by a group of robots painted in the same
[action] material as the ship -- the engineers who built and have
[action] maintained the Endurance for thirty years. 

[scene_heading] INT. MACHINE SHOP, SPACE STATION

[action] One of the robots leads Cooper through a long lab-like room
[action] filled with machines capable of fabricating almost anything
[action] imaginable.  Cooper looks like a kid in a candy store.

[action] The robot reaches a vacuum-sealed package.  Cuts it open,
[action] revealing a bipedal frame. 

[action] Cooper begins LAUGHING -- Tar's new body is beautifully
[action] designed, but tiny, only about four feet tall. 

[character] ENGINEER ROBOT
[dialogue] Would you like me to install the
[dialogue] chip? 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Oh, no.  I want to see this. 

[action] Cooper takes Tars's chip out of its plastic safety case and
[action] looks it over.  The engineer opens a bay in the back of the
[action] frame and Cooper slides the chip inside. 

[action] The frame begins its "handshake" -- lights illuminate on the
[action] body, muscles flicker from a long gestation.  The eyes open. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Good morning, sunshine. 

[page_number] 52.

[action] Tars takes one or two steps forward, rotating his arms --
[action] the robot equivalent of a stretch.  Cooper can barely hide
[action] his mirth at Tars's newfound lack of stature. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Bit of a demotion. 

[action] Tars turns back toward Cooper.  He puffs out his chest and
[action] suddenly his stubby arms and legs telescope, unfolding into
[action] long, svelte limbs.  When he's done he stands eight feet
[action] tall, even more impressive than his earthbound frame.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I wouldn't call it that, exactly.

[action] He reaches out an arm and pats Cooper on the shoulder. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENDURANCE, SPACE STATION

[action] Cooper and the others haul themselves into the ship.  To
[action] Cooper's surprise, it's quite compact, and divided into two
[action] chambers, like nestled spheres. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The ship is tiny. 

[action] Doyle, squeezing past him, smiles at Cooper's surprise. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] So is the wormhole. 

[action] Doyle pats Cooper on the back.  Cooper begins hauling gear
[action] inside. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE, NEAR EARTH ORBIT

[action] In complete silence, the Endurance detaches from the space
[action] station and rolls gently away. 

[action] After a moment, its nuclear engines fire and the Endurance
[action] begins to accelerate steadily away from the Earth. 

[scene_heading] INT. SPACE STATION

[action] The engineer robots who built the Endurance watch as their
[action] creation disappears into space. 

[action] Their mission is complete.  One by one, they shut down. 

[scene_heading] INT. CREW QUARTERS, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew watch through a translucent section of the ship's
[action] hull as the Earth gets steadily smaller. 

[action] Then they settle in for the long journey to the wormhole.  

[page_number] 53.

[scene_heading] INT. CREW QUARTERS, ENDURANCE

[action] Tars is hunched over a small communications relay, one hand
[action] is holding a paint brush -- he is making delicate strokes,
[action] painting the relay a bright blue. 

[action] Cooper watches him for a moment.  Tars looks up. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] It's the comms relay.  It will allow
[dialogue] us to talk to earth, even on the far
[dialogue] side of the wormhole. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I know.  So why are you painting it? 

[action] Tars looks almost bashful. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] It helps me calibrate my fine motor
[dialogue] control. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sure it does. 
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] You're pretty good. 

[action] Tars double checks his work. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I learned it during the war.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What'd you paint? 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Tombstones.

[action] Cooper watches him finish in silence.

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper watches a highly-compressed video of his son, Tom,
[action] talking about school. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] They said I can start an agriculture
[dialogue] class a year early.

[action] Cooper shakes his head. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I've got to go, Dad.  Hope you're
[dialogue] safe up there. 

[page_number] 54.

[action] Tom gets up to leave.  Donald sits down in his place. 

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I'm sorry, Coop.  I asked Murph to
[dialogue] record you a message but he's still...
[dialogue] well, he's still angry with you. 
[dialogue] I'll try again next week. 

[action] The video cuts out.  Cooper stares at the darkened screen.

[scene_heading] INT. ENGINE ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper, Brand and Tars are moving the bundles of colonization
[action] equipment into bins along the wall of the craft. 

[action] Tars pulls a stack of equipment out the stack and stows it
[action] against the wall.  Cooper copies the procedure. 

[action] They labor in silence, working their way along the hull. 
[action] Cooper gets quicker with each bundle, keeping pace with Tars. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Be careful.  It's difficult to gauge
[dialogue] mass in zero gravity. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How much do these things weigh?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Four tons. 

[action] Cooper looks at the incredibly heavy bundle spinning easily
[action] in his hands.  He tries to stop it.  Catches his hand. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Damn. 

[action] He jerks his hand away, then pushes himself after the spinning
[action] bundle, trying to stop it before it can damage the hull. 
[action] Tars helps him catch it inches from the hull wall. 

[action] Brand floats over to Cooper, smiling at him the whole time
[action] with an exaggerated grin.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Smile. 

[action] Cooper smiles, taken aback by Brand's sudden friendliness. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Because it lowers your blood pressure. 

[page_number] 55.

[action] Cooper looks at his hand.  Blood is pouring out of his palm
[action] in large glistening bubbles. 

[scene_heading] INT. INFIRMARY, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper, slightly embarrassed, is seated while Case is hunched
[action] over his hand, sewing the meat of it back together with
[action] perfect little stitches. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] How are you feeling?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fine.  The anesthetic is working. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] No -- I mean how is your mood?  You
[dialogue] seem to be developing good relations
[dialogue] with everyone on the mission.  Except
[dialogue] perhaps Ms. Brand.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (embarrassed)
[dialogue] You worry about my hand and I'll
[dialogue] worry about my mood. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Only five percent of my resources
[dialogue] are devoted to human anatomy.  Ten
[dialogue] percent is the mission protocol. 
[dialogue] The rest is human psychology. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We are floating in a total vacuum in
[dialogue] a plastic ship powered by nuclear
[dialogue] engines.  But the most dangerous
[dialogue] thing onboard is the three pounds of
[dialogue] organic material in your skull. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] If we're such a liability, why take
[dialogue] us along?  You and Tars could build
[dialogue] the colony without us.  You wouldn't
[dialogue] need to bring food or oxygen. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Because humans, despite your obvious
[dialogue] physical shortcomings, are better at
[dialogue] surviving than we are.  Your
[dialogue] programming is better than ours. 

[page_number] 56.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Humans aren't programmed. 

[action] Case stops, looking Cooper in the eye. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Would you prefer I was honest?  These
[dialogue] things can be uncomfortable for
[dialogue] humans. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Did they program you to be
[dialogue] condescending? 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Yes.  Of course. 
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] But you're not supposed to notice. 

[action] Case finishes the stitches.  Ties off the end. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Humans are good at surviving because
[dialogue] evolution gave you magical thinking --
[dialogue] the idea that your relationships
[dialogue] mean something.  You can't explain
[dialogue] the feelings, so you think of them
[dialogue] as irrational.  But they're not. 
[dialogue] They're programming. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (defensive)
[dialogue] My relationships aren't programming. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Exactly.  You believe it so much you
[dialogue] won't listen to me. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How would that make us better at
[dialogue] surviving? 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] When I die, the last thing I will
[dialogue] see will be a diagnostic of my own
[dialogue] power cycle.  Would you like to know
[dialogue] the last thing you will see? 

[action] Cooper hesitates.  Case senses the jump in his heart rate. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] This conversation is making you
[dialogue] uncomfortable.  We should stop. 

[page_number] 57.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No.  I want to know. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] The last thing you will see before
[dialogue] you die will be your  children . 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] Your mind does this to you to get
[dialogue] you to fight a little harder to
[dialogue] survive, to try to return to them,
[dialogue] even if death is certain. 

[action] Cooper looks away, overcome for a second with emotion.  Case
[action] watches him, gauging his mood, whether he has said too much. 

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper begins recording a message.  He looks unsure. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We've almost reached the wormhole.  
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] Just in case anything happens, I
[dialogue] just wanted to say...
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] I love you boys.  And I hope whatever
[dialogue] your lives become, whatever is coming
[dialogue] your way... you make the most of it.  

[action] Cooper stops recording.  Looks at the equipment, thinking it
[action] over.  Erases the message.  Stands to leave.

[action] As he steps to the door he notices Brand watching him through
[action] the window.  She looks away.  

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper steps out.  Brand looks up at him. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We'll be able to communicate with
[dialogue] Earth even from the far side of the
[dialogue] wormhole.

[action] Cooper smiles, grateful for this small kindness. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I know.  Thank you.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I can't imagine how tough it would
[dialogue] be to leave your kids behind. 

[page_number] 58.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You never had any?  I thought I was
[dialogue] a pariah for only having two. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Hard to settle down when you've spent
[dialogue] your life waiting to leave the planet. 

[action] Cooper looks at the holographic model of the black hole
[action] system.  The ice planet looks precariously balanced, orbiting
[action] the smaller black hole. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Strange place to look for a new home.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You wouldn't want to get too close
[dialogue] to the surface of the sun, either. 
[dialogue] Black holes are a more stable supply
[dialogue] of power than stars in many ways. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You really think there's a plan?  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I hope so.
[parenthetical] (looks at him)
[dialogue] You don't?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I guess I just think we're on our
[dialogue] own.  

[action] Cooper looks at the impossibly complicated system of black
[action] holes orbiting each other.

[scene_heading] EXT. LAGRANGE POINT, SPACE

[action] Behind the ship, the sun is a distant light, not much bigger
[action] than the other stars.

[character] CASE (O.S.)
[dialogue] We've reached the wormhole. 

[action] The ship slows as it nears a tiny, crystal mouth, just four
[action] meters or so in diameter. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Case looks at the wormhole on the screen.  It glows with the
[action] light of stars billions of light-years away.  

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Deploy the comms relay. 

[page_number] 59.

[action] Cooper moves to the communications touchscreen.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE

[action] The relay is released from the ship, and we finally get a
[action] look at Tars' paint job -- the stubby device looks like a
[action] 20th century mail box. 

[action] The relay drifts in space.  A signal light illuminates as it
[action] sends a test packet of data to the ship. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper watches the comms screen.  After a moment it TONES as
[action] it receives a data packet from Earth. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Everyone take their station for
[dialogue] transit.  We're heading into the
[dialogue] wormhole mouth.

[action] Cooper and the rest begin folding away equipment in the
[action] control room and moving into the outer layer of the ship. 

[action] Case initiates a sequence on the keypad and the nuclear
[action] engines disengage themselves from the ship. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE, SPACE

[action] The nuclear engines drift a safe distance back from the ship
[action] and then snap tight on their tether.

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER HULL, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew move into the tight, claustrophobic outer layer of
[action] the ship.  They will have very little room as the ship passes
[action] through the wormhole. 

[action] Tars detaches his legs in order to take up less space during
[action] transit.  Then he tethers himself to the hull wall.

[action] Case is the last to join them.  He detaches a small control
[action] module from the console, then pulls himself into the outer
[action] hull and seals the passageway. 

[action] Case presses a button on the control module.  With a GROAN,
[action] the hull walls of the ship begin to BEND. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE, SPACE

[action] The ship's hull begins to CRACK open, revealing the inner
[action] chamber.  

[page_number] 60.

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER HULL, ENDURANCE

[action] The SHUDDERING continues.  The crew watch nervously as the
[action] control chamber below them suddenly opens itself to the cold
[action] blackness of space. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE, SPACE

[action] The ship silently rolls itself into position, pointing the
[action] opening in its hull toward the wormhole mouth.  

[action] Slowly, the Endurance pushes itself forward, closer and closer
[action] to the crystal-like mouth.  Finally, it envelops the mouth,
[action] bringing it into the open chamber. 

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER HULL, ENDURANCE

[action] As the crew watch, the wormhole mouth is positioned in the
[action] center of the inner compartment. 

[action] Case presses a sequence key on the control panel and the
[action] ship's hull closes again, trapping the wormhole inside it.

[action] With a GRUMBLE, the ship begins contracting, squeezing itself
[action] down around the wormhole mouth. 

[action] Cooper takes a deep breath as the center of the ship begins
[action] gently lowering itself into the wormhole mouth, feeding itself
[action] into the wormhole from the inside out.  Cooper watches as
[action] Doyle is swallowed into the compressing ship with a GRUNT. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Would you like me to make a joke? 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (firm)
[dialogue] No. 

[action] Cooper's turn: he is fed into the hole, legs first, then
[action] waist, torso, and, finally, his head. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE

[action] The Endurance shrinks as it sinks from the inside out into
[action] the wormhole. 

[action] After a moment, it's gone.  The only thing left behind is
[action] the comms relay, drifting in space, waiting for a signal. 

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER HULL, ENDURANCE

[action] The ship continues to slide through the wormhole.  Through
[action] the outer hull they see images of themselves repeating -- a
[action] trick of the narrow collar of space they are sinking through. 
[action] Cooper smiles at himself.  The experience is unnerving. 

[page_number] 61.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where are we? 

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (smiling)
[dialogue] Nowhere.  Nowhere at all. 

[action] Nowhere is still pretty damn claustrophobic.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] The hull is intact.  Thirty more
[dialogue] seconds transit. 

[action] For a moment, the ship slides gently, silently, through the
[action] wormhole.  The quiet is eery. 

[action] Suddenly, a point of distortion appears in the hull next to
[action] Cooper.  It looks like someone is pushing against the hull
[action] of the ship with a giant finger. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Something's happening to the hull
[dialogue] over here. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Hull integrity is fine. 

[action] The distortion moves along the hull, growing in diameter. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Well, I don't know what your display
[dialogue] is telling you but something is
[dialogue] happening over here. 

[action] Suddenly, along the hull, Doyle speaks up, panicked. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I've got a problem over here, too. 

[action] Doyle is watching a separate distortion move across the inner
[action] wall of the ship.  This one seems to be TWISTING the material
[action] of the hull. 

[action] Suddenly, the point in front of Cooper detaches itself from
[action] the outer wall and moves through the space in front of him,
[action] bending the empty space itself, distorting the ship behind
[action] it like a sphere-shaped magnifying glass. 

[character] DOYLE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] It's not the hull... it's inside the
[dialogue] ship... it's...

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] It's beautiful. 

[page_number] 62.

[action] Roth watches as the distortions move through the ship.  His
[action] curiosity doesn't make anyone else feel any better. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What the hell are they, Case? 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] I don't know.  It could be
[dialogue] gravitational turbulence. 

[action] The twisting sphere in front of Doyle begins to grow. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It's getting bigger. 

[action] Doyle puts up his hand to defend himself.  The sphere absorbs
[action] it, twisting Doyle's hand.  Doyle CRIES OUT. 

[action] His hand is twisted completely around, impossibly mangled. 
[action] But Doyle, hyperventilating, isn't in any pain.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] It's not bending your hand.  It's
[dialogue] bending the space around your hand.

[action] The sphere begins to pass through Doyle's body.  Doyle is
[action] freaking out. 

[action] The sphere in front of Cooper makes contact with him, also. 
[action] He holds his breath as it touches him, squeezing and
[action] distorting his body. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This isn't turbulence.  Look at the
[dialogue] way they're moving -- it's like
[dialogue] something's examining us. 

[action] Cooper watches the sphere distort his arm, running along the
[action] length of it. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Can you ask it to stop?

[action] Suddenly, as quickly as they appeared, the distortions vanish. 
[action] For a moment, the crew is silent, still spellbound by the
[action] encounter. 

[action] An ALARM sounds.  Suddenly, the inner chamber begins to
[action] distort from a spherical shape to bispherical: two spheres
[action] joined.  The ship GROANS as if it's being pulled apart. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] The wormhole is splitting into two
[dialogue] paths.  Radiation is pouring from
[dialogue] one path to the other. 

[page_number] 63.

[action] Case stares at the controls.  Decides. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Release the second mouth. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] None of our testing involved opening
[dialogue] the ship  inside  the wormhole.  We
[dialogue] have no idea what might happen.  

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We're going to find out. 

[action] Doyle reaches over to an auxiliary panel and punches in a
[action] sequence. 

[action] After a moment, the hull cracks open, creating a channel
[action] through which the radiating mouth can escape. 

[action] The opening in the ship allows the pressure to begin to
[action] concentrate on one fracture point.  The ship SHUDDERS as
[action] it's squeezed along its axis. 

[action] Case punches in a code and the ship begins to close again,
[action] painstakingly slowly.  Finally, the ship calms as it closes
[action] around the original wormhole. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We're reaching the far end of the
[dialogue] wormhole.  

[action] Suddenly, the wormhole mouth begins to grow inside the inner
[action] chamber.  What was a ball of light spreads out into a black
[action] canvas studded with points of light -- like looking into the
[action] universe through the wrong end of a telescope. 

[action] Cooper presses himself against the wall and holds on as the
[action] hull beneath him opens outward. 

[scene_heading] EXT. WARPED SIDE OF THE UNIVERSE

[action] The Endurance emerges from the opposite end of the wormhole
[action] from the inside out.  It drifts in space. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] For a moment, the crew are silent, taking it in. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Look at that. 

[action] Roth is looking through the translucent panel on the hull. 

[action] They are on the warped side of the universe. 

[page_number] 64.

[scene_heading] EXT. WORMHOLE MOUTH, WARPED SIDE OF THE UNIVERSE

[action] The vista is dazzling -- an ocean of massive stars and black
[action] holes, some adorned with jets and brilliant gas disks. 

[action] At the center, like a king at the center of his court, is
[action] Gargantua, plasma jets spewing from its poles. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew pull themselves back into the control deck from the
[action] outer hull.  Their incredible surroundings are visible in
[action] all directions as Endurance passes the light into the cabin.  

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Reconfigure the engines and test the
[dialogue] communications array. 

[action] The crew break themselves away from the view and get to work. 
[action] Roth begins adapting his models of the local system with the
[action] real observational data pouring in from the ship's
[action] instruments.

[action] Cooper pulls himself to the comms post.  PINGS the relay
[action] they left on the far side of the wormhole. 

[scene_heading] EXT. WORMHOLE MOUTH 'A', SPACE

[action] The blue and white comms relay LIGHTS up as it receives a
[action] packet through the wormhole.

[scene_heading] INT. ENDURANCE

[action] After a nervous moment, the comms computer TONES with a
[action] response -- they're still in touch with the Earth. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It's working. 

[action] Tars finishes reeling the nuclear engines back in from their
[action] tether and locks them into place. 

[action] Roth's updated model appears on the monitors.  Brand stares
[action] at it, startled. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We're moving. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] That's not possible.  We haven't
[dialogue] activated the engines yet. 

[action] The ship's skin illuminates, overlaying a plotted course on
[action] top of the view.  

[page_number] 65.

[action] They are moving, very rapidly, on a course that leads directly
[action] between the black hulks of Gargantua and Pantagruel. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Roth.  Why are we moving?

[action] Roth looks at the stars, then back to his model. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] The smaller black hole.  We're much
[dialogue] closer to it than the models
[dialogue] predicted.  We're being pulled by
[dialogue] its swirl.  Very quickly.

[action] The crew looks out through the hull.  They are being pulled
[action] into Pantagruel's swirl -- a glittering disc of matter
[action] spinning at high speed around the hole. 

[action] Doyle looks behind them.  The wormhole mouth is rapidly
[action] growing smaller. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Doyle.  Fire the engines.  Now. 

[action] The ship's engines FIRE, straining to fight the irresistible
[action] pull of the supermassive black hole.  They won't be able to
[action] fight it for long. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We're being pulled into it? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] No.  I don't think so...

[action] Roth looks at the instrumentation for a moment. 

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] It appears to be pulling us on exactly
[dialogue] the trajectory we modelled.  If we
[dialogue] try to fight it, we could push
[dialogue] ourselves off of that trajectory. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] You don't know that. 

[action] Case stares ahead into the darkness. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] We should turn off the engines.  Let
[dialogue] the swirl take us. 

[character] DOYLE
[parenthetical] (frantic)
[dialogue] Listen to me.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 66.

[character] DOYLE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] If the modelling was wrong, then we
[dialogue] can't be sure about anything.  We
[dialogue] need to go back.  

[action] Case thinks it over.  Decides. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Shut down the engines.  

[action] The engines shut down.  

[action] SILENCE.  The ship drifts for a moment in the swirl, then
[action] begins to move. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE

[action] The ship accelerates as it is pulled by the irresistible
[action] force of the black hole onto an inspiraling orbit. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew watch as the ship hurtles around the black hole at
[action] higher and higher speeds. 

[action] The sky overhead begins to spin due to their motion -- faster
[action] and faster until it becomes a blur.  The ship begins to GROAN
[action] as it appears to be pulled in two different directions. 

[action] The ship is now speeding around the black hole at incredible
[action] speed -- one revolution every four seconds.  The crew are
[action] suddenly SLAMMED against the hull in opposite directions --
[action] some towards the black hole, some towards the opposite side.  

[action] Roth looks at his model, which shows the projected path of
[action] the ship.  It looks perilously close to the event horizon. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] It's the tidal gravity caused by the
[dialogue] black hole.  It means we're right on
[dialogue] the critical orbit. 

[action] The comms screen lights up, TONING again and again, as it
[action] receives a long garbled update.  Then it shuts down.  Cooper
[action] drags himself along the hull until he reaches the controls. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We've lost contact with the relay. 

[action] Case joins Cooper at the comms screen.  

[dialogue] While they're distracted, Doyle maneuvers himself over to
[action] the engine control. 

[page_number] 67.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] One long garbled transmission came
[dialogue] in.  Then nothing. 

[action] Case looks at the screen. 

[action] Suddenly, the ship is JOLTED as the engines fire at full
[action] power. 

[action] The crew looks over.  Doyle is standing by the controls.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I can't let you kill us. 
[dialogue] We have to go back. 

[action] The engines strain to fight the swirl -- but they're
[action] hopelessly outgunned by the gravity of the black hole.  

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE

[action] Instead of reversing course, the Endurance begins to speed
[action] up, as it plummets closer and closer to the black hole. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Doyle's face sinks as he watches the controls -- on the
[action] 'volcano' model, the ship is now passing the crest and
[action] spiraling towards destruction.  He has made a tragic mistake. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Why isn't it slowing down?

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] We're being pulled towards the event
[dialogue] horizon. 

[action] ALARMS begin sounding throughout the ship as the projected
[action] course on Roth's model shifts, showing the Endurance being
[action] pushed up the rim, past the critical orbit and down towards
[action] the black hole's event horizon. 

[action] Case takes control of the ship, trying to fire the engines
[action] forwards to speed them back up to safety, but it's too late. 

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (calm)
[dialogue] The engines don't have enough power
[dialogue] to push us back. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] They would if we used it all at once. 

[action] Cooper is still trying to understand what Roth means as Tars
[action] locks himself into the engine compartment. 

[page_number] 68.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What is he doing? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Saving us. 

[action] Tars tears open the engine's control panel and begins
[action] overriding it. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE

[action] Tars rips out the cooling circuitry.  Then, holding on tight,
[action] he fires the engine. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew watch as Tars holds the engine, blasting it at full
[action] power into the swirl.  The engine heats up white-hot.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Prime the remaining engine.  When he
[dialogue] detonates it, we'll only have a few
[dialogue] seconds.  If we overshoot we could
[dialogue] be pulled into the bigger hole. 

[action] Roth moves to the controls. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Secure yourselves.  The ship should
[dialogue] be able to withstand the blast. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What about Tars?

[action] As the crew watches, Tars continues to hold the engine even
[action] as the casing around it begins to melt.  Finally, it EXPLODES. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE

[action] Tars is thrown backwards from the explosion, tumbling through
[action] space as the ship is ROCKETED upwards. 

[scene_heading] INT. ENDURANCE

[action] The ship is SLAMMED by the explosion.  Cooper and the rest
[action] of the crew are SMASHED against the hull.  Doyle is knocked
[action] unconscious.  Brand steps over to him and cradles his head,
[action] trying to protect him. 

[action] On Roth's model, the course slowly pushes outward, out of
[action] the danger zone, back to the original delicate orbit. 

[action] As they near the original orbit, Case fires the remaining
[action] engine, pushing them back onto the outspiraling orbit. 

[page_number] 69.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] It's going to be close, but we're
[dialogue] going to make it. 

[action] Brand looks at the instruments.  Points to a tiny radar
[action] contact receding towards the massive black hole. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Tars.  His transponder is still
[dialogue] working.  

[action] The ship's instruments TONE every few seconds as it
[action] communicates with Tars' onboard computer. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] He's being pulled toward Gargantua. 
[dialogue] We can't help him. 

[action] The crew watch, helpless, as the tones grow further and
[action] further apart.  Then they stop. 

[action] Cooper looks at Doyle, anger rising.  Then he looks down. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE AROUND PANTAGRUEL

[action] The Endurance slowly spirals back away from Pantagruel, the
[action] sky slowing as the orbit grows longer. 

[action] Finally, a tiny speck of light appears in front of the ship. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Roth watches as the Endurance races back away from the black
[action] hole and into a perfect orbit around a tiny ice planet.

[action] Roth looks up from his screen, smiling.  

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] We're here. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] As Roth and Doyle study the frozen planet below, Cooper tries
[action] in vain to signal the relay on the Earth side of the wormhole.  

[action] Brand and Case are having a private conversation away from
[action] everyone else.  Cooper walks over. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I still can't contact the relay. 

[action] Brand ignores him at first.  She nods at Case. 

[page_number] 70.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Did you hear me?  We're not able to
[dialogue] communicate back home. 

[action] She waves him off.  But she looks shaken by something. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I've already checked it.  It's
[dialogue] electromagnetic interference.  We'll
[dialogue] try again in a few hours. 

[action] Cooper begins to ask her what's wrong, but she moves off. 

[action] Roth looks up from his monitor, excited. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] I've got a signal.  But it's local. 

[action] A familiar CHIRP plays over the ship's speakers.  

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The remaining probes.  We found them. 

[action] She looks at the monitor.  The probes ping the ship with
[action] their locations, which pop up on the monitor.  They're all
[action] clustered in one spot. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How could they all have landed in
[dialogue] the same place?

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Let's go find out. 

[action] Case programs a course that will lock the ship in orbit above
[action] the probes on the surface. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SPACE ABOVE ICE PLANET

[action] The ship settles into orbit a few hundred kilometers above
[action] the surface of the ice planet.  It can't get any closer:

[action] The space below it is choked with hundreds of tiny moons --
[action] a cruder version of Saturn's rings.  The moons are hurtling
[action] around the planet at high speed. 

[scene_heading] INT.  AIRLOCK, ENDURANCE

[action] Brand ushers Doyle into the landing module.  Cooper objects. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're bringing him along? 

[page_number] 71.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We need all the help we can get. 
[dialogue] Besides, the alternative is to leave
[dialogue] him on the ship alone.  You think
[dialogue] he'll still be here when we get back?  

[action] Cooper steps aside, allowing Doyle onboard. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE ABOVE ICE PLANET

[action] The lander detaches from Endurance, rolls over on its belly,
[action] and FIRES thrusters to sink towards the planet's surface. 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER

[action] The crew watches, fascinated, as the ship descends, navigating
[action] between the moons that hurtle past.  

[action] The moons are vastly different than our own; potato shaped
[action] and only one to two miles in circumference, they are hugged
[action] in a close embrace with the ice planet, only a few hundred
[action] thousand feet from the surface. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] The lander touches down on the ghostly surface of the planet. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] After a moment, the hatch HISSES open and the team steps
[action] out, led by Case, holding a rifle. 

[action] The team sets out, moving slowly in their cumbersome suits. 

[action] Lit by the nebula, the surroundings are a little brighter
[action] than a full moon on Earth.  

[action] Not that there's much to look at.  They are standing on a
[action] sea of ice, which spreads for miles.  In the distance, small
[action] rock formations break through the ice.  

[action] Brand takes a surface reading of the ice. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (radio)
[dialogue] The probe was right.  Looks like
[dialogue] significant amounts of oxygen trapped
[dialogue] in the ice. 

[action] Case leads the way, drawn by a signal only he can hear.  He
[action] walks fifty yards, then stops on a gentle slope that leads
[action] down into a small valley. 

[page_number] 72.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] The other probes should be directly
[dialogue] beneath us. 

[action] Case and the others begin digging into the hillside.  Cooper
[action] is drawn to a small mound in the middle of the valley, four
[action] feet high.  He takes out a small folding shovel and begins
[action] scraping at the ice and snow. 

[action] Case hits something solid.  But it's not a probe.  He digs
[action] around a little more, then reaches up and hacks away at the
[action] hillside, revealing the outline of something metal:

[action] A door.  They're not standing at the base of a hill -- it's
[action] a shelter.  The crew stands back, unsure what to make of it. 

[action] A few feet away, Cooper cuts enough of the mound away to
[action] reveal something flexible -- fabric caked with ice.  He
[action] scrapes away the snow, revealing a bright patch of red fabric:

[action] It's the flag of the People's Republic of China. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] How could the Chinese have gotten
[dialogue] here first?  The federal government
[dialogue] kept the wormhole a secret.

[action] Case shrugs.  He has a soldier's gallows humor about his old
[action] employer's ability to keep anything a secret. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] They didn't do a very good job.

[action] Case reaches for the door of the shelter.  It's sealed shut
[action] with ice.  He wrenches it open. 

[scene_heading] INT. CONTROL MODULE, CHINESE BASE CAMP -- NIGHT

[action] Case steps into the shelter, followed by the rest of the
[action] crew.  His lights pick up several years worth of dust. 

[action] Case steps up to an equipment locker.  Forces it open.  Inside
[action] are half a dozen black probes.  Exposed to light, the probes
[action] begin TONING like the one Cooper found in Texas. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] The Chinese must have captured them. 
[dialogue] So they couldn't return to us. 

[action] The crew stare at the probes for a moment, taking it in. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Then how did the probe that Cooper
[dialogue] found return?

[page_number] 73.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] The more immediate question is what
[dialogue] happened to the Chinese expedition. 
[dialogue] There's no sign of their ship in
[dialogue] orbit.  And they never returned to
[dialogue] earth. 

[action] Roth picks up an ancient vacuum sealed package of pickled
[action] egg.  Virtually none of the rations have been eaten. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  VALLEY, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper and Brand scrape ice from one of the other small hills
[action] surrounding the valley, revealing another structure.  Cooper
[action] forces the door open. 

[scene_heading] INT.  BARRACKS, CHINESE BASE CAMP, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper and Brand let themselves in.  The shelter is well
[action] stocked with food and equipment. 

[action] Brand is taking readings with a radiation detector. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The whole place has been dosed with
[dialogue] huge levels of radiation.  What
[dialogue] happened here? 

[action] Cooper looks at the pieces of a modular, one-person rocket,
[action] a last-ditch means of escape, stored, untouched in the
[action] shelter.  He steps back, realizing something.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This is the Taichung mission. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The Mars mission?  The Chinese claimed
[dialogue] it had been destroyed on landing. 

[action] Cooper looks over the relay log. 

[character] COOPER 
[dialogue] They never landed.  Not on Mars. 
[dialogue] Four human crew,  fifteen robots. 
[dialogue] The log says the base has been here
[dialogue] for thirty years.

[action] Case's familiar voice comes through the radio. 

[character] CASE (O.S.)
[dialogue] I've found something.

[page_number] 74.

[scene_heading] INT. UTILITY MODULE, CHINESE BASE CAMP -- NIGHT

[action] The structure is filled with drilling equipment.  Cooper and
[action] the rest of the team stand at the edge of a three-foot hole
[action] that has been drilled into the ice. 

[action] A descending rig is anchored to the top of the hole.  Doyle
[action] and Case struggle to pull themselves out of the hole. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It goes down for a hundred and fifty
[dialogue] feet, then stops.  Tunnel's old --
[dialogue] the ice has reformed at the bottom.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I'm going to descend to take some
[dialogue] samples.  Cooper, want to make
[dialogue] yourself useful? 

[action] Cooper begins to say something, then bites his tongue. 

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper emerges, loaded down with several containers of Brand's
[action] equipment. 

[action] As he struggles through the wind and snow, his radio picks
[action] up data chatter between the mother ship and Case.  The comms
[action] are just noise.  After a moment, Case's voice cuts in. 

[character] CASE (O.S.)
[dialogue] Get back here, Cooper.  We've got a
[dialogue] problem.  

[action] Cooper continues to wade through the snow.  Suddenly, he
[action] trips over something, dropping the equipment. 

[action] Cooper looks back.  He tripped on a white plastic post
[action] sticking out of the snow.  He dusts it off, to reveal a
[action] picture of a Chinese Taikonaut smiling in his flight suit. 
[action] This is a grave marker.

[action] Cooper sweeps his hand through the snow, revealing three
[action] more white posts with pictures.  The entire human crew has
[action] been buried here. 

[scene_heading] INT. UTILITY MODULE, CHINESE BASE CAMP -- NIGHT

[action] Roth and Case are looking at a portable monitor, showing a
[action] projection of the nearby system -- the ice planet is a speck
[action] orbiting the local black hole.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The entire human crew is buried out
[dialogue] there in the snow. 

[page_number] 75.

[action] Roth looks up from the monitor and assesses the situation
[action] with his usual detachment. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] I think I know what killed them. 
[dialogue] This planet isn't the only thing
[dialogue] orbiting this black hole. 

[action] Roth zooms the monitor in, revealing a tiny, impossibly-bright
[action] object appearing at the horizon of the black hole. 

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] It's a neutron star.  The black hole
[dialogue] shields the planet's surface from it
[dialogue] for twenty hours at a stretch.  Time's
[dialogue] about up.

[action] Even Brand looks rattled. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] How long do we have left?

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] About five minutes. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Then what? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Then we die.  The radiation will
[dialogue] cook us alive. 

[action] Cooper looks around, their predicament settling in.  Case
[action] walks over to the edge of the mine shaft. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We have to go down. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What about returning to the ship?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Not enough time.  Case is right. 
[dialogue] The ice can shield us from some of
[dialogue] the x-rays. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It would take a thousand feet of ice
[dialogue] to shield us.  

[action] Brand gives up on the argument, sheds her equipment and clips
[action] herself to the line to follow Case down into the ice. 

[action] Cooper looks at Doyle, then follows her. 

[page_number] 76.

[scene_heading] INT. ICE MINE SHAFT -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper descends into the shaft.  The only light is from his
[action] suit's light array. 

[action] He reaches the bottom of the shaft, which opens into a small
[action] cave, the ice ribbed in wave-like patterns like the seafloor . 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What is this? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] A pocket formed by gas.  There may
[dialogue] be more below. 

[action] The ice below them reveals nothing but murky blackness. 
[action] Case has found some of the equipment left behind by the
[action] Chinese -- a battery-powered drill and a pick. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We'll never make it far enough down. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Shut up.   How much time do we have,
[dialogue] Roth?

[action] Roth checks his watch with his usual detachment.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] About three minutes or so until we're
[dialogue] fully exposed. 

[action] The rad meter Brand is holding begins to CHIRP with activity --
[action] the radiation levels are rising.  The ice will not be enough
[action] to protect them. 

[action] Cooper sees that Brand has exhausted herself with the pick. 
[action] He takes over for her.

[action] The drill that Case is operating GROANS as the thirty-year-
[action] old battery runs out of charge.  Case casts it aside and
[action] begins SMASHING at the ice with his bare hand.

[action] Brand is looking at the walls of the ice pocket, looking for
[action] fissures.  Her lights pick out something in the ice.  She
[action] looks closer:

[action] Tiny black flecks.  Brand, ever the scientist, forgets their
[action] predicament and begins chipping at the ice.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I've found something. 

[action] Case continues to pummel the ice with his hand, which is
[action] badly smashed.  

[page_number] 77.

[action] He pulls off the hand and continues to hack away at the ice
[action] with the stump, trying to save his crew. 

[action] Brand is examining a piece of ice in her hands, which contains
[action] several black flecks.  As she shines her light on them, they
[action] FLUORESCE, giving off a tiny flicker of light, like a firefly. 
[action] Brand steps back. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] These things... they're  alive . 

[action] Roth joins her, looking down at the tiny creatures trapped
[action] in the ice.  Brand shines her flashlight over the ice again,
[action] but nothing happens.  Roth takes the depth meter dangling by
[action] a lanyard from Brand's suit.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] It's not your light they're responding
[dialogue] to.  It's this--

[action] Roth takes the depth meter and waves it over the ice. 
[action] Suddenly, the black flecks begin to shine. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] X-rays.  They feed on them and emit
[dialogue] visible light.  They've found a way
[dialogue] to survive here. 

[action] Roth looks at the shimmering light of the tiny life-forms
[action] trapped in the ice, mesmerized. 

[action] Case hammers down with his arm, gouging a deep hole in the
[action] ice below.  Suddenly, GAS sprays back up at him.  He's found
[action] the gas pocket beneath them. 

[action] Too late.  Roth's rad meter begins BEEPING frantically. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ICE PLANET -- ALIEN DAWN

[action] Overhead, a tiny ball is orbiting into view from the dark
[action] side of the black hole -- the NEUTRON STAR. 

[action] Its humble size belies its power.  As its rays hit the ice,
[action] the ice CRACKLES with energy. 

[scene_heading] INT. ICE MINE SHAFT -- ALIEN DAWN

[action] Brand steps back from the ice in awe as it begins to glow. 
[action] The microbes are absorbing the x-rays and emitting light. 
[action] Within seconds, they are bathed in an incredible glow. 

[action] Even Cooper stops, awed by the beauty of the display.  Only
[action] Case is unmoved, continuing to SMASH at the ice with the
[action] broken end of his wrist. 

[page_number] 78.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (looks at rad meter)
[dialogue] They're absorbing most of the x-rays. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Not enough, unfortunately. 

[action] Suddenly, a CRACK as Case punches into the ice.  Cooper looks
[action] down -- a great fissure has opened in the ice beneath them. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Case, wait--

[action] Case PUNCHES again.  Suddenly, with a great BANG, the ice
[action] floor disintegrates beneath them. 

[action] They fall into the darkness. 

[scene_heading] INT. CAVERN -- ALIEN DAWN

[action] Cooper falls.  For a moment, the only light he can see is
[action] from Case, falling calmly a few yards beneath him. 

[action] As they fall, the light from the microbes trapped in the ice
[action] above sweeps across the inside of the cavern, illuminating
[action] the floor of the massive cavern thousands of feet down. 

[action] The rock formations they saw on the surface are actually the
[action] tip of a mountain range extending into the distance,
[action] disappearing into the ice above as if it were cloud cover.

[action] The base of the mountains is covered in a thick jungle-like
[action] foliage that runs into a perfectly-still inland sea.  On the
[action] far side of the sea, another mountain range stretches into
[action] the distance, supporting the thick ice and rock cavern roof. 

[action] Cooper only has a few seconds to take this in -- he is falling
[action] towards the huge inland sea below. 

[action] Seconds before impact, a tiny warning light flickers on inside
[action] his helmet: IMPACT DETECTED. 

[action] Suddenly, a small drag chute EXPLODES from a panel on the
[action] back of suit, slowing his fall.  Airbags EXPLODE on his legs
[action] and torso to cushion the impact.  

[action] Cooper SMASHES into the water. 

[action] BLACKNESS.

[scene_heading] EXT. UNDERWATER -- DAY

[action] Cooper comes to underwater, as liquid is flowing into his
[action] suit from his shattered helmet.  

[page_number] 79.

[action] Bubbles pour out from his suit and Cooper GASPS as his helmet
[action] fills with water and he begins to sink. 

[action] As he sinks, Cooper pulls off his helmet, then tears off
[action] pieces of his suit.  He forces himself through the neck of
[action] the suit and then swims upward. 

[action] He is about to surface when he remembers where he is.  He
[action] hesitates, just beneath the surface, lungs tightening as he
[action] runs out of oxygen. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SEA, ICE PLANET -- DAY

[action] Cooper breaks the surface, COUGHS out water, and takes a
[action] panicked breath. 

[action] He waits.  Nothing happens.  He opens his mouth again and
[action] breathes in almost pure oxygen.  He can breathe. 

[action] Treading water, he looks around.  Spots lights in the water
[action] below nearby -- Brand, in her suit, is sinking. 

[action] He dives down and struggles to haul her up by the suit.  

[action] Cooper breaks the surface, looks at Brand.  Her mask is also
[action] broken. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Breathe.  Trust me. 

[action] She refuses at first.  Water is bubbling into her suit through
[action] the open mask.  Finally, she takes a deep breath. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Your suit is filling with water. 
[dialogue] I'm not going to be able to hold it
[dialogue] much longer.  I'm going to let go.

[action] Brand's eyes widen. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What do you mean, you're going to
[dialogue] let go?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Take a deep breath. 

[action] Brand takes a deep breath and Cooper lets go of her. 

[scene_heading] EXT. UNDERWATER -- DAY

[action] Brand sinks.  Cooper swims down, following her, ripping away
[action] at her suit.  The tear-away seals on her suit are stuck fast,
[action] damaged in the fall. 

[page_number] 80.

[action] Cooper finally breaks them off.  Brand pulls herself out,
[action] free, and they swim for the surface. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SHORE, ICE PLANET -- DAY

[action] Cooper drags himself out of the water and onto the beach. 
[action] He turns back to offer Brand a hand.  She waves him off.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't need your help. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I thought we were supposed to be a
[dialogue] collective organism.

[action] She gives up and lets him help her out of the water. 

[action] They sit together, soaking wet, trying to catch their breath. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Thank you. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don't mention it. 

[action] Cooper feels the rocks beneath him, which are translucent,
[action] like glass, and polished to an impossible shine. 

[action] He looks up to see Case dragging Roth and Doyle, still in
[action] their suits, out of the water.

[scene_heading] EXT. SHORE, ICE PLANET -- DAY

[action] Doyle and Roth have stripped down from their suits and are
[action] breathing in the pure oxygen atmosphere. 

[action] Case and Cooper are pulling modular pieces from their
[action] equipment packs to form a small shelter. 

[action] Brand is taking readings with her instruments. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Oxygen atmosphere.  Pure water. 
[dialogue] Temperate climate.
[parenthetical] (to Cooper)
[dialogue] You still think this is a coincidence?

[action] Cooper looks at the tree line, less convinced. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Looks like we can climb up the
[dialogue] mountains back to the ice.  We're
[dialogue] down to three suits.  We'll have to
[dialogue] send someone ahead to get a fourth.

[page_number] 81.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I won't need one. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Why not?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Because I'm not going anywhere. 
[dialogue] This is our new home.  

[action] Brand picks up some of her instruments and a sample case and
[action] hikes into the jungle. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SHORE, ICE PLANET -- DAY

[action] Cooper finishes constructing the modest shelter.  They load
[action] the suits and the rest of their gear inside. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Any sign of Brand? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] She's still taking samples.  If you
[dialogue] find her, tell her to head back. 
[dialogue] Night should be falling soon. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What happens then? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Your guess is as good as mine.  

[action] Cooper hikes into the jungle to look for Brand. 

[scene_heading] EXT. JUNGLE, ICE PLANET -- DAY

[action] Cooper finds Brand taking samples from the tree-like life-
[action] forms, which are wildly different from their counterparts
[action] back on Earth, piled in torturous coils, as if frozen in a
[action] struggle to punch through the canopy to reach the light above.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This is incredible.  The organisms
[dialogue] trapped in the ice above absorb x-
[dialogue] rays and emit light.  In return,
[dialogue] these plants absorb the light and
[dialogue] emit oxygen, which feeds the animals
[dialogue] trapped in the ice.

[action] Brand cuts off a sample and drops it into the case.  The
[action] transparent wall of the case magnifies the structure onto
[action] the glass automatically.  She compares it with the flecks
[action] she collected in the ice above, fascinated. 

[page_number] 82.

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Look at this.  I think they're the
[dialogue] same organism at a different stage
[dialogue] of the life cycle.
[parenthetical] (shows him the sample)
[dialogue] It's  fractal .  No individual cells --
[dialogue] the structure repeats all the way
[dialogue] down.

[action] Cooper looks at the sample, then continues scanning the jungle
[action] around them, nervous.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Any sign of big life-forms? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The soil's been disturbed.  So
[dialogue] something's been moving.  But I
[dialogue] haven't seen anything.  
[parenthetical] (notices his look)
[dialogue] Cooper... are you nervous?

[action] Cooper stops scanning the trees, embarrassed.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I just think we should get back to
[dialogue] the shelter before night falls. 

[action] Overhead, the light from the ice above begins to fade -- the
[action] neutron star must have orbited out of view.  Night is falling.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (laughing)
[dialogue] Relax.  If there are any large
[dialogue] organisms here, even predatory ones,
[dialogue] they're not going to attack us --
[dialogue] they have no idea what we are. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You don't know that.  You just have
[dialogue] blind faith this place is going to
[dialogue] be some kind of Eden.

[action] Brand stops.  He's struck a nerve.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (truly pissed  off)
[dialogue] Faith has nothing to do with it. 
[dialogue] You know what you are, Cooper?  You're
[dialogue] just another in a long line of people
[dialogue] saying 'no.'  That this isn't going
[dialogue] to work.  And frankly, I don't have
[dialogue] to convince  you . 

[page_number] 83.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What's that supposed to mean?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You're on this mission because you're
[dialogue] another pair of hands.  You want the
[dialogue] truth?  I told Case we should bring
[dialogue] another robot over you.  At least
[dialogue] then I could just reprogram you to-

[action] She stops.  Something has caught her attention. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Nothing.  I just -- I could have
[dialogue] sworn it just moved. 

[action] She walks to the thick, knotted trunk of a tree.  Puts a
[action] hand on it.  

[action] Overhead, the ice flickers out and the darkness begins to
[action] descend, more rapidly than on Earth. 

[action] Suddenly, the tree SHUDDERS.  Then, the bark begins to ooze
[action] over Brand's fingers.  

[action] Brand snaps her hand away and steps back.  Even for her,
[action] this is too much.  She turns back to Cooper, who is looking
[action] at the jungle around them, spooked. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You know how plants don't usually
[dialogue] move?  Is that a universal rule?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No.  There really aren't any rules. 

[action] All around them, the life-forms begins to melt toward the
[action] ground, breaking down into different, smaller forms. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (trying to stay calm)
[dialogue] Remember, we're the aliens. 

[action] Cooper steps back as several pieces of the nearest tree drop
[action] onto the ground and begin scuttling toward him. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Hold very still.  Don't make any
[dialogue] sudden movements. 

[action] Two of the pieces join together, stacking themselves up and
[action] staggering toward him.  

[page_number] 84.

[action] A third piece climbs onto the first two, looking for a good
[action] place to hang on.  After a moment it hunkers down on top,
[action] forming a crude head.  Thirty eye-like structures blink open
[action] on the creature.  

[action] The skin of the life-form begins to flicker with a dim light
[action] like the animals trapped in the ice above, bathing Cooper
[action] and Brand in a warm glow.  

[action] The creature creeps closer to them, taking them in.  Cooper
[action] tries his best to seem non-threatening. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Hello there. 

[action] The creature lurches toward him on three legs, then its head
[action] splits open and it BELLOWS.  

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] OK.  Now we run.

[action] Brand doesn't argue.  They turn and sprint through the trees. 

[scene_heading] EXT. JUNGLE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper and Brand smash headlong through the jungle.  At first,
[action] they're running from the rumbling, smashing melee behind
[action] them.  But as they run, the entire jungle around them begins
[action] to break apart and move. 

[action] After a moment, they're surrounded.  The jungle is completely
[action] disassembling itself into a million different pieces, each
[action] one a different size and shape than the others. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We have to reach the shelter.

[action] They head off, crashing through the melee.

[scene_heading] EXT. SHORE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Case, Doyle and Roth are standing in front of the shelter
[action] finishing the day's work.  

[action] Doyle turns around.  The jungle is breaking apart and crawling
[action] down the beach towards them. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I think we've got a problem.

[action] Case and Roth look up to see the organisms picking up speed
[action] as they move towards them. 

[action] Roth begins to step out towards them. 

[page_number] 85.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Fascinating.  I think-

[action] Case grabs him by the arm and pushes him bodily into the
[action] shelter after Doyle. 

[action] Case pushes the door closed.  

[scene_heading] INT. SHELTER, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] They crouch inside the shelter as the organisms begin POUNDING
[action] at the walls, trying to force their way inside. 

[action] It's going to be a long night. 

[scene_heading] EXT. JUNGLE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Ahead, Cooper can see the gleaming water of the inland sea,
[action] light still flickering in patches from the ice above it. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This way. 

[action] Brand begins to follow.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No... wait.

[action] Too late.  Cooper forces his way between two writhing
[action] creatures, steps through, and falls...

[scene_heading] EXT. WATERFALL -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper gasps as he surfaces from the ice-cold water.  Brand
[action] floats past. 

[action] The river appears to flow randomly down the slope, with good
[action] reason -- the forest rearranges itself every night. 

[action] Ahead, the ground drops away again and the water rushes over
[action] it in a torrent. 

[action] Cooper catches himself at the last minute, grabbing a thick
[action] tree branch with one hand and, a second later, Brand with
[action] the other as she slides past. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I've got you. 

[action] Suddenly, the branch in Cooper's hand illuminates and wrenches
[action] itself out of the rock -- it's a limb of a huge colony
[action] organism.  It shakes Cooper loose and they fall again...

[page_number] 86.

[scene_heading] EXT. POOL -- NIGHT

[action] Brand surfaces first.  She grabs Cooper and hauls him up and
[action] out of the water.  She smiles down at him, a little smug. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I've got  you .

[action] Creatures begin splashing down into the pool behind them, as
[action] if imitating them. 

[action] Cooper stumbles to his feet and Brand guides them backward
[action] into a small cave carved into the rock above the pool. 

[scene_heading] INT. CAVE -- NIGHT

[action] Brand CRACKS a glow stick, basking the cave around them in a
[action] soft light. 

[action] As they watch, horrified, the pool and the rocks in front of
[action] the cave fill up with creatures of all sizes, as they tumble
[action] down the hill in what appears to be a battle royal. 

[action] For a moment, it looks like Brand and Cooper may go unnoticed. 
[action] Then, a three-foot-tall creature with a dozen legs creeps
[action] its way toward the entrance to the cave.  Another follows,
[action] and another, until the cave entrance is full. 

[action] Cooper looks around the cave, frantic.  He picks up a rock. 
[action] Brand, scared but still thinking, takes the rock from his
[action] hand and drops it back to the ground. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It's game theory.  The best move is
[dialogue] always to cooperate at first. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] By "cooperate" do you mean let it
[dialogue] eat me? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] These organisms have no interest in
[dialogue] us.  They survive by photosynthesis. 
[dialogue] All they need is light.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Then why are they killing each other?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  
[parenthetical] (looks around)
[dialogue] But we're outnumbered, Cooper.  So
[dialogue] unless you have a better idea, I
[dialogue] suggest we make nice. 

[page_number] 87.

[action] The creatures move toward them hesitantly, then faster.  

[action] Cooper stands stock still as the creatures gather around
[action] him.  Several of them join up into bigger animals to get a
[action] better look at him. 

[action] One of them wraps itself around his chest and begins prodding
[action] at his shoulders, then his neck. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (trying to be calm)
[dialogue] What is it doing? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It's a colony organism.  It's trying
[dialogue] to incorporate you into its structure. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I don't want it to incorporate me. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Don't open your mouth. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why n-

[action] As he speaks, the creature on his chest extends four spindly,
[action] pointed feelers and reaches gently into his mouth. 

[action] Cooper holds his breath as the organism taps against his
[action] teeth.  Its skin has a roughened texture, as if incomplete,
[action] and it appears to be searching for the same texture on
[action] Cooper's skin, without much luck. 

[action] Bored, the life-forms climb back down off of Cooper.  The
[action] last two creatures link up in an imitation of Cooper's lower
[action] body and attempt to walk across the room like a human.  After
[action] a moment, the creatures tumble to the ground and flail away.

[action] Cooper is still breathing hard as the cave empties and he
[action] and Brand are left alone again. 

[action] Brand steps to the edge of the cave and watches, fascinated,
[action] as the creatures resume wrestling and battling each other.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] They're killing each other.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No.  They don't bleed... they don't
[dialogue] die... they're just competing... 
[dialogue] trying out different shapes, looking
[dialogue] for the best one. 

[page_number] 88.

[action] Cooper watches as a hideously-awkward-looking, five-legged
[action] beast stumbles past and tackles another animal. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I'd say they have a long way to go. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (amazed)
[dialogue] They do this every night. 

[action] As they watch, a two-foot-long creature with one huge claw
[action] scuttles along the ground, grabbing smaller opponents and
[action] smashing them apart, then sorting through the wreckage and
[action] adopting some of the writhing parts as its own. 

[action] Something about the movement is endearing, the way it
[action] experiments with each piece -- less like a massacre and more
[action] like an over-caffeinated self-assembling erector set. 

[action] Suddenly, a massive, lumbering creature SMASHES down in front
[action] of the claw beast.  No match, the smaller creature turns to
[action] scuttle away.  Too slow.  The larger organism brings one
[action] club-like limb smashing down on top of it.  After a moment,
[action] it lifts its claw -- the two animals have become one.  

[action] The new organism lumbers away through the jungle, happily
[action] snapping its new claw at larger opponents. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The behavior changes as the animals
[dialogue] get bigger and bigger.  More
[dialogue] sophisticated.  More calculated.

[action] Two of the larger organisms square off, circling each other,
[action] lights pulsing up and down their bodies in a fierce display. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But what are they competing for?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know. 

[action] Brand shivers -- it's getting colder. 

[scene_heading] INT. CAVE -- NIGHT

[action] Brand and Cooper huddle around a small chemical fire they've
[action] brought with them. 

[action] Brand looks at the sample of the fractal wildlife in her 
[action] case.  It's moving around, splitting apart, reforming, trying
[action] to find a way out. 

[page_number] 89.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] These creatures are billions of years
[dialogue] older than we are.  But they're
[dialogue] relatively primitive.  They haven't
[dialogue] developed tools, culture, language.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why not?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  No one knows how
[dialogue] intelligent life began on Earth. 
[dialogue] But the surface of this planet has
[dialogue] virtually no craters.  No impacts. 
[dialogue] It's been sheltered by the local
[dialogue] black holes. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What difference would that make? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Maybe not enough has gone wrong here. 
[dialogue] Maybe bad luck is the key to
[dialogue] intelligent life. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (quietly)
[dialogue] Murph's Law.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Exactly.  Maybe our interaction with
[dialogue] them will push them over the top. 
[dialogue] Maybe that's part of the plan.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (shakes his head)
[dialogue] The  plan .

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Why is it so hard for you to accept
[dialogue] that someone might be trying to help
[dialogue] us? 

[action] Cooper is silent for a moment.  His mood darkens. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I was in Denver during the first
[dialogue] year of the famine.  I was just a
[dialogue] kid.  We kept waiting for someone to
[dialogue] come help us.  People starved to
[dialogue] death sitting on the ground, waiting
[dialogue] for someone to come. 
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 90.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (looks at her)
[dialogue] I only made it because I realized
[dialogue] that nobody was coming to save us. 
[dialogue] We were on our own.  

[action] Cooper looks away.  The memories are never that far away. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Honestly?  After the things I saw...
[dialogue] you want to know why I don't believe
[dialogue] that someone would be trying to help
[dialogue] us?  Because I'm not sure we're worth
[dialogue] saving. 

[action] They sit in silence for a while. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CAVE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper sleeps.  After a moment, Brand steps back out into
[action] the darkness. 

[scene_heading] EXT. PLAIN -- DAWN

[action] Brand exhales great streams of frosted breath -- the
[action] temperature is well below freezing.  The first flickers of
[action] light are visible in the ice above. 

[action] The creatures are huge now, forty or fifty feet tall, battling
[action] each other more and more slowly.  As Brand watches, the
[action] largest of them beats down his rivals, climbing to the top
[action] of the heap. 

[action] Just as the beast reaches the top, it freezes, as if stuck. 

[action] Above, the ice begins to shine brighter and brighter. 

[action] Suddenly, the beast on top breaks apart, unfurling into planes
[action] to catch the light.  This is what the contest has been about --
[action] a better place in the sun. 

[action] Brand takes it in.  

[scene_heading] EXT.  JUNGLE -- DAY

[action] Cooper and Brand pick their way their way through the foliage,
[action] which is now perfectly still, absorbing the light.  Cooper
[action] looks at the plants, wary. 

[action] Brand sees his trepidation and laughs at him. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] They're not going to move now, Cooper. 
[dialogue] They need to spread themselves as
[dialogue] thin as they can to absorb the light.

[page_number] 91.

[action] Cooper leads the way, pushing through the trees. 

[action] Suddenly he stops again.  Brand, annoyed, pushes through the
[action] foliage to join him.  

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Cooper, honestly, you're a bit of a-

[action] She stops.  Cooper is standing at a sheer cliff. 

[action] Spread out on the plain below is a massive, fortified base. 

[scene_heading] INT. CHINESE FORTIFIED COLONY -- DAY
