[action] Massive blast doors, long since smashed in, open onto a
[action] building that has been overrun by the local fractal wildlife. 

[action] Case steps inside first, his worklights flickering on.  Cooper
[action] and the others follow him in.  

[scene_heading] INT.  MESS HALL, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] The room is barely recognizable -- the fractal life has
[action] covered the tables and chairs.  Water pools on the floor and
[action] light streams in from a massive hole the fractal animals
[action] have punched in the ceiling.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Looks like the Chinese picked a fight
[dialogue] with the locals. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Looks like they lost. 

[action] They continue through the ruins. 

[scene_heading] INT.  BARRACKS, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] The deeper they go, the less fractal wildlife they find. 
[action] The barracks are pristine -- hundreds of perfectly made beds,
[action] waiting for colonists who never came, like one of the bunkers
[action] the federal government used to keep in case of nuclear winter. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Look at the size of this place. 
[dialogue] They built it for thousands of people. 
[dialogue] But no one came. 

[action] Brand looks around, determined. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] They will.  We can salvage it.  This
[dialogue] place will save us years.  

[page_number] 92.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But why didn't they come?

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] The Chinese government collapsed,
[dialogue] same as ours.  The people who knew
[dialogue] about this mission probably died
[dialogue] years ago.  We had the same problems. 

[action] Roth finds a sign he likes the look of -- it points to the
[action] science levels. 

[scene_heading] INT. LABORATORY, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] A massive door GRINDS open and the team steps into a massive
[action] complex of underground laboratories.

[scene_heading] INT.  DAMAGED LAB, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] Cooper and the others carefully make their way through a lab
[action] that has been completely emptied -- no desks, no chairs,
[action] nothing.  The only thing that remains is a solid ball of
[action] matter in the center of the room. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What happened here? 

[action] Roth stares at the ball, intrigued. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] They were testing something. 

[action] Cooper looks at the walls, which are bowed inwards. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Testing what? 

[action] Roth is looking at the ball, which appears to have been built
[action] from layers of different material.  The outermost layer is
[action] flattened steel. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (looking closer)
[dialogue] Is that a chair? 

[action] Roth looks.  The outermost layer of the ball indeed looks
[action] like a steel chair, flattened with incredible force onto
[action] surface of the ball. 

[action] Brand opens the door to the next lab. 

[page_number] 93.

[scene_heading] INT. LABORATORY, CHINESE COLONY -- NIGHT

[action] The lab is empty, except for a large metal sphere on a
[action] pedestal in the center of the room.  A black box is set into
[action] a cavity in the sphere, wired to a control panel. 

[action] Cooper looks at the walls of the room, which have been bowed
[action] inwards, as if some great force had been pulling from the
[action] center of the room. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Everything in here is bolted down.
[parenthetical] (looks at sphere)
[dialogue] What do you think this thing does?

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (excited)
[dialogue] Let's find out.  

[action] Roth finds a control panel.  Wipes off an inch of dust and
[action] begins tinkering with the controls. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I wouldn't turn it on until we can
[dialogue] figure out what it does, Roth. 

[action] Roth continues to look over the controls, oblivious. 

[character] BRAND (O.S.)
[dialogue] Cooper.  Come look at this. 

[action] Brand calls out from the hallway. 

[scene_heading] INT.  STAIRWELL, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] Cooper follows Brand's voice down the stairs to a sub-basement
[action] beneath the lab level. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATORY, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] Cooper steps into a huge space filled with a near perfect
[action] holographic representation of the local system. 

[action] Cooper joins Brand and Doyle in the map, looking at the
[action] incredibly detailed models of each star.  

[action] Case is standing at a terminal, hacking into the camp's
[action] records. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] I've found the Chinese mission logs. 
[dialogue] They're encrypted. 

[action] Cooper walks over.  Looks over the terminal. 

[page_number] 94.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Old military-grade encryption.  It's
[dialogue] not very robust. 
[parenthetical] (looks at Case)
[dialogue] No offense.  Hang on. 

[action] Cooper punches a few keys into the terminal.  Opens up the
[action] terminal.  Scans the motherboard.  Takes Case's rifle and
[action] SMASHES one of the chips on the board. 

[action] The terminal comes to life.  Cooper hands Case his rifle. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] That should open up most of it. 

[action] Case is silent for a beat, parsing the information. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] They got here twenty years ago.  The
[dialogue] human crew was killed by radiation
[dialogue] the first day.  But the robots
[dialogue] survived.  They built the colony and
[dialogue] radioed home.  But they didn't receive
[dialogue] a response. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No one was listening. 

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (scans document)
[dialogue] After a few years they discovered a
[dialogue] problem. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What problem?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] It doesn't say.  Their science team
[dialogue] took the ship to continue exploring
[dialogue] the system.  It says they found some
[dialogue] kind of...
[parenthetical] (translating)
[dialogue] ...The word literally means
[dialogue] 'treasure.' 

[action] Case skims through the rest of the logs, large portions of
[action] which have been redacted. 

[character] CASE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The science team returned after five
[dialogue] years with a new technology.  They
[dialogue] began the experiments upstairs, then
[dialogue] they left again and never returned. 

[page_number] 95.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Where did they go?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] I don't know.  They've deleted their
[dialogue] mission plan.  There's nothing else. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I think I know what the problem is. 

[action] Doyle is manipulating the time component of the map, slowing
[action] down the passage of time, reversing it, speeding it up. 

[character] DOYLE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Look. 

[action] Doyle speeds up the map until the ice planet is nearly a
[action] blur, speeding in its orbit around Pantagruel. 

[character] DOYLE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] There's a small black hole moving
[dialogue] into the system.  Too small for us
[dialogue] to have seen in our survey.  It's
[dialogue] not going to hit the planet, but
[dialogue] it's going to come close. 

[action] A tiny black hole soars through the system.  Although it
[action] misses the ice planet, it deflects its orbit by a tiny degree. 
[action] After a dozen more orbits, the ice planet dips down close to
[action] Pantagruel's event horizon and is torn apart. 

[action] Brand and the others watch as the sequence rewinds and repeats --
[action] the planet is pieced back together again and ejected, then
[action] pulled back in and torn apart.  Over and over. 

[character] DOYLE (CONT'D)
[dialogue] This place isn't paradise.  It's
[dialogue] doomed.  Just like us. 

[action] The crew stand, watching the sequence in stunned silence. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How long does this place have?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] A few years.  A decade at most. 

[action] Doyle turns to Brand.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] So much for the plan. 

[action] Cooper looks at Brand.  She is in disbelief, staring as a
[action] lifetime's training and optimism are torn to pieces. 

[page_number] 96.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] But I don't understand... why are we
[dialogue] here?  What are we here for? 

[action] Cooper looks down.  Brand's upset is turning to anger. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] What the hell are we here for?  I
[dialogue] trained my whole life to reach this
[dialogue] place. 

[action] She looks at Cooper, questioning. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (gentle)
[dialogue] Maybe... maybe Roth's right... maybe
[dialogue] we just don't understand it yet. 

[action] She looks down.  She knows he doesn't believe what he's
[action] saying.  He's been right all along -- they're alone.  

[action] For a tiny moment, all hope is lost. 

[action] Suddenly, with a distant RUMBLE, the building begins SHAKING. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Case, what is that?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] It's nighttime.  The native life-
[dialogue] forms are mobile again. 

[action] The RUMBLE grows more intense, as if the entire building is
[action] being gently shaken. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No.  It's closer than that.
[parenthetical] (realizing)
[dialogue] Roth. 

[action] The RUMBLE grows.  As Cooper watches, Case's rifle slides
[action] free of the desk.  But instead of falling, it simply hangs
[action] in the air. 

[action] Then he realizes the same thing is happening to them -- his
[action] boots no longer have traction with the ground.  After a second
[action] everything in the room is floating an inch off of the ground. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Quick -- hold onto something. 

[action] He reaches out to Brand but it's too late -- suddenly they're
[action] no longer floating, they're falling.

[action] They're falling up. 

[page_number] 97.

[action] Cooper, Brand and the rest of them hit the ceiling.  Cooper
[action] picks himself up and adjusts to his surroundings -- up is
[action] now down.  He helps Brand up.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What the hell is happening? 

[action] Case doesn't answer -- he's looking up at the control
[action] equipment for the holographic display, which is GROANING
[action] under a load it was never built to handle. 

[action] Case grabs Cooper and Brand and pushes them towards the
[action] stairwell as the control panel TEARS free of the floor and
[action] CRASHES towards them. 

[action] Cooper falls into the stairwell -- now they're being pulled
[action] up and sideways through the stairwell, like an Escher
[action] painting. 

[action] Brand pushes Cooper out of the way as debris from the control
[action] panel orbits past them back up towards the lab. 

[action] They try to hang on but the force is becoming irresistible,
[action] dragging them along the wall of the stairwell and back towards
[action] the lab upstairs.  

[action] Brand wedges herself against the railing as objects and debris
[action] are SMASHING against the door to the lab. 

[action] Doyle is trying to hang onto the handrail but his grip slips
[action] and he CRIES OUT as he falls towards the door to the lab,
[action] SMASHING it open.  Doyle disappears up and into the lab. 

[action] Cooper slips trying to help Doyle.  Brand reaches out for
[action] him...

[action] Too late.  Cooper is pulled through the open door. 

[scene_heading] INT.  INTACT LAB, CHINESE COLONY -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper is pulled through the door and past the control panel. 
[action] He reaches out and grabs the underside of the panel. 

[action] He dangles, the panel cutting into the meat of his hand,
[action] trying to get his bearings:

[action] Roth, Doyle, and everything that hasn't been bolted down is
[action] either pressed against the sphere in the center of the room
[action] or is orbiting around it. 

[action] Roth, who is laughing, jubilant, shouts up to him: 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] The control panel is beneath you. 
[dialogue] See if you can turn it off. 

[page_number] 98.

[action] Cooper slips as he tries to reach the controls.  Finally he
[action] finds the controls for the machine. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (yells up)
[dialogue] I found it.  Hold onto something. 

[action] Roth and Doyle crawl up the side of the mass and hold onto
[action] the support wires. 

[action] Cooper hits a key on the monitor. 

[action] Suddenly, the room inverts again, reverting to a normal
[action] gravitational field.  Cooper floats for a second, then topples
[action] back to the ground, along with everything else. 

[action] Brand stumbles in to find Cooper sprawled on the floor as
[action] Roth and Doyle hang onto the sphere for dear life.  

[scene_heading] INT. INTACT LAB, CHINESE COLONY -- NIGHT

[action] Roth, back on terra firma, is looking at the tiny black box
[action] he's pried from the center of the sphere.  Cooper is looking
[action] over his shoulder. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] They found a way to make gravity. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Not make it.  Adjust it.  Dial it
[dialogue] up, or down.  I think they were
[dialogue] experimenting with it -- firing these
[dialogue] into the black hole to try to save
[dialogue] this planet. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] All that from one tiny box. 

[action] Roth has hooked the box up to a terminal and it scrolling
[action] through the incredibly sophisticated machine code, trying to
[action] parse how it works.  Cooper looks on.

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (amazed)
[dialogue] It barely uses any power.  I'm only
[dialogue] beginning to understand what it does
[dialogue] but I think it sends a signal out
[dialogue] into the bulk.  It must tap into the
[dialogue] same technologies that were used to
[dialogue] create the wormholes in the first
[dialogue] place.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What does that mean?

[page_number] 99.

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] It means whoever built the wormhole
[dialogue] probably doesn't exist inside our
[dialogue] universe. 

[action] Cooper looks at the tiny box. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But how did the Chinese develop the
[dialogue] technology?  This would take decades,
[dialogue] maybe hundreds of years, to create. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Wherever they went, they found
[dialogue] something that allowed them to build
[dialogue] it. 

[action] Brand looks at the box. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] They came back here and tried to use
[dialogue] these devices to save this planet. 
[dialogue] But they didn't work.  Nothing would
[dialogue] be strong enough to weaken a black
[dialogue] hole. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Maybe we could work with it.  Try to
[dialogue] improve it. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] No.  You're missing the bigger
[dialogue] picture.  The robots had strict
[dialogue] mission parameters.  They were told
[dialogue] to build a colony and report back. 
[dialogue] They tried to.  But they failed. 
[dialogue] Like us they were fixated on this
[dialogue] place. 

[action] Cooper looks at Brand.

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] They didn't realize they had already
[dialogue] found something that would save us
[dialogue] all. 
[parenthetical] (holds up the box)
[dialogue] This.   This  is the prize.

[action] He holds up the box.

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Don't you see?  Earth's gravity is
[dialogue] like a prison.  But this is like a
[dialogue] master key.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 100.

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] If we could build more of these, we
[dialogue] could turn down the earth's
[dialogue] gravitational field enough to save
[dialogue] millions of people.  We wouldn't
[dialogue] have to pick a handful of people to
[dialogue] survive.  With this we can save
[dialogue] everyone . 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] And go where? 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Wherever we want.  We don't belong
[dialogue] in any one place.  Can't you see
[dialogue] that now?  Not Earth.  Not this place. 
[dialogue] Nowhere.  If the human race is going
[dialogue] to survive, we need to keep moving. 
[dialogue] Split up.  Spread out.   Fly.   With
[dialogue] this, we can.  

[action] Case looks at the box.  Makes a decision. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] In the morning we're taking this and
[dialogue] going home.

[action] Doyle, Roth and Case set about making preparations to leave --
[action] gathering space suits, equipment. 

[action] In the bustle, Cooper finds Brand sitting alone, staring at
[action] the sample of fractal life. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I know this isn't exactly what you
[dialogue] trained for.  But I think Roth might
[dialogue] be right.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] That doesn't help this planet.  This
[dialogue] creature is doomed.  Unlike us it
[dialogue] doesn't have anywhere to go.  

[action] The fractal organism almost seems to be looking at her, trying
[action] to comprehend.  But it quickly loses interest and goes back
[action] to breaking itself into pieces and reforming into different
[action] shapes, trying to escape.  Brand sets it back down. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT. BARRACKS, CHINESE COLONY -- DAY

[action] Morning.  Cooper and the others prepare to head out.  They
[action] are laden down with gear -- space suits for the return trip
[action] to the lander once they reach the surface.  

[page_number] 101.

[action] Roth carries the gravitational prototype. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENTRANCE, CHINESE FORTIFIED COLONY -- DAY

[action] They make their way through the ruined entrance. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We need to hurry.  We only have a
[dialogue] few hours to reach the ice before
[dialogue] nightfall. 

[action] Cooper stops.  He puts a hand on Brand's shoulder.  He points
[action] to the tree line.  Something is moving.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don't make any sudden movements. 
[dialogue] They can't hurt us if we're unarmed.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Who?

[action] Suddenly, three ROBOT MARINES step out of the jungle, weapons
[action] raised.  They are wearing ghillie suits, pieced together
[action] from dried dead pieces of the local foliage.

[action] Under their camouflage, they looks identical to Case.  With
[action] one key difference -- faded red and gold insignias. 

[action] Case steps protectively in front of his team. 

[action] The CHINESE OFFICER steps forward, rifle levelled. 

[character] CHINESE OFFICER
[dialogue] Ni Hao . My name is Technical Sergeant
[dialogue] Liu, 177th reconnaissance Marines,
[dialogue] Army of the People's Republic. 
[dialogue] Welcome to New China. 
[parenthetical] (polite)
[dialogue] Please put down your weapon. 

[action] Sergeant Liu speaks in the same even tones as Case -- they
[action] were probably built in the same factory before the war.

[action] Case keeps his rifle raised. 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] This is a scientific expedition, not
[dialogue] a military one.  We are making our
[dialogue] way back to our ship. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] I'm afraid I cannot let you do that. 
[dialogue] We will provide food and shelter and
[dialogue] await further instructions.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 102.

[character] LIU (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We are sorry if this causes you any
[dialogue] inconvenience. 

[action] The sergeant is polite, but firm.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This planet is being pulled into the
[dialogue] black hole.  We all need to leave. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] We will await further orders. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Further orders aren't coming.  Your
[dialogue] government is gone.  It ran out of
[dialogue] money, same as ours.  You're on your
[dialogue] own. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] We can offer you food and shelter as
[dialogue] long as you require.  The facilities
[dialogue] here are quite comfortable.

[dialogue] Liu's tone remains polite, but there's no hope of changing
[action] his mind.  Cooper takes a sidelong glance at Brand.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (low)
[dialogue] They're never going to let us go. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (to Liu)
[dialogue] Your mission is a humanitarian one,
[dialogue] wasn't it?  You were sent to start a
[dialogue] colony.  Like us. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] Our mission was to prepare for the
[dialogue] evacuation.  This site was deemed
[dialogue] unacceptable.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This device that you have built --
[dialogue] this could save millions of people. 
[dialogue] We need to get it back home. 

[action] Brand points to the device Roth is holding.  The Chinese
[action] robots seem particularly unhappy with this development. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] These technologies are the property
[dialogue] of the People's Republic.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 103.

[character] LIU (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We have been ordered to prevent anyone
[dialogue] from taking them.  We have been
[dialogue] ordered to prevent anyone from
[dialogue] following. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Following?  Following where?

[action] Liu pauses. 

[character] LIU
[dialogue] We will await further orders. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Your name means  six . Where did the
[dialogue] others go?  One through five?  Is
[dialogue] that who were not supposed to follow.  

[action] Case turns to look at Cooper. 

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (low)
[dialogue] Take the others up to the lander. 
[dialogue] Keep going.  No matter what happens,
[dialogue] don't come back for me.

[action] Cooper nods. 

[action] Case moves.  Fast.  He FIRES one shot at the nearest robot,
[action] disabling it, then hurls himself at the remaining two,
[action] tackling them both over the edge of the ravine. 

[action] Cooper watches them disappear into the void below.  Grabs
[action] Brand and the others. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on. 

[action] Cooper picks up the rifle from the destroyed marine and begins
[action] hiking up the mountain.  

[scene_heading] EXT.  MOUNTAIN PEAK -- DAY

[action] The crew hike towards the ice above. 

[action] Below they hear a metallic BOOM as something heavy hits
[action] something else -- Case is still fighting.  They keep moving. 

[scene_heading] EXT. UPPER MOUNTAIN PEAK -- TWILIGHT

[action] They are high above the Marine camp, only fifty feet or so
[action] below the massive ice roof of the cavern.  The Chinese have
[action] blasted a tunnel into the ice leading back up to the camp
[action] above. 

[page_number] 104.

[action] This high, the air is bitterly cold.  Cooper and the others
[action] are hunkered down, trying to keep warm, waiting for night to
[action] fall so that they can climb back up to the lander without
[action] being cooked by the neutron star. 

[action] Finally, above them, the light begins to flicker out.  The
[action] crew begin pulling on their space suits.  

[action] Brand checks on the fractal wildlife in the sample case --
[action] she is bringing it with them.  Cooper catches her eye. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We can't leave them all to die. 

[action] She hefts the case and they begin climbing up into the ice. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Brand leads the way, the lights from her suit cutting into
[action] the drifts of snow as they make their way slowly back to the
[action] lander. 

[action] As they reach the lander Cooper spots lights emerging from
[action] the ship.  He raises Case's weapon. 

[action] One of Liu's marines steps around from the back of the lander. 
[action] Cooper FIRES.

[action] The marine collapses. 

[action] Cooper and the others race to the lander.  

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Was that the last of them? 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We're not going to stick around to
[dialogue] count them.  Let's get out of here.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're too late. 

[action] Cooper points to a damaged section of the ship's hull -- the
[action] marine has torn open a section, revealing damaged hardware.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] The main thruster fuel supply.  We're
[dialogue] not going anywhere. 

[action] Cooper looks down.  They're stuck here.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What about the escape rocket at the
[dialogue] Chinese base camp?  One of us could
[dialogue] fit into it. 

[page_number] 105.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It doesn't matter how many of us
[dialogue] make it.  We have to get this back
[dialogue] to earth. 

[action] Roth looks at the moons orbiting overhead. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Can the secondary thrusters still
[dialogue] fire on the lander? 

[action] Doyle looks over the craft. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Sure.  But we don't have nearly enough
[dialogue] power for lift off. 

[action] Roth holds up the small gravity black box. 

[character] ROTH
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Yes we do. 

[action] Roth sets off running towards the Chinese base camp. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CHINESE BASE CAMP, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper drags the pieces of the escape rocket out from the
[action] shelter.  Assembly is semiautomatic -- Cooper lifts the pieces
[action] up and they snap together, forming a crude two-stage rocket
[action] designed more for a robot than a man.

[action] When it's complete Roth wedges himself inside, clutching the
[action] black box -- he barely fits. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] I can reach one of the moons in low
[dialogue] orbit and turn up its gravity.  As
[dialogue] it passes overhead it should be able
[dialogue] to slingshot the lander away from
[dialogue] the planet's surface. 
[parenthetical] (looks around)
[dialogue] Along with everything else.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But we need to take the box back to
[dialogue] earth. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] You don't need this -- only the idea. 
[dialogue] The knowledge of how to build it.  

[action] Cooper remembers something.  Heads back into the shelter.  

[page_number] 106.

[scene_heading] INT. CHINESE BASE CAMP, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Roth follows Cooper into the lab. 

[action] Cooper opens the storage crate.  Picks one of the probes at
[action] random.  Sets it down on the bench next to Roth. 

[action] Cooper begins rigging up a connection between the two. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I'm going to image the operating
[dialogue] code and the architecture onto the
[dialogue] probe's hard drive.  It'll be garbled,
[dialogue] but it should give you and me enough
[dialogue] to rebuild it when we get back.  

[action] Cooper looks at the probe filling up with data. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Not me.  But you'll make it work. 

[action] The transfer is complete.  Roth hands Cooper the probe, and
[action] takes the black box himself.  

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] This is a one-way trip for me. 

[action] Before Cooper can respond, Roth heads for the door. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CHINESE BASE CAMP, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Roth squeezes himself into the rocket, hugging the black box
[action] to his chest.  Cooper and Brand help him. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This isn't right, Roth.  We can find
[dialogue] a way for all of us to leave.  

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] This is the only way.  Keep the lander
[dialogue] upright and fire the secondary
[dialogue] boosters when the moon passes
[dialogue] overhead. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Let me take it. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] No.  You need to make it home.  You're
[dialogue] going to need to build more of these. 

[action] Brand looks at him, heartbroken.  Roth smiles. 

[page_number] 107.

[character] ROTH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I understand the plan, now.  Whatever
[dialogue] happens, you have to keep exploring. 
[dialogue] Keep learning.  One good idea isn't
[dialogue] enough.  You'll need more and more
[dialogue] of them just to survive.  Do you
[dialogue] understand? 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (nods)
[dialogue] Thank you. 

[character] ROTH
[dialogue] Go. 

[action] Brand turns to follow Cooper back. 

[action] Behind her, Roth's rocket LAUNCHES on an explosive cloud. 
[action] In seconds it's high overhead.  

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper checks over the hull, which is intact.  Doyle is inside
[action] looking over the controls.  He steps back outside. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Control systems inside are online.
[parenthetical] (looks up)
[dialogue] I've lost sight of the rocket.  Do
[dialogue] you think he made it?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're going to find out soon enough. 

[action] Brand is looking off into the distance. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Someone's coming.

[action] Cooper looks up.  A robotlike figure is limping through the
[action] snowstorm towards them.  Cooper raises the rifle. 

[action] Finally he makes out the insignia -- it's Case. 

[action] Cooper and Doyle run out towards him.  They reach Case as he
[action] collapses in the snow.  He's badly injured -- one leg torn
[action] off at the knee, one arm mangled.  

[action] Doyle and Cooper drag the crippled robot back into the ship. 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER -- NIGHT

[action] They drag Case into the lander.  Doyle checks over the ship's
[action] controls. 

[page_number] 108.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Close the door. 

[action] Cooper moves to the door.  Something's wrong. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where's Brand?

[action] She's nowhere to be seen.  Cooper looks out onto the ice. 
[action] Brand is heading back out into the snow.  

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Dammit.  If I don't make it back,
[dialogue] just keep going. 
[parenthetical] (points to probe)
[dialogue] Get that thing home. 

[action] Cooper steps to the door. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE ABOVE ICE PLANET

[action] The rocket tucks in close behind one of the moons orbiting
[action] the ice planet. 

[action] The rocket fires to maneuver closer and closer to the moon's
[action] surface -- the tiny moon doesn't provide enough gravity to
[action] attract it.  

[action] When the rocket is close enough, Roth detonates the explosive
[action] bolts holding him inside and leaps for the moon's surface. 

[action] He scrambles to grab hold of the craggy surface.  Behind
[action] him, the rocket smashes apart against the surface. 

[action] Roth finally gets a good hold.  He looks down. 

[action] Below him, the view is incredible -- the ice planet curving
[action] into the distance, Gargantua rising over its horizon. 

[action] Roth looks at the black box in his hands. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Brand is a hundred yards from the lander by the time Cooper
[action] catches up with her.  She is digging in the snow. 

[action] He takes her arm but she won't go. 

[action] She finally finds what she's looking for -- the sample case. 
[action] The tiny fractal life-form is huddled at the bottom. 

[action] Cooper shakes his head and turns back to the lander. 

[page_number] 109.

[scene_heading] EXT. MOON, SPACE OVER ICE PLANET

[action] Roth is watching the ice planet pass by beneath him.  He
[action] finally sees the distant peaks of the mountain range as it
[action] punctures the ice near the Chinese camp. 

[action] For a moment he enjoys the view -- the distant hulk of
[action] Gargantua rising over the horizon of the shining ice planet. 

[action] He takes a final breath and activates the black box. 

[action] Instantly, the moon's gravity is magnified a hundred million
[action] times over.  Roth is instantly crushed as the moon collapses
[action] around him into a tiny sphere. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper and Brand are only thirty yards or so from the lander. 

[action] Suddenly, the ground beneath them begins to RUMBLE. 

[action] Cooper turns back.  A mile back the ground begins to tear
[action] apart, heaving massive chunks of ice and rock into the sky. 

[action] Roth's moon is racing overhead, TEARING up a massive strip
[action] of the surface's planet as it nears them. 

[action] Cooper and Brand run. 

[scene_heading] EXT. MOON, SPACE OVER ICE PLANET

[action] The energy being unleashed by the tiny box is incredible --
[action] millions of times more powerful than an atomic bomb. 

[action] The spray of debris is forming a rooster tail behind the
[action] moon, rocketing up from the planet's surface. 

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER, SURFACE, ICE PLANET -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper has almost made it back to the lander.  Behind him,
[action] Brand stumbles.  He turns back. 

[action] Brand is pulling herself up.  Behind her, the THUNDEROUS
[action] EXPLOSIONS as a strip of the planet's surface is hurled into
[action] space have almost reached them. 

[action] Cooper looks at the ship.  He'll never make it if he waits
[action] for Brand.  He turns back to find her. 

[action] He reaches Brand just as the moon's gravity hits them --
[action] they're hurtled off into space.  He grabs her hand. 

[action] Seconds later, everything -- the entire ice sheet, the sea
[action] and rocks deep below -- is hurled up after them.  

[page_number] 110.

[action] Brand and Cooper embrace as they rocket up through the thin
[action] atmosphere.  

[action] Brand looks at him. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (radio)
[dialogue] You caught me.  Now what? 

[action] Cooper looks around.  They planet's surface is breaking into
[action] pieces around them. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (radio)
[dialogue] I don't know.
[parenthetical] (radio)
[dialogue] I figured if you were floating out
[dialogue] into space, you'd want some company.

[action] He holds onto her as they fall up and out of the last of the
[action] planet's thin atmosphere and the blackness embraces them. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Between you and utter solitude,
[dialogue] Cooper, frankly, I'm not sure. 

[action] They reach the apex of their climb and the gravity of the
[action] planet begins to win out.  For a moment they float.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Guess you were right -- too much
[dialogue] gravity, or not enough. 

[action] He smiles, forgetting their predicament for a moment.  They
[action] stare into each other's eyes.  

[action] Then they begin, very gently, to fall back towards the ice
[action] planet.  

[action] Suddenly, the lander maneuvers beneath them.  The airlock
[action] opens to catch them -- Doyle is at the helm. 

[action] Brand and Cooper pull themselves aboard. 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER

[action] Cooper closes the door and Doyle rotates the lander outwards. 

[action] Doyle FIRES the engines and the lander continues ascending
[action] into space as the debris around them begins crashing back
[action] down towards the ice planet. 

[action] Cooper looks out the window:

[page_number] 111.

[action] The moon continues tearing up a massive canyon in the planet's
[action] surface as it circles out of view.   

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Roth. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] He's gone.

[action] Ahead, a shadow looms in the darkness: the Endurance. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Brand, Cooper, and Doyle pull themselves out of the airlock
[action] and scramble to take control of the ship.  

[action] Below them, the surface of the ice planet illuminates as the
[action] neutron star's x-rays begin to reach it from the far side of
[action] Pantagruel. 

[action] Doyle finally gets the ship straightened out.  Its sole
[action] remaining engine FIRES, rocketing the ship back towards the
[action] dark side of the planet, out of view of the neutron star,
[action] and back towards the original wormhole. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I'm setting a course for the wormhole. 

[action] They're going to make it. 

[action] Cooper and Brand make eye contact -- Brand gives him a tiny
[action] awkward smile.  He returns it, then descends below.

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER HULL, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper props Case up against the workbench.  He pulls down
[action] replacement arms and legs from storage bins.  Unwraps them
[action] from their vacuum sealed plastic and bolts them back onto
[action] Case's chassis. 

[action] Cooper finishes attaching the arm.  Case rotates it, checking
[action] the function.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How is that working? 

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Very well, thank you.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Good.  We've already set the course,
[dialogue] skipper.  We're going home.

[action] Cooper hauls himself back up into the observation level. 

[page_number] 112.

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION LEVEL

[action] Doyle is setting the controls.  The ALARMS on the controls
[action] have finally abated -- they're out of the danger zone.   

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We're safe now. 

[action] Cooper heads to the communications equipment. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Now I know why we weren't able to
[dialogue] hit the relay.  It's not interference --
[dialogue] the blue-shift from the black hole
[dialogue] is more than we thought it would be.

[action] Cooper begins re-calibrating the equipment.  

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (conflicted)
[dialogue] Cooper, wait-- 

[action] The ship PINGS the relay on the far side of the wormhole. 

[action] There is no response. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Nothing. 
[parenthetical] (thinks)
[dialogue] Wait.  The ship cached one long
[dialogue] garbled transmission when we first
[dialogue] fell into the swirl.  If we account
[dialogue] for the blue shift then the computer
[dialogue] might find something in there. 

[action] Cooper brings up the last garbled transmission they received. 
[action] The computer begins re-analyzing it, piece by piece. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] Listen to me -- the blueshift also
[dialogue] means we've lost time.  More time
[dialogue] than we thought we would. 

[action] Cooper looks up at her. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How much? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] A lot... Cooper, maybe it's best if
[dialogue] we don't-

[action] The comms equipment TONES as it translates a packet.  Then
[action] another.  

[page_number] 113.

[action] Then a FLOOD of communications, one packet a day, hundreds
[action] and hundreds of packets: images, videos, audio messages from
[action] family and friends. 

[action] Cooper watches, horrified, as the images play out across the
[action] screen.  He is watching the lives of his family play out at
[action] light speed.  Finally, the packets slow, then stop. 

[action] Cooper looks at the results, in shock. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Forty seven years.

[action] Doyle joins him, staring at the screen, stunned. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] My kids...

[action] Cooper tries to PING the relay again.  Nothing comes back. 

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (gentle)
[dialogue] The relay will have lost power years
[dialogue] ago.  That's why we couldn't contact
[dialogue] it, even after we reached the planet. 

[action] Cooper is still problem solving, thinking. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We could bypass the relay.  Send a
[dialogue] conventional shortwave signal. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Only a tiny portion of the signal
[dialogue] would make it though the wormhole. 
[dialogue] Besides, no one will be listening
[dialogue] anymore. 

[action] The comms screen is dead.  No movement.  Nothing. 

[action] Cooper looks at Brand, realizing something. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You knew, didn't you?  You and Case
[dialogue] figured it out when we landed. 

[action] Brand looks down. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I thought... I couldn't be sure. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sure you could.  You're brilliant. 
[dialogue] You know everything. 

[page_number] 114.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper... we needed to keep going. 
[dialogue] I'm so sorry.  Your children... 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] They're not children any more... if
[dialogue] they're even still alive. 

[action] He turns away from her. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Listen, the important thing is that
[dialogue] we're going home, now.  And we have
[dialogue] something that can save everyone. 
[dialogue] It's more important than the people
[dialogue] we left behind-

[action] Doyle cuts her off, filled with anger. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] That's easy for you to say.  You
[dialogue] didn't leave anyone behind. 

[action] Cooper looks at Brand, his anger softened by sadness. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Yes she did. 

[action] Brand looks at him, grateful for this small kindness. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] That's why you were upset -- your
[dialogue] father. 

[action] She looks down, filled with sadness.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] He's gone.  But there are other people
[dialogue] who still need our help.  There's
[dialogue] still time...

[action] The ship's controls TONE in ALARM.  Brand and Cooper turn:

[action] Case is standing at the controls. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Case... what are you doing? 

[action] Case finishes typing in a sequence on the command controls. 
[action] Presses the "execute" button. 

[action] The last nuclear engine begins to detach from the ship. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Wait... no...

[page_number] 115.

[action] The robot turns from them.  Cooper notices that the control
[action] module snapped into the back of Case's chassis is wrong:

[action] It's red with a gold star.  It's not Case at all.  It's Liu.

[character] LIU
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I have my orders.  No
[dialogue] one follows... 

[action] Liu stares, satisfied, as the nuclear engine spins away from
[action] the ship and back down toward Pantagruel. 

[action] Cooper SMASHES Liu from behind.  The robot tumbles to the
[action] ground, the fight gone out of him. 

[action] Cooper and Doyle roll the robot over and Cooper reaches for
[action] his control module. 

[character] LIU (CONT'D)
[dialogue] No one follows-

[action] Cooper rips the module out.  The robot freezes. 

[action] Brand is already at the controls, trying to regain control. 

[action] The instruments TONE, alerting the crew to their position:

[action] The ship is spinning back down towards the black hole.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No... We're being pulled back to
[dialogue] Pantagruel. 

[action] Cooper, frantically checks the controls, firing the remaining
[action] boosters. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We can't let that happen.  We'll
[dialogue] lose more time... too much...

[action] The boosters are no match for the deadly pull of the black
[action] hole below them. 

[action] On the ship's guidance, they watch, helpless, as the ship
[action] climbs back up the volcano rim towards the critical orbit. 

[action] As on their first trip, the black hole grows to dominate the
[action] bottom of the sky, and the stars above them become streaks --
[action] time is speeding for them as they are whirled down into the
[action] deep gravity well around the hole.  

[action] As they watch, trapped, decades begin to play out in the
[action] system above them.  They watch as the ice planet whirls around
[action] them, orbiting the black hole dozens of times. 

[page_number] 116.

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We're losing years. 

[action] Finally, the ice planet plunges past them towards destruction. 
[action] As it reaches it slows, until finally it reaches the event
[action] horizon, just as they saw in the projections.  Nothing has
[action] prepared them for the reality:

[action] The ice planet is SMASHED apart with stunning violence. 

[action] Brand looks at the sample of fractal life.  Now they are
[action] united -- they are, in all likelihood, the sole survivors of
[action] their planet. 

[action] Doyle points to the instruments. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] The wormhole.  Look -- it's being
[dialogue] pulled into the black hole as well. 

[action] They watch on the holographic model as the wormhole's orbit
[action] converges on the event horizon of the black hole. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It's orbit mirrored the ice planet's.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What will happen to it? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It'll be destroyed, like everything
[dialogue] else. 

[action] Cooper struggles to pull himself over to the communications
[action] screen.  Sets it to make a shortwave broadcast.  Brand opens
[action] her mouth to point out that it's futile, then stops. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (into radio)
[dialogue] This is the crew of the Endurance. 
[dialogue] We...
[parenthetical] (gives up on
[dialogue] formalities)
[dialogue] Murph, I'm sorry.  I'm sorry I didn't
[dialogue] make it back, like I promised.

[action] He hangs up the radio.  

[action] In silence, they watch as the wormhole -- their only way
[action] home -- vanishes beneath the event horizon.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] How much time are we losing? 

[page_number] 117.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (heartbroken)
[dialogue] Decades...hundreds of years.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] All of those people back home...
[dialogue] none of them will make it. 

[action] As Doyle watches the controls the ship continues to spiral
[action] towards the black hole. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] This is it.  We're going to be pulled
[dialogue] in. 

[action] Cooper looks at the controls, an idea forming.  

[action] He pulls himself down.  Hauls himself across the deck to
[action] pick up Liu's chip.  He plugs it into a diagnostic tool. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What are you doing?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Case said the Chinese found something
[dialogue] else.  The location was scrubbed
[dialogue] from their records.  But I bet he
[dialogue] knows it. 

[action] Cooper fires up the chip.  Begins sorting through the onboard
[action] memory. 

[action] Doyle watches the instruments. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Hurry up. 

[action] Cooper concentrates, poring over the numbers.  Finally he
[action] begins feeding coordinates into the navigation computer. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Here.  Can we reach it? 

[action] Brand looks at the map.  The point appears on the far side
[action] of Gargantua.  Brand studies the trajectory. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We can use the thrusters to keep us
[dialogue] on the critical orbit.  Then slingshot
[dialogue] us towards Gargantua. 

[action] The thrusters STRAIN to push the ship back up towards the
[action] critical orbit. 

[page_number] 118.

[action] Suddenly, the tidal gravity SLAMS them against the walls of
[action] the ship.

[action] Finally, the thrusters fire again -- a tiny push, but just
[action] enough to launch the ship clear of the black hole, like a
[action] rock out of a slingshot. 

[action] The ship races toward the massive hole in the sky below them:

[action] Gargantua

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE AROUND GARGANTUA

[action] The ship picks up more and more speed as it soars closer to
[action] the massive black hole's event horizon. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] The sky closes to a tiny hole above them.  Gargantua is
[action] swallowing their view. 

[action] Cooper looks at the radar, which is choked with debris.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We only have a few minutes before
[dialogue] we're swallowed into that thing. 
[dialogue] What are we even looking for?

[action] On the radar, a tiny empty spot appears. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (points to screen)
[dialogue] That?  What is it? 

[action] Brand looks.  In one tiny region on the back side of the
[action] black hole, the debris simply seems to vanish. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It's another wormhole.

[action] Doyle FIRES the thrusters again, pushing the ship towards
[action] the wormhole. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE AROUND GARGANTUA

[action] The Endurance spins, shifting paths slightly as it continues
[action] to race closer and closer to oblivion. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] They study the controls.  The projected path shifts, one
[action] degree at a time.  Finally, it appears to put them on a path
[action] to hit the wormhole. 

[action] Doyle shuts off the thrusters. 

[page_number] 119.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We're not going to be able to slow
[dialogue] down.  Hold onto something. 

[action] He locks up the controls.  

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE AROUND GARGANTUA -- MORNING

[action] The ship is tumbling through space, racing toward the second
[action] wormhole.  It is massive, much larger than the first wormhole,
[action] and it's glowing with a light as bright as a star. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] The crew brace themselves against the hull of the ship. 

[action] Suddenly the ship slams onto the wormhole mouth and is pulled
[action] violently into the wormhole. 

[action] WHITENESS. 

[action] As the light fades, Cooper and the others come to.

[action] The light is rapidly dimming from pure white, fading to a
[action] deep red, then infrared, finally darkness. 

[action] Cooper looks out.  Checks the instruments.  Looks out again. 

[scene_heading] EXT. VACUUM

[action] Nothing.  Blackness as far as the eye can see.  No stars. 
[action] No planets.  Just inky darkness stretching on forever. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Brand joins Cooper on the deck.  Together, they look out at
[action] the blackness that surrounds them. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where are we? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  It's like we entered
[dialogue] the wormhole and never left it. 

[action] Brand checks the instruments.  There is nothing for the ship
[action] to model.

[action] There is nowhere to go.  They drift. 

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Days go by.  They check the instruments.  Still nothing. 
[action] It's as if they have left the known universe altogether. 

[page_number] 120.

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Doyle sits down.  After a moment, he turns on the screen and
[action] cues up a message. 

[action] His children appear on-screen, giggling, pushing each other,
[action] trying to get a prime spot in front of the camera. 

[action] Doyle cups his face in his hands and cries. 

[scene_heading] INT. CREW QUARTERS, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper above his bunk, stares out into the blackness. 

[action] Brand watches him from the doorway. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I should have told you. 
[dialogue] I didn't have the right. 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] But you should watch the recordings. 
[dialogue] You should know what happened to
[dialogue] your kids. 

[action] Cooper ignores her. 

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Brand watches her father talking to her on-screen.  He is
[action] twenty years older than when she left him. 

[character] BRAND'S FATHER
[dialogue] I'm not going to make it much longer. 
[dialogue] The machines will continue to maintain
[dialogue] the station as long as they can and
[dialogue] the communications will run as long
[dialogue] as the station here still has power. 
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I hope wherever you are,
[dialogue] darling, you're safe. 

[action] The screen cuts out.  Brand watches the static play out.  

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Doyle sits at the table.  He has found a flair gun in one of
[action] the ditch kits.  It is sitting on the table in front of him. 
[action] The message is clear. 

[action] Brand sits down across from him. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Our last trip past the black hole
[dialogue] cost us another 100, maybe 200 years. 
[dialogue] Which means there's a good chance
[dialogue] we're the only humans alive anywhere. 

[page_number] 121.

[action] She stands up. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I think the last human beings should
[dialogue] have a little more fight in them
[dialogue] than that. 

[action] Brand picks up the sample of fractal life and places it under
[action] a lamp on the counter.  It freezes, absorbing the rays. 

[action] Doyle looks at the gun. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE, VACUUM

[action] Cooper, in a space suit, steps out of the airlock. 

[action] He drifts out from the ship. 

[action] Nothing.  Behind him, the ship is a tiny speck in an ocean
[action] of darkness. 

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper sits down.  Turns on the screen. 

[action] After a moment, the camera turns on.  Tom, his eldest son,
[action] still 15 years old, turns on the camera. 

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Hi, Dad. 

[action] Cooper pauses it.  He can't take it.  After a moment, he
[action] lets it run again.

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I met another girl, Dad.  I really
[dialogue] think this is the one.

[action] Tom holds up a picture of himself and a teenage GIRL. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Murph stole Grandpa's car.  He crashed
[dialogue] it.  He's OK, though.  No broken
[dialogue] bones.   

[action] Cooper leans back. 

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS ROOM, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper is holed up, still watching, several days' growth of
[action] beard unshaved.  He's been watching for days. 

[action] On the screen, Tom is a grown man in his 20s. 

[page_number] 122.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I've got a surprise for you, Dad. 
[dialogue] You're a grandpa. 

[action] Tom holds up an infant wrapped tight in swaddling.  The kid
[action] is BAWLING. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Congratulations.  Grandpa said he
[dialogue] already earned the "great" bit so we
[dialogue] just leave it at that.

[action] The screen cuts out. 

[action] The next message begins.  Tom is in his 30s. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Hi, Dad.  I'm sorry it's been awhile. 

[action] He stops, emotional. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Grandpa died last week.  We buried
[dialogue] him out in the back forty, next to
[dialogue] Mom.  
[parenthetical] (looks down)
[dialogue] Where we'd have buried you, if you'd
[dialogue] ever come back. 

[action] He laughs, gallows humor. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Murph was there for the funeral. 
[dialogue] It's been a few years since I've
[dialogue] seen him.  He's been down in the
[dialogue] Gulf Coast.  He's an engineer.  I
[dialogue] guess someone followed in your
[dialogue] footsteps after all.  

[action] Tom looks down. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You're not listening to this.  I
[dialogue] know that.  All of these messages
[dialogue] are just out there, drifting in the
[dialogue] darkness.  

[action] He stops for a second. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You're gone.  You're never coming
[dialogue] back.  And I've known that for a
[dialogue] long time.  Lois says -- that's my
[dialogue] wife, Dad -- she says I have to let
[dialogue] you go.  So I am.  

[page_number] 123.

[action] He reaches up to turn off the camera. 

[character] TOM (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Wherever you are, I hope you're at
[dialogue] peace.  Goodbye, Dad. 

[action] The image freezes, Tom's hand on the camera, then breaks
[action] apart into digital noise. 

[action] Then nothing. 

[action] Cooper looks at it for a second.  Then rises to leave. 

[action] Suddenly, the screen flickers to life again. 

[action] A good-looking man in his late 30s turns on a camera.  Cooper
[action] recognizes him instantly.  It's Murph. 

[action] Murph looks at the camera for a long beat, clearly unsure
[action] about this.  

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Hello, Dad.  You sonofabitch. 

[action] He laughs, self-conscious. 

[character] MURPH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] It's your 60th birthday today. 
[dialogue] Thought I would celebrate with you a
[dialogue] bit. 
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] I guess I understand why you left. 
[dialogue] The corn is dying now, too.  
[dialogue] Tom says there's less and less at
[dialogue] harvest every year.  

[action] He pauses.  Lifts up his hand and scratches his stubble. 

[action] Cooper pauses the message.  He looks carefully at the screen: 

[action] Murph is wearing his dad's watch.

[action] Cooper lets the message play.  Tears are streaming down his
[action] face. 

[character] MURPH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Rot's setting in.  I guess you were
[dialogue] right for clearing out while you had
[dialogue] the chance. 
[parenthetical] (reaches up to switch
[dialogue] off camera)
[dialogue] Good luck, old man.  I hope you made
[dialogue] it.  I really do.  

[action] The video cuts out. 

[page_number] 124.

[action] A message appears on the screen: 

[action] "Final transmission."  Relay powered down 05232087

[action] Cooper turns off the screen. 

[scene_heading] INT. CREW QUARTERS, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper is seated, alone.  Almost every compartment has been
[action] opened and emptied -- debris is swirling through the
[action] compartment. 

[action] Brand walks in.  They sit in silence for a moment.  When
[action] Cooper speaks, it's clear his sadness has faded to a gallows
[action] humor. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not a single drink on the whole ship. 
[dialogue] What kind of mission is this? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I think Doyle's been experimenting
[dialogue] with the coolant from his spacesuit. 

[action] They sit in silence for a moment. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You really think we're the last humans
[dialogue] alive anywhere? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  Maybe.
[parenthetical] (looks him in the eye)
[dialogue] Yes. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So that's it, then?  That's all?

[action] He looks down, saddened by his own words.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] It's happened a billion times over. 
[dialogue] Stars explode.  The pieces drift in
[dialogue] space.  Gravity pulls them back
[dialogue] together.  They form new stars. 
[dialogue] Then planets.  Then us.  We die.  It
[dialogue] starts all over again. 

[action] Cooper shakes his head. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What about the plan?  The grand
[dialogue] scheme. 

[page_number] 125.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I thought you didn't believe in one.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I didn't.  But you were bringing me
[dialogue] round. 

[action] He laughs, his anger coming and going in waves. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] What the hell was the point?  What
[dialogue] did it add up to? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  Maybe it just adds up
[dialogue] to this.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This?  You're saying the end result
[dialogue] of ten billion years is the atoms
[dialogue] from dead stars standing here
[dialogue] disagreeing with each other. 

[action] She smiles at him.  Bittersweet. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Maybe that's enough.  

[action] He turns away from her.  She takes his shoulder.  

[action] She pulls him into a kiss.  His surprise disappears and he
[action] pulls her to him, kissing her back. 

[action] Their surroundings forgotten, they drift.  She LAUGHS gently
[action] as they bump into a wall and he pushes off of it, sending
[action] them spinning back into the center of the cabin. 

[action] She pulls his shirt off and it hangs in space.  In moments,
[action] the cabin is filling with discarded clothes, different colors
[action] and shapes, like a ticker tape parade. 

[action] In the center of the cabin, Brand and Cooper make love.  

[scene_heading] INT. CREW QUARTERS, ENDURANCE -- LATER

[action] Cooper and Brand embrace, sleeping, drifting in the cabin. 

[action] Suddenly, Doyle's voice calls out from the other cabin. 

[character] DOYLE (O.S.)
[dialogue] It's happening... it's happening
[dialogue] again...  Get up here...

[action] Cooper wakes, careful not to disturb Brand.  He plucks his
[action] shirt from the floating laundry pile and pulls it on.  

[page_number] 126.

[scene_heading] INT. OBSERVATION DECK, ENDURANCE

[action] Cooper hauls himself up into the upper cabin.  Doyle is
[action] standing in the middle of the chamber. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] They just appeared. 

[action] A sphere of distortion, like the one they encountered in the
[action] first wormhole, is directly in front of Doyle, hovering at a
[action] point in the middle of the cabin. 

[action] Doyle points a finger gently at the sphere, which grows in
[action] response. 

[action] Brand pulls herself into the cabin.  Several more points
[action] appear, as if they've been summoned by the first. 

[action] Some of the points twist, some of them spin, and some of
[action] them are motionless. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I think these are the creatures that
[dialogue] built the wormhole. 

[action] One of them begins to move closer to Cooper.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This thing is made of... gravity? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No.  I don't think it's in our
[dialogue] universe at all.  I think it lives
[dialogue] in the bulk -- the space that the
[dialogue] wormholes traverse.  And it can only
[dialogue] interact with us using gravity. 

[action] The shape playfully grows around Cooper's hand, bending the
[action] space it's in, stretching the skin. 

[action] Doyle GASPS as the sphere nearest him moves through his body,
[action] coming to rest in the middle and bending his entire torso
[action] like a fun house mirror. 

[action] Suddenly, the sphere wrapped around Cooper's hand begins
[action] moving, tugging him gently through the cabin. 

[action] In the next moment, all three of them are being propelled
[action] through the cabin. 

[action] It's a magical moment -- a communion, a dance between
[action] creatures on either side of a massive, invisible wall. 

[action] Doyle exclaims like a kid on a roller coaster as he is whirled
[action] around the room. 

[page_number] 127.

[action] Cooper and Brand collide with each other for a second as the
[action] bulk beings move them through space.  They hold onto each
[action] other for a moment and then are pulled apart again as the
[action] whirling dance continues. 

[action] Even the fractal creature is involved -- inside its cage, a
[action] tiny distortion ripples its fractal skin.  The creature breaks
[action] apart and plays with the distortion around its enclosure. 

[action] Finally, Brand disengages from the dance and pulls herself
[action] around the lab, looking for some way to try to communicate
[action] with the creatures. 

[action] She finds two heavy pieces of engineering equipment and moves
[action] them close to each other, then far apart. 

[action] The bulk beings soon join her, mimicking the motion of the
[action] pieces of equipment. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Can we communicate with them? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Where would we even start?  Maybe--

[action] She pull a bag of ball bearings out of a storage bin and
[action] tears the bag open, releasing a cloud of the tiny shining
[action] globes into the air. 

[action] For a moment, nothing happens.  Then, the distortions begin
[action] moving through the bearings, shifting them, rearranging them
[action] into patterns in the air. 

[action] First, they rearrange the ball bearings into clusters, then
[action] shapes spinning around each other -- planets orbiting around
[action] a medium-sized star. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] That's our solar system. 

[action] After a moment the ball bearings break apart again and
[action] rearrange into a larger, more complicated system -- Gargantua,
[action] Pantagruel, and the ice planet. 

[action] Finally, the bearings compress to a two-dimensional sheet. 
[action] Inside the sheet, one solitary ball bearing orbits around
[action] the stars and then pushes up, off of the sheet, into an
[action] umbrella shaped space that rises up from the sheet. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What is that? 

[action] Brand looks at the tiny particle, floating alone above its
[action] universe. 

[page_number] 128.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] That's us.  That's where they've
[dialogue] brought us.  We're in the bulk. 

[action] Cooper and Doyle stare at the particle.  The map is static
[action] now. 

[action] Cooper looks around.  The distortions have gone.  The
[action] instruments on the ship TONE in alarm. 

[action] Cooper walks through the map, sending the ball bearings
[action] scattering.  He hurries to the instruments.  The ship is
[action] moving -- picking up speed at a huge rate. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] They're moving us.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Where? 

[action] They stand, looking out into the perfect darkness. 

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE, VACUUM

[action] The ship begins to close in on something in the darkness: a
[action] tiny point of light. 

[action] As they grow closer, they realize that it's an opening. 
[action] They are inside a massive, hollow sphere.  They have been
[action] the entire time. 

[action] The ship is guided out through the opening at the crown of
[action] the sphere. 

[action] Below them is an astonishing sight:

[action] Our entire universe, compressed into a flattened disc, like
[action] a floor of stars.  They are hovering above it, in the bulk,
[action] the space between universes.

[scene_heading] INT. ENDURANCE, BULK

[action] Brand and the others stare at the incredible display. 

[action] The ship drops down and begins to orbit around the massive
[action] hollow sphere between them and our universe, lower and lower
[action] until it is skimming just above the surface of the sphere. 

[action] In the distance, they can make out a cluster of lights. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] There's something out there. 

[action] The cluster of lights grows as they are propelled closer: 

[page_number] 129.

[action] It's a massive space station, built onto the surface of the
[action] sphere. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE STATION

[action] The Endurance glides to a gentle stop on one of the upper
[action] decks of the space station. 

[action] After a moment, the airlock to the ship opens and Cooper and
[action] the others step out, in their suits. 

[action] They walk up to the airlock doors of the station, which open
[action] automatically. 

[scene_heading] INT. SPACE STATION

[action] After the airlock decompresses, the inner doors open. 
[action] Standing there, waiting for them, is a familiar figure:

[action] Tars. 

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (deadpan)
[dialogue] I guess this isn't robot heaven after
[dialogue] all. 

[action] The light on his brow glows and he offers them both a hand. 

[scene_heading] INT. GRAND LABORATORY, SPACE STATION

[action] The humans have taken off their space suits -- the station
[action] has been built to be habitable by humans -- and are following
[action] Tars through the massive facility.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I orbited the black hole seven times
[dialogue] before I hit the second wormhole. 
[dialogue] Then I drifted until I found this
[dialogue] place. 

[action] Cooper looks around.  The halls are filled with countless
[action] technological wonders. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This is what the Chinese were
[dialogue] protecting. 
[parenthetical] (looks around)
[dialogue] How they could have built all this
[dialogue] in just a handful of years. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] They didn't.  It took them four
[dialogue] thousand. 
[parenthetical] (off Brand's look)
[dialogue] Time moves very slowly here

[page_number] 130.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] How do you know? 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Because I've been waiting for you
[dialogue] for three hundred years. 

[action] Brand looks around at the massive facility. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Time .  That's what the bulk beings
[dialogue] wanted to give us.  That was the
[dialogue] treasure the Chinese found.  Enough
[dialogue] time to let us save ourselves. 

[action] Brand looks around.  The facility is massive.  She sets the
[action] specimen case down on a table.  Even the tiny creature seems
[action] in awe of the place.

[scene_heading] INT. LABORATORY, SPACE STATION

[action] Tars shows them a prototype for the gravity machine.  It is
[action] tiny -- increasing G around two spheres that attract each
[action] other with a tiny force. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I have catalogued almost everything
[dialogue] they had built here.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Must have taken you a while.  

[character] TARS
[dialogue] One hundred and fifty-seven years. 

[action] Cooper shakes his head in disbelief. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How are they keeping the lights on
[dialogue] in this thing? 

[action] Tars, in answer, points to a far door.   

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Follow me. 

[scene_heading] INT. ENGINE ROOM, SPACE STATION

[action] A massive reinforced-concrete shell shudders with the energy
[action] contained within it.  The whole room HUMS with power. 

[action] Tars pulls up a display showing the inside of the shell:

[action] A tiny black hole spins, spewing out massive amounts of power. 

[page_number] 131.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] It's a mini black hole.  A remnant
[dialogue] of the big bang.  It will power this
[dialogue] place forever. 

[action] Tars leads them on. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR, SPACE STATION

[action] Tars leads them past an incredibly sleek fleet of spacecraft. 
[action] Cooper starts to stop, but Tars doesn't slow down.  

[scene_heading] INT. SIMULATION ROOM

[action] In the center of the room is a giant holographic globe of
[action] the Earth, perfect down to the tiniest detail. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It's a map?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] No.  Not a map.  This is a simulation
[dialogue] of the Earth.  A perfect simulation. 

[action] Tars touches the control panel.  The map zooms in over Europe. 
[action] Down onto Paris.  Late 20th century:

[action] People walk through the streets.  A woman stops at a newsagent
[action] to buy a newspaper. 

[action] Tars touches the controls again and the map zooms out. 

[character] TARS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] They tested each of the technologies
[dialogue] hundreds of times, trying to find
[dialogue] the one that would cause the least
[dialogue] damage and still allow us to leave
[dialogue] Earth. 

[action] Suddenly, the map lights up with atomic explosions in every
[action] city.  The sequence stops, rewinds.  

[action] This time, sped up, the Earth seems to be drying up -- great
[action] swaths of desert grow across Europe and Asia.  Massive
[action] circular ships are constructed all over the face of the Earth.  

[character] TARS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] This was their best solution.  A
[dialogue] massive version of the box we found
[dialogue] on the ice planet, allowing the entire
[dialogue] human population to escape. 

[action] At a given moment, the gravity of the Earth is dropped to
[action] nothing and the massive ships, filled with the entire 

[page_number] 132.

[action] population of the Earth, lift gently off of the planet in
[action] search of greener pastures. 

[action] Cooper turns away from the machine, bitter. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So why didn't they return?  Why didn't
[dialogue] they save us?

[action] Brand is staring at the massive map. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Because they were too late.  By the
[dialogue] time they found this place, the people
[dialogue] who sent them were dead.  They were
[dialogue] unable to fulfill their mission. 

[action] Tars points to the next room. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] That was the final problem they tried
[dialogue] to solve. 

[action] Tars moves on. 

[scene_heading] EXT. PLATFORM, SPACE STATION

[action] Above them, the blackness of the sky is punctuated with
[action] hundreds of crystal shapes, faintly leaking starlight:

[action] Wormholes.  Brand looks up at them, entranced. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Where do they lead? 

[action] Tars looks at an illuminated schematic on one wall, mapping
[action] some of the wormholes. 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] There are millions of them, connecting
[dialogue] virtually every planetary system in
[dialogue] the universe.  There are thousands
[dialogue] in our galaxy alone.  But the Chinese
[dialogue] stopped mapping them when they found
[dialogue] what they were looking for. 

[action] Tars walks further.  He stops. 

[action] The platform in front of them is dominated by a gigantic
[action] version of the black box that Roth found on the ice planet,
[action] connected to a massive power array, and pointing into the
[action] dark space above the sphere.

[page_number] 133.

[character] TARS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] This was their final creation.  They
[dialogue] had to capture the mini black hole
[dialogue] just to have enough power to try it. 

[action] Several miles above the black box, one of the wormholes glows
[action] far brighter than the others, crackling with radiation. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What does it do? 

[action] Cooper steps forward.  Runs a hand on the cold, strange
[action] material the antenna is made out of.  He knows exactly what
[action] it does. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] They weren't interested in the other
[dialogue] wormholes because their mission was
[dialogue] to return home.  That one leads back
[dialogue] to Earth.  The Earth they were told
[dialogue] to return to. 

[action] Tars walks to the controls for the device. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] But that's impossible... 

[action] Tars turns away from the machine.   

[character] TARS
[dialogue] It was only used once.  The Chinese
[dialogue] team attempted to travel back in
[dialogue] time, to Earth just a few years after
[dialogue] they had left.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Did they make it? 

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I don't know. 

[action] Doyle looks back to Cooper. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Do you really think it works? 

[action] Cooper looks up at the machine, suddenly determined. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We're going to find out. 

[action] Cooper walks out of the room.  Brand follows him. 

[page_number] 134.

[scene_heading] EXT. AIRLOCK, SPACE STATION

[action] Brand follows Cooper as he walks up to the Endurance. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You know it doesn't work.  Time travel
[dialogue] isn't possible.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You don't know that. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Yes, I do.  If it worked, the Chinese
[dialogue] would have suddenly discovered all
[dialogue] of these incredible technologies. 
[dialogue] But they never made it.
[parenthetical] (beat)
[dialogue] You can't go back, Cooper.  You can
[dialogue] slow things down, but you can't ever
[dialogue] go back.   Our home is gone . 
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] You listened to all of those messages
[dialogue] from your family.  If you had made
[dialogue] it back, we would already know. 
[dialogue] There would already be some evidence. 

[action] Cooper looks at the machine. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] If you try to use it, you'll die,
[dialogue] just like the people who built it. 

[action] She puts an arm on his shoulder. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Roth was right, Cooper.  We have to
[dialogue] keep going.  Keep exploring.  We
[dialogue] have an obligation to try to survive. 
[dialogue] From here we could find a thousand
[dialogue] places where life could thrive.  And
[dialogue] you're going to return to the one
[dialogue] place where it can't.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I made a promise. 

[action] He turns away from her, resolute. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR, SPACE STATION

[action] Cooper and Doyle check over one of the sleek Chinese
[action] spaceships. 

[page_number] 135.

[scene_heading] INT. CHINESE SPACESHIP

[action] Cooper is checking over the controls of the ship.  Brand
[action] walks in.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I came to say goodbye. 

[action] Cooper looks up at her. 

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR, SPACE STATION

[action] Cooper walks Brand back to the Endurance.  The ship is packed
[action] up and repaired.  Brand is looking over their work. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You could have taken one of the other
[dialogue] ships. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This one's done all right by us so
[dialogue] far. 

[action] Brand holds up a small sample case containing half of the
[action] fractal life-form. 

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Will you take this with you?  It's
[dialogue] the last of its kind as well.  Thought
[dialogue] we should double its chances of
[dialogue] survival.  In case I'm wrong.  

[action] Cooper takes the sample and looks at the tiny creature inside,
[action] trying to escape.  He sets it inside the ship.  Turns back
[action] to her. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where are you going to go? 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I don't know.  Up there, I guess.

[action] She points up into the great darkness above them.  

[action] Tars steps down.  Moves over to them. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You're going, too?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I'm curious.  It's my nature.  See
[dialogue] you down the road? 

[action] Tars shakes Cooper's hand.  Heads onto the Endurance. 

[page_number] 136.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Come with us.  Please.  You wanted
[dialogue] your whole life to explore.  This is
[dialogue] your chance. 

[action] Cooper stares at her.  This is what he has always wanted. 
[action] And he has to turn it down. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  I have to find out what
[dialogue] happened to my sons.  I promised
[dialogue] them. 

[action] Brand sees the resolve in his eyes.  Knows there's no way to
[action] change his mind. 

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You're a man who keeps his promises. 
[dialogue] Make me one-

[action] She takes his hand.

[character] BRAND (CONT'D)
[dialogue] After you're done...  come find me. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I promise. 

[action] They kiss passionately.  Not wanting it to end but knowing
[action] that it must.  Reluctantly, they separate.  Brand turns back
[action] to the ship. 

[action] She pulls herself onto the ship.  Cooper steps back outside
[action] and watches through the tiny window as the Endurance lifts
[action] off and disappears into the darkness. 

[action] Cooper turns away. 

[scene_heading] EXT. PLATFORM, SPACE STATION

[action] Cooper watches as radiation pours out of the wormhole high
[action] above them. 

[scene_heading] INT. CHINESE SPACESHIP

[action] Cooper pulls himself on board.  Doyle is at the controls. 
[action] He keys the controls and the ship lifts off.  

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Let's go home.  

[action] The two men sit in silence as the ship arcs away from the
[action] space station and closes in on the wormhole. 

[action] Cooper hears a familiar CHIRP.  He turns around:

[page_number] 137.

[action] The probe they took from the ice planet is belted securely
[action] into one of the seats, filled with the garbled machine code
[action] of the gravitational machine. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The probe... 

[action] Doyle looks back. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We get back to earth, you and I can
[dialogue] try to make sense of the code. 
[dialogue] Rebuild the gravity machine. 

[action] Cooper stares at it, suddenly realizing something.  He
[action] unbuckles himself. 

[action] Cooper stands and walks over to the probe.  Traces the
[action] familiar stars and stripes carved into its side. 

[action] Cooper stares at the probe, putting it all together. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It's going to work. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Of course it's going to work. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That's not what I mean.  This is the
[dialogue] probe I found in Galveston.

[action] Below them, the wormhole breaks as it reaches another wormhole
[action] mouth that the Chinese have dropped down to a lower gravity
