[action] well, creating a time machine. 

[action] The wormhole mouth is FLARING with radiation. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Brand said there'd be some evidence
[dialogue] we'd made it.  And there was.  This. 
[parenthetical] (holds up probe)
[dialogue] But only this.  Everything will be
[dialogue] destroyed except for this.  

[action] He moves toward Doyle.  Doyle stands. 

[character] COOPER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] We have to stop. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] I don't know what you're talking
[dialogue] about, but you're not touching the
[dialogue] controls.  I'm going home. 

[page_number] 138.

[action] Cooper moves closer to Doyle.  Doyle pulls out the flair
[action] gun.  Points it at Cooper. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You don't need to do this.  We won't
[dialogue] make it back, but this does.  The
[dialogue] secrets are already right on it. 
[dialogue] Maybe someone found it.  There's
[dialogue] hope. 
[parenthetical] (sad)
[dialogue] But we don't get to go with. 

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] You're not stopping me.  I'm going
[dialogue] home. 

[action] Doyle forces Cooper into the ship's lander.  Then he closes
[action] the airlock. 

[action] Cooper pounds on the other side of the glass, trying to reason
[action] with him. 

[action] The landing craft detaches from the ship. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CHINESE LANDING CRAFT

[action] Cooper watches, helpless, as Doyle's ship races ahead towards
[action] the glowing wormhole mouth. 

[action] As the ship speeds toward the next wormhole, the radiation
[action] suddenly FLARES, annihilating the ship and everything in it. 

[action] Almost everything. 

[action] Cooper's landing craft spins away from the wormhole mouth. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE, OUR SOLAR SYSTEM

[action] A hole opens in the sky with a FIERY EXPLOSION.  As the
[action] radiation subsides, all that shoots out of the hole is a
[action] vaporous wisp of atomized metal, and a burned, blackened
[action] probe, which hurtles toward Earth. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE, NEAR EARTH ORBIT

[action] The probe collides with a satellite, hurling debris into the
[action] upper atmosphere. 

[action] PROBE'S ONBOARD CAMERA  P.O.V. -- 

[action] The probe's onboard camera documents its journey.  Fragments
[action] of video:

[page_number] 139.

[action] -- The probe HURTLES through the atmosphere, toward North
[action] America, the Gulf Coast. 

[action] -- The probe SMASHES down into a sandbar . 

[action] -- Daylight.  A man is descending toward it, dangling from a
[action] rope.  It's Cooper. 

[action] -- Night.  A kitchen.  A little boy -- Murph -- stares at
[action] the probe, while Cooper works at it with a blowtorch. 

[action] -- Tars pulls the probe out of the back of a plane. 

[action] -- Brand's father, older, studies the probe.  Gives up. 

[action] -- Much later.  People are moving around in the darkened
[action] base, scavenging equipment. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD -- DAY

[action] A combine harvester is dead in a field, service hatch opened. 
[action] A MAN is lying under the machine, working. 

[action] The man hauls himself out from under the huge machine.  Dusts
[action] himself off. 

[action] It's Murph, 30s.  He looks at the FARMER who is waiting for
[action] the verdict.  Murph shakes his head. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] It's done.  Auto-pilot's packed up
[dialogue] for the last time. 

[character] FARMER
[dialogue] You can't make it work a little
[dialogue] longer? 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Can't do anything for you.  There
[dialogue] aren't any more parts for these. 
[dialogue] Not anywhere. 

[character] FARMER
[dialogue] You don't understand.  We're getting
[dialogue] less than a hundred pounds per acre. 
[dialogue] We need to plant more, not less. 

[action] Murph looks around him at the pathetic crop of corn that
[action] stretches around them.  The plants are feeble, barely able
[action] to support themselves. 

[page_number] 140.

[character] FARMER (CONT'D)
[dialogue] You've got to find us some more parts,
[dialogue] Murph.  It's getting desperate. 
[dialogue] Isn't there anywhere you can look? 

[action] Murph begins packing up his tools, thinking it over. 

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR -- DAY

[action] Murph stares at the darkened shape of a plane under a tarp. 
[action] He stares at the tarp, unsure if he wants to keep going. 

[action] He pulls the tarp off, revealing Cooper's old Piper Cub.  He
[action] checks over the engine, lights, prop.  Turns the key.  Fires
[action] her up.  The diesel wakes with a GRUNT. 

[scene_heading] EXT. AIRSTRIP -- DAY

[action] Murph wheels the old plane out onto the field by hand. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SKIES OVER THE SOUTHWEST -- DAY

[action] The ancient plane skirts the San Gabriels . 

[scene_heading] EXT. FIELD, SANTA CRUZ ISLAND

[action] Murph sets the plane down.

[action] He parks the plane under a copse of trees and climbs down. 

[action] He scans the horizon.  Nothing.  Is this the place? 

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST -- DAY

[action] Murph pushes his way through the undergrowth.  Stops.

[action] He's standing at the blast doors to the facility.  They've
[action] been blown open with dynamite. 

[scene_heading] INT. NASA FACILITY -- DAY

[action] Murph lets himself inside.  Lights a flare. 

[action] The place has been gutted.  Thieves and scavengers have taken
[action] almost everything. 

[action] Murph finds one of the robots, or what's left of it -- it's
[action] been stripped, leaving only the bare composite skeleton. 
[action] The empty eye sockets stare back at Murph. 

[action] Murph looks around the place.  There is nothing left to
[action] salvage.  He turns to leave.  Hears a familiar CHIRP. 

[action] In the corner, under a pile of rain-soaked garbage:

[page_number] 141.

[action] The probe. 

[action] No one has bothered to steal it.  Murph stares at it. 

[scene_heading] INT. UNDERGROUND FACILITY -- NIGHT

[action] Murph sweeps detritus off of an ancient computer mainframe
[action] that survived the looting.  He hooks the probe up to it. 

[action] After a minute, the screen fills with a picture of an ice-
[action] covered planet.  Then nothing.  The rest of the probe's drive
[action] is filled with garbage, noise. 

[action] Murph switches off the screen. 

[action] He stands.  Begins to leave without the probe.  Stops.  Turns
[action] back.  Picks it up and takes it with him. 

[scene_heading] INT.  KITCHEN, MURPH'S HOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Murph is hunched over the probe, trying to pull data off of
[action] it with a defibrillator.  He has incorporated an ancient
[action] laptop into the chain, and is tweaking values on it, trying
[action] to decrypt the contents. 

[action] His WIFE, several months pregnant, turns back to him from
[action] the stove. 

[character] MURPH'S WIFE
[dialogue] Would you get that thing off of the
[dialogue] table?

[action] Murph nods, absorbed.  Keys in a final tweak to the decryption
[action] software.  Hits return. 

[action] Suddenly, the screen begins filling with data. 

[action] Murph stares at it, wide eyed. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT.  BARN -- NIGHT

[action] Murph, now in his 40s, is putting the finishing touches on a
[action] large machine.  Although slightly different, we recognize
[action] some of the components and their configuration:

[action] It's a crude version of the gravitational device. 

[action] Murph double checks it, then fires up a small gas powered
[action] generator.  Throws a breaker, feeding power to the unit. 

[action] The unit lights up.  HUMS.  But nothing happens.  

[page_number] 142.

[action] Murph, disappointed but undaunted, shuts it down and begins
[action] checking it over.  He hears a noise at the door.  Turns back. 
[action] His daughter, Emily, 8, is standing at the door. 

[character] EMILY COOPER
[dialogue] Dad?  You said you'd take us to the
[dialogue] game. 

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (distracted)
[dialogue] Did I? 

[action] He looks at the impossible nest of wires.  Shakes his head. 

[character] MURPH (CONT'D)
[dialogue] All right.  Let me get my jacket. 

[action] Murph picks up his jacket from the workbench.  Next to it on
[action] the bench is a shortwave radio playing STATIC. 

[character] EMILY COOPER
[parenthetical] (staring at machine)
[dialogue] What is it supposed to do, Dad? 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I have no idea. 

[action] Murph switches off the lights and they walk out. 

[action] After a moment, the radio CRACKLES to life in the darkness. 

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio, garbled)
[dialogue] This is the crew of the Endurance...
[dialogue] Murph... I'm sorry...

[action] The signal crackles out. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, MURPH'S HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Murph, 50s, slightly gray, sits at his kitchen table.  He
[action] hears a distant RUMBLE. 

[action] He looks up.  His wife is standing by the sink. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Where's Emily?

[character] WIFE
[dialogue] Out in the barn.  She's been tinkering
[dialogue] with your old projects. 

[page_number] 143.

[action] Murph stands up.  The roof of the barn is visibly shaking. 
[action] He steps outside. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  YARD, MURPH'S HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Murph moves towards the barn, concerned.  The shaking is
[action] growing stronger -- the entire structure is buckling. 

[action] Emily, now 18, backs out of the barn. 

[character] EMILY COOPER
[dialogue] Dad... I'm sorry...  I made some
[dialogue] changes to the machine.  I think I
[dialogue] did something wrong. 

[action] Murph puts an arm around his daughter and as they watch, the
[action] entire barn IMPLODES.  Their tractor begins sliding towards
[action] the wreckage of the barn, chunky tires plowing up the soil. 

[action] Finally, the RUMBLING STOPS as the power lines short out in
[action] a FIERY display. 

[action] As the dust clears, Murph and his daughter examine the mess:

[action] The entire barn has been crushed into a tiny ball. 

[action] Murph looks at his daughter. 

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Do you remember what you changed? 

[action] Wide-eyed, she thinks about it.  Then nods. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. EARTH, 2320 -- DAY

[action] This is North America.  But it doesn't look much like it. 

[action] Mother Nature has just about wiped the slate clean.  Most of
[action] the vegetation is gone, and unchecked winds sweep across the
[action] barren plains.  Patches of ice lie think on the ground, as
[action] if a heavy snow has come and gone. 

[action] SUPER TITLE: "TWO HUNDRED YEARS LATER"

[action] One of the Chinese spaceships descends through the thick
[action] clouds above and settles gently onto the plain. 

[action] The hatch opens and Cooper steps out.  He takes a cautious
[action] look around.  He is holding the fractal life in its small
[action] container. 

[action] The clouds are threatening, but the weather looks calm enough
[action] right here. 

[page_number] 144.

[action] Cooper starts to walk. 

[scene_heading] EXT. COLLAPSED HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Remarkably, part of Cooper's old house is still standing.  

[scene_heading] INT. COLLAPSED HOUSE -- DAY

[action] Cooper stands in the middle of his kitchen.  Two walls are
[action] missing and the rest is collapsed in a heap.  But he can
[action] still see where he used to feed his kids breakfast. 

[action] He has kept his promise.  Several hundred years too late. 

[action] Cooper hunkers down, staring at the space where his kids
[action] used to be.  Rainwater covers the ancient formica. 

[action] It has taken him years to finally reach this place.  He has
[action] had plenty of time to come to grips with what he might find. 
[action] But nothing has prepared him for this:

[action] He is completely alone.  Nothing is left. 

[scene_heading] INT. COLLAPSED FARMHOUSE -- NIGHT

[action] Cooper is still sitting there when the ice storm hits. 

[action] Unchecked by trees or vegetation, the wind rockets through
[action] the house, blasting Cooper and pelting what's left of the
[action] structure with fist-sized hail. 

[action] Cooper takes shelter against the remaining wall.  He's going
[action] to have to sit this one out -- the hail and winds are the
[action] brutal descendants of the weather he knew.  They'll finish
[action] him off if he steps back outside. 

[action] As he watches, the pool of water on the kitchen floor freezes
[action] over in seconds.

[scene_heading] INT. COLLAPSED FARMHOUSE -- DAWN

[action] Cooper shivers, pressed against the wall.  The storm is still
[action] raging outside.  He is freezing to death.  He has to move
[action] now or he will die.  He pulls the hood tight around his face
[action] and stumbles outside. 

[scene_heading] EXT. SNOWBOUND FIELD -- DAY

[action] Cooper makes his way through the blinding snow.  He tries to
[action] find his way, but the ship has been consumed by the blizzard. 

[action] He stumbles to the ground, dropping the glass case with the
[action] fractal wildlife in it.  The case shatters. 

[action] Cooper tries to stand back up, but his strength is dwindling. 

[page_number] 145.

[action] As he watches, the fractal wildlife creeps tentatively out
[action] of its broken habitat.  It forms together against the cold. 

[action] After a moment, it burrows into the ice.  As it does, the
[action] ice glows faintly.  It seems to be right at home.  

[action] Cooper laughs, glimpsing the outline of a plan.  Was this
[action] what the beings who made the wormhole intended all along? 

[action] Cooper suddenly spots the outline of the ship through the
[action] driving snow.  He stumbles back up to his feet and struggles
[action] a few more steps, then stumbles again, spent.  

[action] Finally, he sits down in the snow to die. 

[action] As his senses flicker in and out, he is struck with memories
[action] of his boys, so vivid he reaches out for them, crawling
[action] forward in the snow. 

[action] He stops, and laughs, remembering what Case told him about
[action] what happens when humans die. 

[action] He struggles to his feet and stumbles a few more feet,
[action] eventually coming to rest just steps from the ship. 

[action] Cooper takes a final step.  Finally, he collapses under his
[action] ship, as the wind continues to HOWL around him.

[action] As the snow clears for a moment, Cooper is alone on a vast
[action] arctic tundra.  He will die alone.

[subheading] BLACK

[transition] FADE IN:

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM -- DAY

[action] Cooper wakes. 

[action] He is in a sunlit room.  A breeze rustles the curtains as it
[action] makes its way in through a large open window. 

[action] Cooper sits up.  As he a does, a control panel on the wall
[action] TONES gently, as if in response to his movement. 

[action] He stands and looks around.  He can see sunlight through the
[action] open windows and hear people -- kids YELLING as they play. 
[action] He moves toward the window.  Reaches for the curtains.  Before
[action] he can, the door opens behind him.

[action] A WOMAN in a white coat steps in, smiling warmly. 

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] Good morning.  Don't go outside just
[dialogue] yet, Mr. Cooper.  
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 146.

[character] DOCTOR (CONT'D)
[parenthetical] (offers him a pill)
[dialogue] This will help with the
[dialogue] disorientation. 

[action] Cooper opens his mouth to talk, but his voice is cracked. 
[action] He gives up.  Swallows the pill. 

[character] DOCTOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Wait a minute for that to take effect. 
[dialogue] It can be a bit of an adjustment. 
[parenthetical] (looks him over)
[dialogue] You had some frostbite.  Nothing too
[dialogue] serious.  

[action] As she talks, Cooper looks past her to the window. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where am I?

[character] DOCTOR
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] It's a bit of a coincidence, really. 
[dialogue] When the rangers found you, we were
[dialogue] the nearest facility. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What's coincidence?  What is this
[dialogue] place?

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] We have a lot to show you.

[action] The doctor reaches for the curtains.  She offers him a hand. 
[action] Cooper declines the help and steps outside. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HOSPICE, COOPER STATION -- DAY

[action] Cooper is standing on a rooftop deck of a four-story building
[action] in a medium-sized city surrounded by fields.  But as the
[action] road and the buildings stretch into the distance, the
[action] landscape curves up, not down.  

[action] The entire landscape is contained within a huge cylinder-
[action] shaped space station. 

[action] Cooper stumbles, and reaches instinctively out.  The doctor
[action] catches him. 

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] You're OK.  We get this from people
[dialogue] who move here from planetary colonies
[dialogue] all the time. 

[page_number] 147.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where... where are we?

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] Like I said.  It was a coincidence. 
[dialogue] There was a facility closer to Earth,
[dialogue] but they had a problem, so you wound
[dialogue] up here.
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] This is the Space Station Joseph A. 
[dialogue] Cooper. 

[action] Cooper takes in the incredible surroundings.  A thousand
[action] feet above them, black specks are sprinkled over an ocean of
[action] green -- Jersey milking cows grazing in a field of wild grass.  

[character] DOCTOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I've got someone who wants to meet
[dialogue] you. 

[action] Cooper looks at her. 

[scene_heading] INT. INTENSIVE CARE ROOM, HOSPICE, COOPER STATION -- DAY

[action] Cooper steps inside.  The room is dark, still, the only noise
[action] is the labored RATTLE of an old man struggling to breathe. 

[action] Cooper steps closer to the bed.  The man's skin is paper-
[action] thin.  He is ancient. 

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] He was moved here after they found
[dialogue] you.  He's a little old for a
[dialogue] transfer, but they made an exception. 

[action] Cooper hovers at the back of the room, unsure.  He turns to
[action] the doctor, questioning.  Then he notices the pictures on
[action] the old man's desk: 

[action] There are dozens of them.  Children, grandchildren.  Then
[action] the older ones -- the man's own parents.  Grandparents. 
[action] Cooper spots a tiny framed picture with someone he recognizes:

[action] Murph, 80 years old, surrounded by his daughter and her
[action] family.  They are standing in front of a re-opened Cape
[action] Canaveral, and a huge spaceship under construction. 

[action] Cooper picks up the picture and stares at it. 

[action] The doctor points to a shy little boy hiding behind Murph's
[action] leg in the picture. 

[character] DOCTOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] That's him. 

[page_number] 148.

[action] She points to the old man lying in the bed. 

[character] DOCTOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] His name is Anthony Welling.  Anthony
[dialogue] Cooper  Welling.
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] He's your great great grandson. 
[dialogue] He's been waiting a long time for
[dialogue] you.

[action] Cooper's eyes well with tears.  He steps over to the bed and
[action] looks down at the ancient man, teetering on the edge of death.  

[action] The old man looks at him, eyes widening in excitement.  He
[action] strains, trying to reach the bedside table.  He's trying to
[action] reach the drawer.  Cooper helps him open it. 

[action] Inside is a simple, familiar wristwatch.  The old man
[action] carefully takes out the watch.  He gives the watch a few
[action] winds and, hands shaking, offers it to Cooper. 

[action] Cooper, eyes filling with tears, closes his hand over the
[action] old man's hand, enveloping both the watch and the man's hand. 

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNFIELD, COOPER STATION -- MORNING

[action] Cooper is sitting in a well-appointed office.  A middle-aged
[action] BUREAUCRAT smiles at him from the far side of a huge desk.  

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] You're a hero, Mr. Cooper.  Let's
[dialogue] just start off by saying that.  It's
[dialogue] an incredible and... unexpected honor
[dialogue] to have you here with us.  

[action] Cooper smiles, uncomfortable.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] None of us would be here without the
[dialogue] efforts of you and the other
[dialogue] crewmembers of the Endurance. 
[dialogue] (smiles, unconvincingly)
[dialogue] So I don't want you to take this the
[dialogue] wrong way.  But there are some
[dialogue] questions I've been told to ask. 

[action] The man looks petrified, like he's been instructed to grill
[action] George Washington on his expense reports. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Shoot. 

[page_number] 149.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] The rangers who found you reported
[dialogue] that you had released a sample of an
[dialogue] alien life-form into the wild.  Which
[dialogue] is, unfortunately, against
[dialogue] regulations. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It was last of its kind.  Their planet
[dialogue] was destroyed.

[action] The administrator cues up a series of images on his computer. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] The rangers attempted to isolate the
[dialogue] life-form, but it had already spread
[dialogue] out of control.  It seems to be
[dialogue] thriving. 

[action] He shows Cooper a satellite image of north america.  A
[action] considerable portion of the frozen tundra is  glowing . 

[action] Cooper begins laughing.  Which makes the administrator even
[action] less comfortable. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Can you tell me why you... elected
[dialogue] to release the life-form back on
[dialogue] earth?

[action] Cooper is still laughing. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Because that was the plan. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] Whose plan?

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] I don't know. 

[action] The administrator tries to smile back.  Makes a few notes in
[action] his file.  Changes the subject. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] My assistant tells me you've applied
[dialogue] for a position with the exploration
[dialogue] fleet.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Feel like I should be pulling my
[dialogue] weight. 

[page_number] 150.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] That's admirable, Mr. Cooper.  The
[dialogue] truth is, most of the fleet's
[dialogue] personnel are automated.  There are
[dialogue] a small number of crewed ships.  But
[dialogue] there are great numbers of candidates. 
[dialogue] Very well trained candidates.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I was hoping to enroll in a course. 
[dialogue] Try to get myself up to speed on the
[dialogue] new systems. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] I don't want you to take this the
[dialogue] wrong way, Mr.  Cooper -- like I
[dialogue] said, you're a hero.  But the truth
[dialogue] is we have somewhat limited resources. 

[action] Cooper remembers this conversation.  He looks down.  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No one's heard anything from Brand?

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] I'm sorry.  Officially, she's been
[dialogue] listed as missing for over 200 years. 
[dialogue] Mind you, I guess you turned up
[dialogue] eventually, didn't you?  

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Am I really going to hurt anybody by
[dialogue] going to look for her?  I just need
[dialogue] a small ship.  I made a promise. 

[action] The bureaucrat looks down.  Is he really going to have to
[action] tell this guy the lay of the land? 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] Listen.  Mr. Cooper.  You're a hero. 
[dialogue] You're the oldest man in the human
[dialogue] race.  Don't you want to take it
[dialogue] easy?  
[parenthetical] (off his look)
[dialogue] I hope you understand, we all you
[dialogue] hold you in the highest possible
[dialogue] regard. 
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] Which is why they're never going to
[dialogue] let you go off by yourself in a
[dialogue] spaceship.  I'm sorry. 

[action] Cooper looks at his hands.  He's got a couple centuries on
[action] the bureaucrat in Earth years, but looks ten years younger. 
[action] He puts his hands in his pockets. 

[page_number] 151.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR (CONT'D)
[dialogue] I've got some good news, however. 
[parenthetical] (big smile)
[dialogue] We all know about your early life,
[dialogue] Mr Cooper.  I wrote a paper on it
[dialogue] when I was a boy.  And I think we
[dialogue] found something you'll really enjoy. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNFIELD, COOPER STATION -- DAY

[action] Corn blows in an artificial breeze.  A red tractor makes its
[action] way through the field, which curves gently up in the distance. 

[character] ADMINISTRATOR (V.O.)
[dialogue] The machines do most of the work, of
[dialogue] course, but we were able to get you
[dialogue] a few acres.  You're going to be a
[dialogue] farmer again. 

[action] After a moment, the tractor stops.  Cooper climbs down from
[action] the seat.  Looks at the front steering linkage, which is
[action] jammed with an errant tree branch.  He wipes his forehead
[action] and begins working the branch out of the machine.   

[action] He looks miserable. 

[action] A robot, a similar unit to Tars, walks over.  Offers Cooper
[action] a bottle of water.  Cooper accepts it. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR BAY, COOPER STATION

[action] Cooper stands on an observation deck, high above the hangar
[action] floor.  Below him, bright young things in uniforms climb
[action] into sleek-looking spaceships and prepare to set out. 

[scene_heading] EXT. BASEBALL DIAMOND, COOPER STATION

[action] Cooper sits in the stands, listening to the familiar crack
[action] of the bat as an intramural team from the university
[action] practices. 

[action] Cooper watches.  He looks bored.  

[action] The kid up to bat cracks a pop fly.  For a second, the catcher
[action] shuffles back and forth, trying to get himself into position. 
[action] But the ball never returns.  The catcher YELLS out a warning.

[action] Above, the ball begins to slowly fall up, not down, toward
[action] the town center above. 

[action] After a second, the ball smashes through a skylight of a
[action] building high above them.

[action] Cooper watches as the kid rounds the bases, laughing. 

[page_number] 152.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE, COOPER STATION -- DAY

[action] Cooper's robot sits at the kitchen table.  Cooper is fiddling
[action] around in the back of his head. 

[character] ROBOT
[dialogue] Settings: general settings, security
[dialogue] settings--

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Curiosity.  New level setting.  100
[dialogue] percent. 

[character] ROBOT
[dialogue] Confirmed.  Would you like to make
[dialogue] any additional changes?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sense of humor.  New level setting. 
[dialogue] 100 percent.  Wait. 
[parenthetical] (thinks)
[dialogue] 80 percent. 

[action] He begins putting the robot back together. 

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE, COOPER STATION -- TWILIGHT

[action] Cooper sits on his porch, joined by the same robot as before. 
[action] They watch as the space station rotates lazily out of
[action] alignment with the local star, casting the inside of the
[action] cylinder into shade, then darkness. 

[action] The shadow races past them.  Another day.  Another night. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION

[action] A maintenance worker finishes looking over one of the sleek-
[action] looking spacecraft.  He packs up his tools and heads out. 

[action] After a moment, two figures pick their way across the hangar
[action] floor, sticking to the shadows. 

[action] As they reach the first ship in the line, we get a better
[action] look.  It's Cooper and his robot pal.  The robot is wearing
[action] a baseball cap and carrying a toolbox. 

[action] Cooper gestures to the robot, who sets down the toolbox with
[action] a click against the mirror-like floor. 

[action] Cooper shakes his head, annoyed, at the robot, and puts his
[action] finger to his lips.  The robot nods, bashful. 

[action] Cooper waves a small handheld computer near the skin of the
[action] ship until it lights up.  Then he punches in a few codes. 
[action] Nothing happens.  He punches in a few more.  

[page_number] 153.

[action] Suddenly, the hatch opens with a HISS. 

[scene_heading] INT. SHIP

[action] Cooper moves quickly to the cockpit of the ship.  Looks over
[action] the controls.  The robot straps himself in next to him. 
[action] Cooper looks up through the windows.

[action] The inky black void of space beckons. 

[action] Cooper smiles and reaches for the controls. 

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where do you want to go first? 

[action] The robot thinks it over. 

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION

[action] The technician walks back into the hangar.  He walks along
[action] the row of ships till he reaches the last one. 

[action] It's not there. 

[action] He looks out into the blackness of the void.  Sees a tiny
[action] glowing speck, getting smaller and smaller. 

[subheading] END
